80	/-! ## §1. Flasque sheaves of `kbar`-modules -/
81	
82	/-- **Flasque sheaf of `kbar`-modules on a topological space**
83	(Hartshorne II.1, Exercise 1.16; III.2, paragraph preceding Lemma 2.4).
84	
85	A sheaf `F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`
86	is **flasque** when, for every inclusion of opens `V ≤ U` in `X`, the
87	restriction map
88	`F.val.obj (op U) → F.val.obj (op V)` is surjective as a map of
89	`kbar`-modules.
90	
91	The predicate is read off the underlying presheaf of `kbar`-modules and
92	applies uniformly to the project's `ModuleCat kbar`-flavoured cohomology
93	pipeline (it does not depend on any topological-space hypothesis on `X`,
94	so the curve specialisation is by instance synthesis on
95	`C.left.toTopCat`).
96	
97	Blueprint reference: `def:isFlasque_sheaf`. -/
98	def Scheme.IsFlasque
99	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
100	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) : Prop :=
101	  ∀ ⦃U V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X⦄ (h : V ≤ U),
102	    Function.Surjective ((F.val.map (homOfLE h).op).hom)
103	
104	/-- **Pushforward of a flasque sheaf is flasque** (Hartshorne II.1,
105	Exercise 1.16(d)).
106	
107	For a continuous map of topological spaces `f : X ⟶ Y` and a flasque
108	sheaf `F` of `kbar`-modules on `X`, the pushforward sheaf `f _* F` is
109	flasque on `Y`. The reason is purely formal: the restriction map of
110	`f _* F` along `V ≤ U` in `Y` is, by definition of pushforward, the
111	restriction map of `F` along `f ⁻¹ V ≤ f ⁻¹ U` in `X`, which is
112	surjective by hypothesis on `F`.
113	
114	**iter-191 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed via the unfolding
115	`pushforward.map (homOfLE h).op = F.val.map ((Opens.map f).map (homOfLE h)).op`
116	(by `rfl`), so flasqueness of `F` along `(Opens.map f).map (homOfLE h)` gives
117	the conclusion. Blueprint reference: `lem:isFlasque_pushforward`. -/
118	theorem Scheme.IsFlasque.pushforward
119	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X Y : TopCat.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)
120	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)}
121	    (hF : Scheme.IsFlasque F) :
122	    Scheme.IsFlasque
123	      ((TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) f).obj F) := by
124	  intro U V h
125	  exact hF ((Opens.map f).map (homOfLE h)).le
126	
127	/-- **Constant sheaf on an irreducible topological space is flasque**
128	(Hartshorne II.1, Exercise 1.16(a)).
129	
130	For an irreducible topological space `X`, every nonempty open is
131	connected and dense; the constant presheaf `U ↦ A` already satisfies the
132	sheaf condition, and its restriction maps are the identity on `A` (for
133	the nonempty branches) and factor through `0` (for the empty branch).
134	
135	**iter-191 Lane H file-skeleton** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry; closure
136	is iter-192+. Blueprint reference:
137	`lem:isFlasque_constant_irreducible`. -/
138	theorem Scheme.IsFlasque.constant_of_irreducible
139	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
140	    [IrreducibleSpace X] (A : ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :
141	    Scheme.IsFlasque
142	      ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
143	        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj A) := by
144	  sorry
145	
146	/-- **Injective sheaves have vanishing higher cohomology** (axiom-clean
147	helper, Hartshorne III §1).
148	
149	For a topological space `X` and an injective object `I` of the
150	`kbar`-module sheaf category, the `kbar`-flavoured derived
151	global-sections cohomology `HModule kbar I i` is the zero `kbar`-module
152	for every `i ≥ 1`. This is immediate from Mathlib's
153	`HasInjectiveDimensionLT` framework: `Injective I` gives
154	`HasInjectiveDimensionLT I 1` (Mathlib
155	`instHasInjectiveDimensionLTOfNatNatOfInjective`), and
156	`HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` yields
157	`Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext Y I i)` for `i ≥ 1` and any `Y`; specialising
158	`Y` to the constant sheaf at `kbar` and applying
159	`Module.finrank_zero_of_subsingleton` closes the goal.
160	
161	**iter-192 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed axiom-clean. Used as the
162	`Ext^i(_, I) = 0` input inside the Hartshorne III.2.5 long-exact-sequence
163	chase for `HModule_flasque_eq_zero` below. -/
164	theorem Scheme.HModule_injective_finrank_eq_zero
165	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
166	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
167	    [HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))]
168	    {I : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)}
169	    [Injective I] (i : ℕ) (hi : 1 ≤ i) :
170	    Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar I i) = 0 := by
171	  have hsub : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule kbar I i) :=
172	    HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton I 1 i hi _
173	  exact Module.finrank_zero_of_subsingleton
174	
175	/-- **The canonical injective-embedding short exact sequence**
176	`0 → F → Injective.under F → cokernel(Injective.ι F) → 0` of sheaves of
177	`kbar`-modules (axiom-clean helper). The embedding `Injective.ι F` is
178	mono by `Injective.ι_mono`, the cokernel projection is epi by
179	construction, and the middle row is exact at `Injective.under F` by
180	`ShortComplex.exact_cokernel`. -/
181	noncomputable def Scheme.injectiveSES
182	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
183	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
184	    [HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))]
185	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) :
186	    CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
187	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) :=
188	  ShortComplex.mk (Injective.ι F) (Limits.cokernel.π (Injective.ι F))
189	    (Limits.cokernel.condition (Injective.ι F))
190	
191	/-- The injective-embedding short complex is short exact. -/
192	theorem Scheme.injectiveSES_shortExact
193	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
194	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
195	    [HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))]
196	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) :
197	    (Scheme.injectiveSES F).ShortExact :=
198	  { exact := ShortComplex.exact_cokernel (Injective.ι F)
199	    mono_f := Injective.ι_mono F
200	    epi_g := Limits.coequalizer.π_epi }
201	
202	/-- **Generic LES vanishing lemma**: in an abelian category with enough Ext,
203	if `0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` is short exact with `S.X₂` injective and the
204	post-composition `Hom(X, S.X₂) → Hom(X, S.X₃)` is surjective, then
205	`Ext X S.X₁ 1` is the zero `Subsingleton`.
206	
207	This is the structural skeleton of the Hartshorne III.2.5 argument
208	specialised at degree 1: the long exact sequence
209	`Ext X S.X₂ 0 → Ext X S.X₃ 0 → Ext X S.X₁ 1 → Ext X S.X₂ 1`
210	collapses because the rightmost term vanishes (injectivity of `S.X₂`)
211	and the leftmost map is surjective by the `Hom`-level hypothesis. The
212	proof composes:
213	- `HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` (injective ⇒ `Ext X S.X₂ 1 = 0`).
214	- `Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁` (any `x₁ ∈ Ext X S.X₁ 1` lifts
215	  to some `x₃ ∈ Ext X S.X₃ 0` via the connecting morphism).
216	- `Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀` (identifies `Ext X S.X₃ 0` with `Hom(X, S.X₃)`).
217	- `comp_extClass_assoc` (the LES "complex" identity used to discharge
218	  `(Ext.mk₀ S.g).comp extClass = 0`).
219	
220	**iter-192 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed axiom-clean. Used to peel
221	off the `i = 1` case of `HModule_flasque_eq_zero` once the
222	`Hom(X, I) → Hom(X, G)`-surjectivity input (Hartshorne II Ex. 1.16(b))
223	is supplied for an injective resolution. -/
224	theorem ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective
225	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u} C] [Abelian C] [HasExt C]
226	    (X : C) {S : ShortComplex C} (hS : S.ShortExact) [Injective S.X₂]
227	    (hsurj : Function.Surjective (fun (f : X ⟶ S.X₂) => f ≫ S.g))
228	    (x₁ : Abelian.Ext X S.X₁ 1) : x₁ = 0 := by
229	  have hinj_subs : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext X S.X₂ 1) :=
230	    HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton S.X₂ 1 1 le_rfl _
231	  have hker : x₁.comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (show (1 : ℕ) + 0 = 1 by omega) = 0 :=
232	    Subsingleton.elim _ _
233	  obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
234	    Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ X hS x₁ hker
235	      (n₀ := 0) (show 0 + 1 = 1 by omega)
236	  obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := hsurj (Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀ x₃)
237	  simp only at hy
238	  have hx3eq : Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀ x₃) = x₃ := by

257	iter-193 Lane H prover dispatch).
258	
259	Given a short exact sequence `0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` in an abelian
260	category with injective `S.X₂` and `n₀ ≥ 1`, if every element of
261	`Abelian.Ext X S.X₃ n₀` is zero, then so is every element of
262	`Abelian.Ext X S.X₁ (n₀ + 1)`. This is the higher-degree analogue of
263	`ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective`: at degrees `n₀ ≥ 1` the
264	`Hom`-surjectivity hypothesis is replaced by the (stronger, recursable)
265	"`Ext^{n₀}(_, S.X₃) = 0`" hypothesis.

267	**Proof structure**: by `HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton` applied to
268	`S.X₂` injective at degree `n₀ + 1 ≥ 2`, `Abelian.Ext X S.X₂ (n₀ + 1)` is

270	Ext X S.X₂ (n₀+1)` makes the connecting morphism δ surjective.

274	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u} C] [Abelian C] [HasExt C]
275	    (X : C) {S : ShortComplex C} (hS : S.ShortExact) [Injective S.X₂]
276	    {n₀ : ℕ} (h_n₀ : 1 ≤ n₀)
277	    (hX₃ : ∀ y : Abelian.Ext X S.X₃ n₀, y = 0)
278	    (x₁ : Abelian.Ext X S.X₁ (n₀ + 1)) : x₁ = 0 := by
279	  have hinj_subs : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext X S.X₂ (n₀ + 1)) :=
280	    HasInjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton S.X₂ 1 (n₀ + 1) (by omega) _
281	  have hker : x₁.comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f)
282	      (show (n₀ + 1) + 0 = (n₀ + 1) by omega) = 0 :=
283	    Subsingleton.elim _ _
284	  obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
285	    Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ X hS x₁ hker (rfl : n₀ + 1 = n₀ + 1)
286	  rw [hX₃ x₃] at hx₃
287	  rw [← hx₃, Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]

289	/-- **Hartshorne II.1, Exercise 1.16(b)** (sections form): the
290	sheaf-morphism `S.g` is sectionwise-surjective on a short exact sequence
291	whose leftmost sheaf is flasque.
292	
293	For a sheaf-level short exact sequence
294	`0 → S.X₁ → S.X₂ → S.X₃ → 0` in `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
295	(ModuleCat kbar)` with `S.X₁` flasque, the section-level map
296	`(S.g.val.app (op U)).hom : S.X₂.val.obj (op U) → S.X₃.val.obj (op U)`
297	is surjective for every open `U`.
298	
299	**Proof sketch** (Hartshorne II.1 Ex 1.16(b), Zorn's lemma):
300	Let `t ∈ S.X₃.val.obj (op U)`. Consider pairs `(V, s)` where `V ⊆ U` is
301	open and `s ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op V)` restricts to `t|_V`. Order by
302	extension. Any chain has an upper bound (sheaf gluing condition on
303	`S.X₂`). By Zorn's lemma, a maximal element `(V₀, s₀)` exists. Suppose
304	`V₀ ≠ U`. Pick `x ∈ U \ V₀`. Stalkwise, `S.X₂_x → S.X₃_x` is surjective
305	(SES in the abelian sheaf category preserves stalks). So in some
306	neighborhood `W` of `x` (after shrinking), there is
307	`s'_W ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op W)` with image `t|_W`. On `V₀ ∩ W`,
308	`s₀ - s'_W` lies in `S.X₁.val.obj (op (V₀ ∩ W))` (since both map to
309	`t|_{V₀∩W}`). Use flasqueness of `S.X₁` to extend the difference to
310	`α ∈ S.X₁.val.obj (op W)`. Set `s'' := s'_W + α`. Then `s''` agrees with
311	`s₀` on `V₀ ∩ W`. By sheaf gluing on `S.X₂`, get a unique
312	`s''' ∈ S.X₂.val.obj (op (V₀ ∪ W))` extending both. This contradicts
313	maximality. Hence `V₀ = U`.
314

316	SES surjection + sheaf gluing + flasqueness extension; estimate
317	~150-200 LOC, scheduled iter-194+. Blueprint reference: out-of-scope
318	subsection of `thm:H1_vanishing_flasque` (the Hartshorne II.1 Ex 1.16(b)
319	input). -/

398	/-- **Auxiliary subsingleton lemma for `HModule_flasque_eq_zero`**
399	(iter-193 Lane H prover dispatch).
400	
401	For a flasque sheaf `F` of `kbar`-modules on `X` and any `n : ℕ`, the
402	`(n+1)`-th derived global-sections cohomology `HModule kbar F (n+1)` is
403	subsingleton (i.e., has at most one element). This statement is the
404	strong-induction-on-`n` carrier of the main theorem
405	`HModule_flasque_eq_zero`: the `F`-generalised quantifier inside the
406	`induction` block lets the inductive step apply the IH on the flasque
407	quotient sheaf at one lower degree. Once subsingleton is established,
408	`Module.finrank_zero_of_subsingleton` gives the `finrank = 0` conclusion
409	in the main statement. -/
410	private theorem Scheme.HModule_flasque_subsingleton_aux
411	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
412	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
413	    [HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))]
414	    (n : ℕ) :
415	    ∀ {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)},
416	      Scheme.IsFlasque F → Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule kbar F (n + 1)) := by
417	  induction n with
418	  | zero =>
419	    intro F hF
420	    refine ⟨fun x y => ?_⟩
421	    -- i = 1 case: use ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective on the
422	    -- canonical injective SES `0 → F → Injective.under F → cokernel → 0`.
423	    have hSES := Scheme.injectiveSES_shortExact F
424	    -- Injective.under F is injective by Mathlib's instance.
425	    have hI_inj : Injective (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₂ := Injective.injective_under F
426	    -- 1.16(b) at U = ⊤ gives section-level surjectivity, which we lift to
427	    -- Hom-from-(constantSheaf k)-surjectivity via the explicit `constantSheaf
428	    -- ⊣ (sheafSections at ⊤)` adjunction (`constantSheafAdj` with terminal
429	    -- `⊤ ∈ Opens X` via `Preorder.isTerminalTop`) and the rank-1 free
430	    -- structure of `ModuleCat.of kbar kbar` (a kbar-linear hom from `kbar`
431	    -- is determined by its image of `1`, with the inverse map
432	    -- `LinearMap.toSpanSingleton kbar _ s` for any chosen lift `s`).
433	    have hsurj : Function.Surjective
434	        (fun (f : (constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
435	            (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) ⟶
436	            (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₂) => f ≫ (Scheme.injectiveSES F).g) := by
437	      intro g
438	      -- Set up the adjunction at the terminal `⊤ ∈ Opens X`.
439	      let hT : Limits.IsTerminal (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :=
440	        Preorder.isTerminalTop (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
441	      let adj := constantSheafAdj (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
442	        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) hT
443	      -- 1.16(b) at U = ⊤ gives section-level surjectivity.
444	      have h_b_top : Function.Surjective
445	          (((Scheme.injectiveSES F).g.hom.app
446	            (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X))).hom) :=
447	        Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective hSES hF ⊤
448	      -- Convert `g` to a section-level morphism via `adj.homEquiv`.
449	      let g_sec : ModuleCat.of kbar kbar ⟶
450	          (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₃.val.obj
451	            (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
452	        adj.homEquiv _ _ g
453	      -- Pick the value of `g_sec` at `1 : kbar`.
454	      let s₃ : (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₃.val.obj
455	          (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) := g_sec.hom 1
456	      -- Apply 1.16(b) at ⊤ to get a lift in `X₂`.
457	      obtain ⟨s₂, hs₂⟩ := h_b_top s₃
458	      -- Construct the section-level lift `f_sec : kbar ⟶ X₂.val.obj (op ⊤)`.
459	      let f_sec : ModuleCat.of kbar kbar ⟶
460	          (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₂.val.obj
461	            (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
462	        ModuleCat.ofHom (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton kbar _ s₂)
463	      -- Lift `f_sec` back to a sheaf-level morphism via `adj.homEquiv.symm`.
464	      refine ⟨(adj.homEquiv _ _).symm f_sec, ?_⟩
465	      -- Verify `(adj.homEquiv.symm f_sec) ≫ S.g = g` by applying `adj.homEquiv`.
466	      apply (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective
467	      rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
468	      change f_sec ≫ ((sheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
469	        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj (Opposite.op ⊤)).map (Scheme.injectiveSES F).g = g_sec
470	      -- Verify the section-level equation at the linear map level.
471	      -- Two `kbar`-linear maps from `kbar` agree iff they agree at `1`.
472	      apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
473	      ext
474	      -- Goal (post `LinearMap.ext_ring`): both sides applied at `1`.
475	      change ((Scheme.injectiveSES F).g.hom.app (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom
476	          ((LinearMap.toSpanSingleton kbar _ s₂) 1) = g_sec.hom 1
477	      rw [LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply_one]
478	      exact hs₂
479	    rw [ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective _ hSES hsurj x,
480	        ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective _ hSES hsurj y]
481	  | succ m ih =>
482	    intro F hF
483	    refine ⟨fun x y => ?_⟩
484	    -- i = m + 2, m + 1 ≥ 1. Use the LES iso via the canonical injective SES,
485	    -- with the flasque quotient G inheriting flasqueness from F + Injective.under F
486	    -- (Hartshorne III Lemma 2.4 + II Ex 1.16(c)). Reduce to IH at degree m + 1.
487	    have hSES := Scheme.injectiveSES_shortExact F
488	    have hI_inj : Injective (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₂ := Injective.injective_under F
489	    have hI_flasque : Scheme.IsFlasque (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₂ :=
490	      Scheme.IsFlasque.injective_flasque _
491	    have h_b : ∀ U, Function.Surjective
492	        (((Scheme.injectiveSES F).g.hom.app (Opposite.op U)).hom) :=
493	      fun U => Scheme.IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective hSES hF U
494	    have hG_flasque : Scheme.IsFlasque (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₃ :=
495	      Scheme.IsFlasque.cokernel_of_shortExact_flasque_flasque hI_flasque h_b
496	    have hG_sub : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₃ (m + 1)) :=
497	      ih hG_flasque
498	    have hX₃_zero :
499	        ∀ y : Abelian.Ext
500	            ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
501	              (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar))
502	            (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₃ (m + 1), y = 0 :=
503	      fun y => Subsingleton.elim (α := Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.injectiveSES F).X₃ (m + 1)) y 0
504	    have h_n₀ : 1 ≤ m + 1 := Nat.succ_le_succ (Nat.zero_le _)
505	    rw [ext_succ_eq_zero_of_injective_of_lower_zero _ hSES h_n₀ hX₃_zero x,
506	        ext_succ_eq_zero_of_injective_of_lower_zero _ hSES h_n₀ hX₃_zero y]

508	/-- **Flasque sheaves have vanishing higher cohomology** (Hartshorne
509	III.2, Proposition 2.5).
510	
511	For a topological space `X` and a flasque sheaf `F` of `kbar`-modules on
512	`X`, the `kbar`-flavoured derived global-sections cohomology
513	`HModule kbar F i` is the zero `kbar`-module for every `i ≥ 1`. In
514	particular, `dim_{kbar} HModule kbar F 1 = 0`.
515	
516	The proof structure mirrors Hartshorne III §2 verbatim: embed `F` into
517	an injective `I` of the abelian sheaf category, form the quotient short
518	exact sequence `0 → F → I → G → 0` (Mathlib: `Scheme.injectiveSES`,
519	axiom-clean), observe that `G` inherits flasqueness from `F` and `I`
520	(the latter by Hartshorne III Lemma 2.4), read off the short exact
521	sequence on global sections from the flasque input
522	(Hartshorne II Ex. 1.16(b)), and apply the long exact sequence
523	(`Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁`) to get `HModule kbar F 1 = 0`
524	and a reduction `HModule kbar F i ≅ HModule kbar G (i - 1)` for `i ≥ 2`;
525	iteration closes the higher-degree cases.
526	
527	**iter-193 Lane H prover dispatch** — body fully closed structurally;
528	sorries are isolated to two named substrate helpers
529	(`shortExact_app_surjective` and `injective_flasque`, both Tier-3).
530	The proof decomposes cleanly into the two cases via the auxiliary
531	subsingleton lemma `Scheme.HModule_flasque_subsingleton_aux`:
532	* `i = 1`: invoke `ext_one_eq_zero_of_hom_surjective_of_injective` on the
533	  `injectiveSES F` short-exact data, supplying the Hartshorne II Ex 1.16(b)
534	  input. The Hom-from-constant-sheaf surjectivity is derived axiom-clean
535	  from `IsFlasque.shortExact_app_surjective` at `U = ⊤` via the explicit
536	  `constantSheafAdj` (at terminal `⊤ ∈ Opens X` via
537	  `Preorder.isTerminalTop`) + the rank-1 free structure of
538	  `ModuleCat.of kbar kbar` (`LinearMap.toSpanSingleton` lift).
539	* `i ≥ 2`: invoke `ext_succ_eq_zero_of_injective_of_lower_zero` on
540	  `injectiveSES F`, supplying `Subsingleton (HModule kbar G (i-1))` via the
541	  IH applied to the flasque quotient `G` (the latter via
542	  `IsFlasque.cokernel_of_shortExact_flasque_flasque` and
543	  `IsFlasque.injective_flasque`).
544	
545	Blueprint reference: `thm:H1_vanishing_flasque`. -/
546	theorem Scheme.HModule_flasque_eq_zero
547	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] {X : TopCat.{u}}
548	    [HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)]
549	    [HasExt (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar))]
550	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)}
551	    (hF : Scheme.IsFlasque F) (i : ℕ) (hi : 1 ≤ i) :
552	    Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F i) = 0 := by
553	  obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ : ∃ n, n + 1 = i := ⟨i - 1, by omega⟩
554	  have hsub : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule kbar F (n + 1)) :=
555	    Scheme.HModule_flasque_subsingleton_aux n hF
556	  exact Module.finrank_zero_of_subsingleton

558	/-! ## §2. Skyscraper sheaves are flasque -/
559	
560	/-- **Skyscraper sheaf as pushforward of a constant sheaf** (Hartshorne
561	II.1, Exercise 1.17).
562	
563	The closed-point skyscraper sheaf `skyscraperSheaf P A` on a topological
564	space `X` is naturally isomorphic to the pushforward, along the constant
565	map `PUnit → X` at `P`, of the constant sheaf with value `A` on
566	`PUnit`. The constant sheaf on `PUnit` is itself isomorphic to the
567	skyscraper sheaf at `PUnit.unit`, so this is the sheaf-level
568	counterpart of Mathlib's presheaf-level
569	`skyscraperPresheaf_eq_pushforward` (Mathlib snapshot `b80f227`
570	`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`).
571	
572	For our usage on a curve, `PUnit` plays the role of `closure {P}`
573	(which on a Noetherian space with `P` closed is a singleton, hence
574	homeomorphic to `PUnit`).
575	
576	**iter-191 Lane H file-skeleton** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry; closure
577	is iter-192+. Blueprint reference:
578	`lem:skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward`. -/
579	theorem Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const
580	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
581	    {X : TopCat.{u}} (P : X)
582	    [∀ U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X, Decidable (P ∈ U)]
583	    [∀ U : TopologicalSpace.Opens (TopCat.of PUnit.{u + 1}),
584	      Decidable (PUnit.unit ∈ U)]
585	    (A : ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :
586	    Nonempty
587	      (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P A ≅
588	        (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)
589	            (TopCat.ofHom (ContinuousMap.const (TopCat.of PUnit.{u + 1}) P))).obj
590	          ((constantSheaf
591	              (Opens.grothendieckTopology (TopCat.of PUnit.{u + 1}))
592	              (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj A)) := by
593	  sorry

595	/-- **Closure of the support point of a `PrimeDivisor` is irreducible**.
596	
597	For any scheme `X` and any `P : X.PrimeDivisor`, the topological
598	closure of the singleton `{P.point}` is an irreducible subset of `X`.
599	This is a project-bespoke ancillary that holds in full generality: the
600	closure of any irreducible set in a topological space is irreducible,
601	and a singleton `{x}` is irreducible (it is nonempty and contains no
602	proper non-empty closed subsets).
603	
604	**iter-191 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed via
605	`isIrreducible_singleton.closure`. Blueprint reference:
606	`lem:closedPoint_closure_irreducible`. -/
607	theorem Scheme.PrimeDivisor.closure_isIrreducible
608	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (P : X.PrimeDivisor) :
609	    IsIrreducible (closure ({P.point} : Set X)) :=
610	  isIrreducible_singleton.closure
611	
612	/-- **The closed-point skyscraper sheaf is flasque**.
613	
614	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / kbar` and a
615	prime divisor `P : C.left.PrimeDivisor` (equivalently, a closed point
616	on the curve), the closed-point skyscraper sheaf
617	`skyscraperSheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)` is flasque as a
618	sheaf of `kbar`-modules on the underlying topological space of `C`.
619	
620	The proof originally was planned to compose the four lemma-blocks of this
621	chapter (`skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const`,
622	`PrimeDivisor.closure_isIrreducible`, `IsFlasque.constant_of_irreducible`,
623	`IsFlasque.pushforward`). The iter-191 prover dispatch takes a more direct
624	route: unfold `skyscraperSheaf.val = skyscraperPresheaf`, then on the
625	`p ∈ V` branch the restriction map is an `eqToHom` (hence iso, hence
626	surjective in `ConcreteCategory`); on the `p ∉ V` branch the codomain is
627	the terminal object of `ModuleCat kbar`, which is the zero object, hence
628	`Subsingleton` on the underlying type. Either branch closes the
629	`Function.Surjective` obligation. This bypasses (3) and (5).
630	
631	**iter-191 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed directly via
632	`skyscraperPresheaf_map`. Blueprint reference:
633	`lem:skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque`. -/
634	theorem Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque
635	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
636	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
637	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
638	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
639	    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
640	    [∀ U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left, Decidable (P.point ∈ U)] :
641	    Scheme.IsFlasque
642	      (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
643	        (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) := by
644	  intro U V h
645	  change Function.Surjective
646	    (((skyscraperPresheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).map
647	      (homOfLE h).op).hom)
648	  by_cases hV : P.point ∈ V
649	  · -- restriction is `eqToHom`, hence an iso, hence surjective
650	    have heq : (skyscraperPresheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).obj
651	        (Opposite.op U) =
652	        (skyscraperPresheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).obj
653	        (Opposite.op V) := by
654	      simp [skyscraperPresheaf, h hV, hV]
655	    have hmap := skyscraperPresheaf_map P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)
656	      (i := (homOfLE h).op)
657	    rw [dif_pos hV] at hmap
658	    rw [hmap]
659	    have : IsIso (eqToHom heq) := inferInstance
660	    exact (ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (eqToHom heq)).2
661	  · -- codomain is zero (terminal in ModuleCat), so surjective trivially
662	    have hzero : Limits.IsZero
663	        ((skyscraperPresheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).obj
664	          (Opposite.op V)) := by
665	      simp [skyscraperPresheaf, hV]
666	      exact (terminalIsTerminal).isZero
667	    have : Subsingleton ((skyscraperPresheaf P.point
668	        (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
669	      ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero hzero
670	    intro y; exact ⟨0, Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
671	
672	/-! ## §3. The closed-point skyscraper sheaf has vanishing `H¹` -/
673	
674	/-- **`H¹` of the closed-point skyscraper sheaf vanishes** (Hartshorne
675	III.2.5 applied to the flasque skyscraper).
676	
677	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / kbar` and a
678	prime divisor `P : C.left.PrimeDivisor` (a closed point on the curve),
679	`dim_{kbar} H¹(C, skyscraperSheaf P (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) = 0`.
680	
681	The proof composes the two substrate inputs of this chapter:
682	`skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque` (the skyscraper sheaf is flasque) and
683	`HModule_flasque_eq_zero` at `i = 1` (flasque ⇒ `H¹ = 0`); then
684	`Module.finrank kbar 0 = 0` closes the dimension identity.
685	
686	**iter-191 Lane H prover dispatch** — closed via composition of
687	`HModule_flasque_eq_zero` (still typed-`sorry` pending iter-192+
688	Hartshorne III.2.5 closure) and `skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque` (now closed
689	directly in this file) at `i = 1`. The same name also occurs as a
690	`private` typed-`sorry` helper at
691	`AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`; the `private` modifier
692	mangles that copy's internal name, so the public name resolved by the
693	blueprint's `\lean{...}` pin (and by `sync_leanok`) is the declaration
694	below. Blueprint reference:
695	`lem:H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero_main`. -/
696	theorem Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero
697	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
698	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
699	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
700	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
701	    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
702	    [∀ U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left, Decidable (P.point ∈ U)] :
703	    Module.finrank kbar
704	        (Scheme.HModule kbar
705	          (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
706	            (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) 1) = 0 :=
707	  Scheme.HModule_flasque_eq_zero (Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque C P) 1 le_rfl
708	
709	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38	
39	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
40	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
41	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
42	
43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/

549	`gmScalingP1_cover` (the `D₊(X 1)` chart of `ℙ¹`) as the composition
550	`Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] ↪ Spec k̄[u] = D₊(X 1) ↪ ℙ¹`
551	where the first map is the open immersion `D(t) ⊂ 𝔸¹` (localization at `t`) and the second
552	is `Proj.awayι` (open immersion onto the basic open `D₊(X 1)`).

554	**Status (iter-182 PARTIAL — kernel-clean modulo `iotaGm_isOpenImmersion`).** The
555	open-range claim is reduced to the strictly-stronger `IsOpenImmersion` claim
556	(`iotaGm_isOpenImmersion`) via `IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range`; the substantive content

558	private lemma iotaGm_range_isOpen [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
559	    IsOpen (Set.range ⇑((lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar)
560	        (𝟙 (Gm kbar))).left ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar).left)) := by

573	substantive chart-1 open-immersion identification, gated on Lane B). The structural derivation
574	of `DenseRange` from `IsOpen (range f) + Nonempty (range f) + IrreducibleSpace target` is

577	private lemma iotaGm_isDominant [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
578	    IsDominant (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
579	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar).left := by
580	  -- Reduce `IsDominant` to `DenseRange` on the composed underlying continuous map.
581	  rw [Over.comp_left]
582	  refine ⟨?_⟩
583	  -- `IrreducibleSpace ℙ¹.left` from `GeometricallyIrreducible PLB.hom` + `Subsingleton Spec k̄`.
584	  haveI : Subsingleton ↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := inferInstance
585	  haveI hirr : IrreducibleSpace (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left :=
586	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton
587	      (f := (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
588	  -- `Nonempty Gm.left` from `PrimeSpectrum`-of-nontrivial-ring.
589	  haveI : Nonempty (Gm kbar).left := inferInstance
590	  -- Closes via `IsOpen.dense`: in a preirreducible space, any non-empty open is dense.
591	  -- `Set.range_nonempty` gives non-emptiness from the source `Gm.left` being non-empty;
592	  -- `iotaGm_range_isOpen` packages the substantive chart-1 open-immersion identification

594	  exact (iotaGm_range_isOpen.dense (Set.range_nonempty _))

596	/-- **Helper (pointed case): a morphism `f : ℙ¹ → A` killing `0 ∈ ℙ¹` is the constant `1`.**
597	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, if `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` satisfies
598	`ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]`, then `f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)`
599	(equivalently `f = toUnit ProjectiveLineBar ≫ η[A]`).
600	
601	This is the pointed core of the `𝔾ₘ`-scaling shortcut: form `h := gmScalingP1 ≫ f`, feed it
602	to Cor 1.5 (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) with `V = ProjectiveLineBar`, `W = Gm`, base
603	points `zeroPt`, `onePt`. The `W`-axis collapses by `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, leaving
604	`h = fst ≫ fV` (the relation `f(λx) = fV(x)`). Specialising at `x = 1` (via the canonical
605	inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` given by `λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ`) and using density of `Gm ⊆ ℙ¹` plus
606	separatedness of `A` (via `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`, the same Mathlib bridge
607	`rigidity_core` uses inline), we conclude `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV)`. The basepoint
608	hypothesis then pins `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]`.
609	
610	**Status (iter-167):** body fully refactored — all five in-line product/Proj `sorry`s have
611	been eliminated. Four of them (`GeometricallyIrreducible`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`, `IsReduced`
612	of the product, and `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`) ship from Lane A
613	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` instances `projGm_geomIrred`,
614	`projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`, `projectiveLineBar_isReduced`) and resolve
615	by `infer_instance` in scope. The fifth — dominance of the canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` map — is
616	exposed as the named top-level bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` above (a single `sorry` pending
617	Lane A's `gmScalingP1` body). The helper itself contains no `sorry`. -/
618	private theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
619	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
620	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
621	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
622	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)
623	    (hf0 : ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]) :
624	    f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
625	  -- W-axis collapse via `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, precomposed with `onePt`:
626	  -- `lift zeroPt onePt ≫ gmScalingP1 = zeroPt`.
627	  have hcollapse :
628	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar) ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar
629	        = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
630	    have hbase := gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero kbar
631	    -- Rewrite `lift zeroPt onePt = onePt ≫ lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm)`.
632	    have hreshape :
633	        lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
634	          = Gm.onePt kbar ≫
635	            lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) := by
636	      rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id]
637	      congr 1
638	      rw [← Category.assoc,
639	        toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
640	    rw [hreshape, Category.assoc, hbase, ← Category.assoc,
641	      toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
642	  -- The Cor 1.5 basepoint hypothesis `lift v₀ w₀ ≫ (σ ≫ f) = η[A]`.
643	  have hcorhyp :
644	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
645	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = η[A] := by
646	    rw [← Category.assoc, hcollapse]; exact hf0
647	  -- Apply Cor 1.5.
648	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
649	    (V := ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (W := Gm kbar)
650	    (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
651	    (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) hcorhyp
652	  -- W-axis restriction: `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ σ ≫ f = (1 : Gm ⟶ A)`.
653	  have hfW :
654	      lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
655	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = (1 : Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
656	    rw [← Category.assoc, gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero, Category.assoc, hf0]
657	    exact Hom.one_def.symm
658	  -- `snd ≫ fW = snd ≫ 1 = 1` (using `toUnit` uniqueness).
659	  have hSndFW :
660	      snd (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)
661	        ≫ (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
662	          ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f))
663	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
664	    rw [hfW, Hom.one_def, ← Category.assoc]
665	    congr 1
666	    exact toUnit_unique _ _
667	  -- `key` now reads `σ ≫ f = fst ≫ fV` (after collapsing the W-axis to 1).
668	  rw [hSndFW, _root_.mul_one] at key
669	  -- Name the V-axis restriction `fV`.
670	  set fV : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A :=
671	    lift (𝟙 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ Gm.onePt kbar)
672	      ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) with hfVdef
673	  -- Precompose `key` with `gmInP1 := lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm) : Gm → ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm`
674	  -- ("λ ↦ (1, λ)"). The resulting morphism `ι := gmInP1 ≫ σ : Gm → ℙ¹` is the canonical
675	  -- inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` ("λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ"); on its image, `f` is constant at `onePt ≫ fV`.
676	  set iotaGm : Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
677	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
678	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar with hιdef
679	  have hιf :
680	      iotaGm ≫ f = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
681	    calc iotaGm ≫ f

682	        = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
683	            ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) := by rw [hιdef, Category.assoc]
684	      _ = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
685	            ≫ (fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar) ≫ fV) := by rw [key]
686	      _ = (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
687	            ≫ fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)) ≫ fV := by
688	          rw [Category.assoc]
689	      _ = (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) ≫ fV := by rw [lift_fst]
690	      _ = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
691	          rw [Category.assoc]
692	  -- Separatedness of the target `A` over `Spec k̄` in `OverClass.fromOver` form.
693	  haveI hAsep : IsSeparated A.hom := IsProper.toIsSeparated
694	  haveI : IsSeparated (A.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := hAsep
695	  -- `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` and the product geom-irred / LOFT / IsReduced
696	  -- instances are all auto-resolved via Lane A's exports in `Genus0BaseObjects`
697	  -- (`projectiveLineBar_isReduced`, `projGm_geomIrred`, `projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`,
698	  -- `projGm_isReduced`). Dominance of `ι.left` is the only file-local bridge, cited
699	  -- explicitly via `iotaGm_isDominant` (the `set`-binding `hιdef` exposes `iotaGm.left`
700	  -- definitionally as `(lift _ _ ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar).left`).
701	  haveI hιDom : IsDominant iotaGm.left := iotaGm_isDominant
702	  -- Globalise: `ι ≫ f = ι ≫ (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV))` gives, by dominance, `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ c`.
703	  have hf_eq :
704	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
705	    -- Promote the Over morphism equality from the underlying scheme equality.
706	    have hgoal :
707	        iotaGm ≫ f
708	          = iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
709	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)) := by
710	      have hreshape :
711	          iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
712	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV))
713	            = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
714	        rw [← Category.assoc,
715	          toUnit_unique (iotaGm ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (Gm kbar))]
716	      rw [hreshape, hιf]
717	    refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
718	    refine ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of kbar))
719	      (X := (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left) (Y := A.left)
720	      (f := f.left)
721	      (g := (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
722	        (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)).left) iotaGm.left ?_
723	    rw [← Over.comp_left, ← Over.comp_left]
724	    exact congrArg Over.Hom.left hgoal
725	  -- Pin `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]` via the basepoint hypothesis `hf0`.
726	  have hc : ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV = η[A] := by
727	    have hcomp := hf0
728	    rw [hf_eq] at hcomp
729	    rw [← Category.assoc,
730	      toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
731	        (𝟙 _),
732	      Category.id_comp] at hcomp
733	    exact hcomp
734	  rw [hf_eq, hc, ← Hom.one_def]
735	
736	/-- **A morphism `ℙ¹ → A` is constant.** Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
737	morphism `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` from the projective line into an abelian variety
738	`A` (a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme) is constant: it factors through
739	a single `k̄`-point `a₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ A`, i.e. `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
740	
741	The single-curve base case of Milne's Proposition 3.10. **Route resolved iter-164: the
742	𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut** — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`,
743	char-general. The proof body proceeds by translating in the group `A` to reduce to the
744	basepoint case `zeroPt ≫ f = η[A]` (handled by the helper
745	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`), then un-translating.
746	
747	See blueprint `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`
748	(Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10).
749	
750	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via the helper's residuals
751	(three product-instance Mathlib bridges + dominance of the canonical `Gm → ℙ¹` map). Lifts
752	to axiom-clean once those are discharged. -/
753	theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const
754	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
755	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
756	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
757	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) :
758	    ∃ a₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ A,
759	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ a₀ := by
760	  -- Witness: `a₀ := zeroPt ≫ f` (the value of `f` at the scaling fixed point `0 ∈ ℙ¹`).
761	  refine ⟨ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f, ?_⟩
762	  -- Translate: form `f' := f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)` and apply the helper to `f'`.
763	  have hf' :
764	      (f / (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f))
765	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
766	    apply morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
767	    -- Show `zeroPt ≫ (f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)) = η[A]`.
768	    rw [GrpObj.comp_div]
769	    have hv :
770	        ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫
771	            toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
772	              (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f)
773	          = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f := by
774	      rw [← Category.assoc,
775	        toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
776	          (𝟙 _),
777	        Category.id_comp]
778	    rw [hv, div_self', Hom.one_def, toUnit_unique (toUnit _) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
779	  -- Untranslate: `f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀) = 1` ⟺ `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
780	  exact div_eq_one.mp hf'

782	/-- **A genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`.** Over an algebraically closed field
783	`k̄`, a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` with `genus C = 0` is isomorphic — in
784	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))` — to the concrete projective line `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`.
785	
786	Hartshorne's Example IV.1.3.5 (Riemann–Roch). Its formalisation is a genuine sub-build:
787	Mathlib has no Riemann–Roch for curves; this is the dominant long pole flagged by the
788	iter-164 progress-critic.
789	
790	See blueprint `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
791	(Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
792	
793	**Status (iter-166):** signature refactored to the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`; body
794	remains `sorry` (RR bridge — iter-167+). -/
795	theorem genusZero_curve_iso_P1
796	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
797	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
798	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
799	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0) :
800	    Nonempty (C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :=
801	  sorry
802	
803	/-- **Headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-`0` curve.** Over an algebraically closed field
804	`k̄` (arbitrary characteristic — no `[CharZero kbar]`), let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically
805	irreducible curve with `genus C = 0` and `A` an abelian variety (smooth proper geom-irred group
806	scheme). Then every morphism `f : C ⟶ A` killing a `k̄`-point `p` (`p ≫ f = η[A]`) equals the
807	constant morphism at the identity, `f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
808	
809	This is the project's committed characteristic-free statement that `genusZeroWitness` consumes
810	(via the `k̄ → k` descent step hosted in `AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian`). Its signature mirrors
811	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` **verbatim except**
812	the `[CharZero kbar]` instance is dropped. Combine `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (`C ≅ ℙ¹`) with
813	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (`ℙ¹ → A` constant) and pin the constant value to `η[A]` via
814	the pointed hypothesis. No `df = 0`, no Serre duality, no Picard representability.
815	
816	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
817	(RR bridge, iter-167+) and `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`'s helper residuals. Once the RR
818	bridge closes and the helper's product-instance + dominance sorries discharge, lifts to
819	axiom-clean. -/
820	theorem rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme
821	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
822	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
823	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
824	    [IsProper C.hom]
825	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
826	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
827	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
828	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
829	    (f : C ⟶ A)
830	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
831	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
832	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) := by
833	  -- Transport `f` along `C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` to a morphism `g : ℙ¹ ⟶ A`.
834	  obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus
835	  set g : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A := φ.inv ≫ f with hgdef
836	  -- `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` gives `g = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀` for some `a₀`.
837	  obtain ⟨a₀, hga₀⟩ := morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g
838	  -- Pin `a₀ = η[A]` via the pointed hypothesis on `f`.
839	  have hpoint : (p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ g = η[A] := by
840	    rw [hgdef, Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc φ.hom, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
841	    exact _hf
842	  have hcst : a₀ = η[A] := by
843	    rw [hga₀, ← Category.assoc,
844	      toUnit_unique ((p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (𝟙 _),
845	      Category.id_comp] at hpoint
846	    exact hpoint
847	  rw [hcst] at hga₀
848	  -- Back-transport: `f = φ.hom ≫ g = φ.hom ≫ toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ η[A] = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
849	  calc f
850	      = φ.hom ≫ φ.inv ≫ f := by
851	        rw [← Category.assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
852	    _ = φ.hom ≫ g := by rw [hgdef]
853	    _ = φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ η[A] := by rw [hga₀]
854	    _ = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
855	        rw [← Category.assoc,
856	          toUnit_unique (φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit C)]
857	
858	
859	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	/-!
11	# The identity component of the Picard scheme (A.3)
12	
13	This file is the **A.3** sub-build chapter for the project's positive-genus arm
14	of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It scaffolds the abstract identity-component
15	substrate for a `k`-group scheme locally of finite type and specialises it to
16	`G = Pic_{C/k}`, packaging:
17	
18	1. the open-and-closed subgroup-scheme structure of the identity component,
19	2. the degree map `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`,
20	3. the abelian-variety identification of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (the Jacobian variety
21	   of `C` when `C/k` is a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of
22	   positive genus).
23	
24	## Status (iter-185 NEW file-skeleton)
25

94	/-! ## §1. The identity component of a group scheme — abstract substrate
95	
96	The identity component is an abstract feature of `k`-group schemes locally of
97	finite type. We package the definition + open-and-closed-subgroup-scheme
98	structure in the `GroupScheme` namespace so they are reusable outside the
99	Picard context.
100	
101	In this project, a "`k`-group scheme" is encoded as an
102	`Over (Spec (.of k))`-object `G` carrying a `[GrpObj G]` instance (the
103	group-object structure in the over-category). The locally-of-finite-type
104	hypothesis is the `[LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]` instance on the structural
105	morphism to the base.
106	
107	Blueprint references: `def:identity_component_group_scheme` +
108	`thm:identity_component_open_subgroup` (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`). -/
109	
110	namespace GroupScheme
111	
112	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, the set of
113	connected components is finite.
114	
115	Proof: the map `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α` sending each
116	irreducible component `C` to the connected component of an arbitrary chosen
117	point of `C` is well-defined (because `C` is preconnected, hence contained in
118	a single connected component) and surjective (because every point lies in
119	some irreducible component, namely `irreducibleComponent x`, which is a
120	subset of `connectedComponent x`). The source is finite by
121	`TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`, so the
122	target is finite by `Finite.of_surjective`. -/
123	private lemma noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
124	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α] :
125	    Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := by
126	  classical
127	  have hfin : (irreducibleComponents α).Finite :=
128	    TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
129	  haveI : Finite ↥(irreducibleComponents α) := hfin.to_subtype
130	  refine Finite.of_surjective
131	    (fun C : ↥(irreducibleComponents α) =>
132	      (ConnectedComponents.mk : α → ConnectedComponents α)
133	        ((C.property : Maximal IsIrreducible C.val).prop.nonempty.some)) ?_
134	  intro c
135	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe c
136	  refine ⟨⟨irreducibleComponent x, irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents x⟩, ?_⟩
137	  rw [ConnectedComponents.coe_eq_coe]
138	  refine (connectedComponent_eq ?_).symm
139	  exact irreducibleComponent_subset_connectedComponent
140	    (isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent (x := x)).nonempty.some_mem
141	
142	/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, each
143	connected component is open.
144	
145	Proof: `ConnectedComponents α` is totally disconnected
146	(`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1
147	(`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by
148	`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`. A finite T1 space is discrete
149	(`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`), so the singleton
150	`{ConnectedComponents.mk x}` is open in the quotient. The preimage of this
151	singleton under the continuous quotient map `ConnectedComponents.mk` is
152	`connectedComponent x` (`connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`), which is
153	therefore open. -/
154	private lemma noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent
155	    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α]
156	    (x : α) :
157	    IsOpen (connectedComponent x) := by
158	  haveI : Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
159	  haveI : DiscreteTopology (ConnectedComponents α) := inferInstance
160	  have h := (isOpen_discrete
161	    ({(ConnectedComponents.mk x : ConnectedComponents α)} : Set _)).preimage
162	    ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe
163	  rwa [connectedComponents_preimage_singleton] at h
164	
165	/-- Helper (iter-188): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
166	underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
167	morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.
168	
169	The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
170	space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
171	Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
172	spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
173	IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
174	`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
175	implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
176	is the iter-188 project-side helper (`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`
177	+ `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent`).
178	
179	The classical proof: each point `y ∈ G.left` has an open affine
180	neighbourhood `W = Spec R` with `R` Noetherian, hence `|W|` is a Noetherian
181	topological space; in a Noetherian space, each `connectedComponent y` is
182	clopen (finite irreducible components ⟹ finite connected components ⟹
183	finite T1 quotient ⟹ discrete quotient ⟹ singletons clopen in quotient ⟹
184	preimages clopen). Pulled back along the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left`,
185	this gives an open connected neighbourhood of `y` inside any open `F`
186	containing `y`. -/
187	private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
189	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
190	    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
191	    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
192	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom
193	  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected]
194	  intro x F hF
195	  -- Extract an open neighbourhood `T ⊆ F` of `x`.
196	  obtain ⟨T, hTF, hT, hxT⟩ := mem_nhds_iff.mp hF
197	  -- Find an affine open `W ⊆ T` with `x ∈ W`.
198	  obtain ⟨W, hW, hxW, hWT⟩ :=
199	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := G.left) (U := ⟨T, hT⟩) hxT
200	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(G.left, W) :=
201	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨W, hW⟩
202	  haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace ↥W :=
203	    noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen W hW
204	  -- Inside `W` (Noetherian) the connected component of `x` is open; its image
205	  -- under the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left` is the connected open neighbourhood
206	  -- we want.
207	  let xW : ↥W := ⟨x, hxW⟩
208	  refine ⟨(Subtype.val : ↥W → G.left) '' connectedComponent xW, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
209	  · rintro _ ⟨z, _, rfl⟩
210	    exact hTF (hWT z.2)
211	  · exact W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val _
212	      (noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent xW)
213	  · exact ⟨xW, mem_connectedComponent, rfl⟩
214	  · exact isConnected_connectedComponent.image _ continuous_subtype_val.continuousOn
215	
216	/-- The image of the identity section `e : Spec k → G` (well-defined as a
217	single point of `|G|` because `Spec k` is a topological singleton). -/
218	private noncomputable def identitySectionPoint
219	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] : G.left :=
220	  ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base :
221	      ↥(Spec (.of k)) → G.left) (default : Spec (.of k))
222	
223	/-- Helper (iter-186; iter-187 closed): the **clopen carrier `Set`** of
224	the identity component of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type,
225	packaged as a `G.left.Opens`. The carrier set is `connectedComponent x`
226	for `x = e(*)` the image of the identity section
227	(`identitySectionPoint G`); openness is `isOpen_connectedComponent` which
228	needs the `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance above
229	(EGA I 6.1.9). -/
230	private noncomputable def identityComponentCarrier {k : Type u} [Field k]
231	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
232	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
233	    G.left.Opens :=
234	  ⟨connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G), isOpen_connectedComponent⟩
235	
236	/-- The **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
237	finite type.
238	
239	Encoded as a `k`-scheme: an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`, carrying the
240	intended substantive identity "this is the connected component of `|G|`
241	containing the identity section `e`, equipped with the open-subscheme
242	structure inherited from `G`". The associated open immersion
243	`IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` is the content of
244	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`.
245	
246	iter-186 body: built from the `identityComponentCarrier G` helper — the
247	open subscheme of `G` whose underlying topological space is the connected
248	component of `|G|` through the image of the identity section. The
249	structure morphism is the inherited `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`.
250	The substantive content (the actual carrier `Set`, plus its openness from
251	EGA I 6.1.9: locally Noetherian spaces have open connected components)
252	lives in the typed-sorry body of `identityComponentCarrier`. -/
253	noncomputable def IdentityComponent {k : Type u} [Field k]
254	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
255	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
256	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
257	  Over.mk ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
258	
259	/-- **The identity component is an open and closed subgroup scheme.**
260	
261	The bundled statement of Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3): the identity
262	component `G^0 = IdentityComponent G` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
263	finite type comes with a morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` (in
264	`Over (Spec (.of k))`) whose underlying scheme morphism is both an open
265	immersion and a closed immersion (i.e. the inclusion of a clopen
266	subscheme).
267	
268	The full Kleiman conclusion also packages the group-subscheme property (the
269	inclusion is a homomorphism of `k`-group schemes), finite-type-ness over `k`
270	(`LocallyOfFiniteType` + quasi-compactness), geometric irreducibility, and
271	base-change-commutation. Those refinements live as separate instances /
272	follow-up lemmas in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the clopen
273	open-immersion conclusion as a Nonempty-witness.
274	
275	iter-186 body: the inclusion morphism is `Over.homMk (identityComponentCarrier G).ι`,
276	with the over-category compatibility holding by definition of
277	`IdentityComponent G`. The `.left` of this morphism is
278	`(identityComponentCarrier G).ι`, an open immersion by the global
279	`Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι` instance. For the closed-immersion
280	half we apply `IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion` to the open immersion
281	and reduce to `IsClosed (↑(identityComponentCarrier G) : Set _)`. The
282	remaining inline `sorry` is the substantive content: the carrier of
283	`identityComponentCarrier G` is closed, by "closure of a connected subspace
284	is connected" applied to the connected component through the image of the
285	identity section. -/
286	theorem IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
287	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
288	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
289	    Nonempty {f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G //
290	        IsOpenImmersion f.left ∧ IsClosedImmersion f.left} := by
291	  -- Over-category compatibility for the underlying open immersion.
292	  have hcomp : (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
293	    simp [IdentityComponent]
294	  -- The over-morphism witness.
295	  let f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G :=
296	    Over.homMk (U := IdentityComponent G) (V := G) (identityComponentCarrier G).ι hcomp
297	  refine ⟨⟨f, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
298	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` (definitionally; `Over.homMk_left` simp).
299	    -- An open immersion by `Scheme.Opens.instIsOpenImmersionι`.
300	    change IsOpenImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
301	    infer_instance
302	  · -- `f.left = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι` is a preimmersion (open immersion ⟹
303	    -- immersion ⟹ preimmersion). Its range is the carrier set (`Scheme.Opens.range_ι`),
304	    -- and closure of that connected subset is itself (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3):
305	    -- the connected component through the identity is closed in `|G|`).
306	    change IsClosedImmersion (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
307	    apply IsClosedImmersion.of_isPreimmersion
308	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
309	    -- The carrier set is `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`, which is
310	    -- closed by `isClopen_connectedComponent.1` (with the
311	    -- `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace` instance providing the closedness
312	    -- half of EGA I 6.1.9: connected components of a locally Noetherian topological
313	    -- space are clopen).
314	    change IsClosed (connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G))
315	    exact isClopen_connectedComponent.1

317	/-! ### iter-192 Lane A.3.i: identity-component substrate
318	
319	Per `analogies/lane-a3i-isconnected-prod.md`, the substrate for the
320	group-structure inheritance and base-change-commutation arguments is
321	Stacks Tag 04KU / EGA IV₂ 4.5.14 (a connected `k`-scheme with a
322	`k`-rational section is geometrically connected) combined with Mathlib's
323	`ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance for
324	`[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f] [ConnectedSpace Y]`.
325	
326	This iter (Lane A.3.i): we add the AXIOM-CLEAN
327	`identityComponentCarrier_connectedSpace` helper below (the carrier is
328	connected by construction). The full Stacks 04KU bridge
329	"`ConnectedSpace X` + section ⟹ `GeometricallyConnected f`" requires
330	the Mathlib substrate "`X` connected with `k` algebraically closed in
331	`Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` ⟹ `X` geometrically connected over `k`" (Stacks 037Q +
332	04KU), not yet available in Mathlib at SHA b80f227 — pending that
333	Mathlib lemma, the geometric-connectedness step lives as a residual
334	sorry inside the downstream theorems
335	(`isSubgroupHomomorphism`, `baseChangeIso`, `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible`).
336	
337	Below: `baseChangeIso` partially closes via
338	`CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd` (iter-192 axiom-clean closure
339	of 2 of the 3 conjuncts; the third conjunct, the iso of identity-component
340	constructions, remains sorry pending the Stacks 04KU substrate). -/
341	
342	/-- Helper (iter-192 Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean): the **identity component
343	carrier** has connected underlying topological space.
344	
345	The carrier is defined as `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`
346	in `|G|` (a `G.left.Opens`), and its subspace topology coincides with the
347	open-subscheme topology; the subspace is connected because the connected
348	component is preconnected (Mathlib's `isPreconnected_connectedComponent`)
349	and nonempty (contains the identity point `identitySectionPoint G`). -/
350	private instance identityComponentCarrier_connectedSpace
351	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
352	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
353	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
354	    ConnectedSpace ((identityComponentCarrier G : G.left.Opens) : Type _) := by
355	  haveI : PreconnectedSpace ((identityComponentCarrier G : G.left.Opens) : Type _) :=
356	    Subtype.preconnectedSpace isPreconnected_connectedComponent
357	  exact ⟨⟨⟨identitySectionPoint G, mem_connectedComponent⟩⟩⟩
358	
359	/-- Helper (iter-192 Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean): the **identity component**
360	`IdentityComponent G` has connected underlying topological space.
361	
362	By definition `(IdentityComponent G).left` is the open subscheme
363	`identityComponentCarrier G : G.left.Opens` regarded as a scheme; its
364	underlying topological space coincides definitionally with the carrier's
365	subspace topology, so `ConnectedSpace` transports via
366	`identityComponentCarrier_connectedSpace` above.
367	
368	Downstream this combines with the (pending) Stacks 04KU substrate
369	`GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom` plus Mathlib's
370	`ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance for
371	`[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f] [ConnectedSpace Y]`
372	to give the key "`G⁰ ×_k G⁰` is connected" substrate for Kleiman's
373	group-structure inheritance argument in `isSubgroupHomomorphism`. -/
374	private instance identityComponent_connectedSpace
375	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
376	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
377	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
378	    ConnectedSpace (IdentityComponent G).left := by
379	  change ConnectedSpace ((identityComponentCarrier G : G.left.Opens) : Type _)
380	  infer_instance
381	
382	/-- **Stacks 04KU / EGA IV₂ 4.5.14**: a connected `k`-scheme with a `k`-rational
383	section is geometrically connected.
384	
385	Given a morphism `f : X ⟶ Spec k` from a `ConnectedSpace`-typed scheme `X`
386	admitting a section `s : Spec k ⟶ X` (i.e. `s ≫ f = 𝟙`), the morphism `f` is
387	geometrically connected: for any field extension `K/k`, the pullback
388	`X ×_{Spec k} Spec K` is connected.
389	
390	iter-193 Lane A.3.i, planner-set authorisation per `PROGRESS.md`: ships as a
391	typed `sorry` to enable downstream consumers (sanctioned temporary sorry-count
392	increase). The full body is Stacks 04KV: a hypothetical clopen partition
393	`X_K = U ⊔ V` would, via the base-changed section `s_K = s ≫ (pullback π s)⁻¹`
394	say `K`-rational point landing in exactly one of `U, V`; the image of the
395	chosen piece under the projection `X_K → X` would then be a clopen subset
396	of `X` whose disjointness from the image of the other piece contradicts
397	`ConnectedSpace X`. Mathlib at SHA b80f227 lacks the descent-of-clopen-
398	partitions-along-fpqc-base-change substrate (Stacks 02LB), preventing an
399	axiom-clean closure this iter.
400	
401	Alternative proof via Stacks 037Q (also project-side, also not yet built):
402	a `k`-scheme `X` is geometrically connected iff `X` is connected AND the
403	algebraic closure of `k` in `Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` equals `k`. With the section `s`,
404	the pullback `s^* : Γ(X, 𝒪_X) → k` is a `k`-algebra retraction; any subfield
405	`F ⊆ Γ(X, 𝒪_X)` containing `k` maps injectively into `k` (as `F` is a field),
406	forcing `F = k`. Combined with `ConnectedSpace X`, gives the conclusion.
407	
408	Downstream consumers:
409	- iso slot of `IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso` (the `(G^0)_K ≅ (G_K)^0`
410	  identification);
411	- chain into `[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f]
412	  [ConnectedSpace Y] ⟹ ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` to derive
413	  `ConnectedSpace (G^0 ×_k G^0)` for `isSubgroupHomomorphism`. -/
414	private theorem geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section
415	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}}
416	    (f : X ⟶ Spec (.of k))
417	    (s : Spec (.of k) ⟶ X) (_hsf : s ≫ f = 𝟙 _)
418	    [ConnectedSpace X] :
419	    GeometricallyConnected f :=
420	  sorry
421	
422	/-- The range-containment hypothesis for `IsOpenImmersion.lift` used to build
423	the section `identityComponentSection G` below: the image of the identity
424	section `MonObj.one.left : Spec k ⟶ G.left` lies in the carrier of the
425	identity component (a singleton image `{identitySectionPoint G}` contained
426	in `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G) = identityComponentCarrier G`). -/
427	private lemma identityComponentSection_range_subset
428	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
429	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
430	    Set.range ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left).base ⊆
431	      Set.range (identityComponentCarrier G).ι.base := by
432	  rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
433	  let f : ↥(Spec (.of k)) → G.left := (MonObj.one (X := G)).left.base
434	  change Set.range f ⊆ _
435	  rintro y ⟨x, rfl⟩
436	  have hx : x = default := Subsingleton.elim _ _
437	  rw [hx]
438	  -- `f default = identitySectionPoint G` (by definition of
439	  -- `identitySectionPoint`); the carrier underlying set is
440	  -- `connectedComponent (identitySectionPoint G)`.
441	  exact mem_connectedComponent
442	
443	private noncomputable def identityComponentSection
444	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
445	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
446	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
447	    Spec (.of k) ⟶ (IdentityComponent G).left :=
448	  @IsOpenImmersion.lift (identityComponentCarrier G).toScheme (Spec (.of k))
449	    G.left (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
450	    ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left)
451	    inferInstance (identityComponentSection_range_subset G)

453	/-- Helper (iter-193 Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean): the **identity section lift is
454	a section of `(IdentityComponent G).hom`**.
455	
456	Composing `identityComponentSection G` with `(IdentityComponent G).hom`
457	returns the identity on `Spec k`, by `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac` plus the
458	over-compatibility `MonObj.one.left ≫ G.hom = 𝟙` (the terminal of
459	`Over (Spec k)` has `.hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)` definitionally). -/
460	private lemma identityComponentSection_isSection
461	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
462	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
463	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
464	    identityComponentSection G ≫ (IdentityComponent G).hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of k)) := by
465	  -- `(IdentityComponent G).hom = (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`
466	  -- (definitionally, by the `IdentityComponent` def).
467	  change identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom = _
468	  rw [← Category.assoc]
469	  -- `identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι = MonObj.one.left`
470	  -- by `IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac` (after unfolding the `identityComponentSection`
471	  -- def to expose the `IsOpenImmersion.lift` head).
472	  rw [show identityComponentSection G ≫ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι =
473	        ((MonObj.one (X := G)).left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left) from
474	      IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ (identityComponentSection_range_subset G)]
475	  -- Now: `(MonObj.one (X := G)).left ≫ G.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of k))`.
476	  -- This is the over-morphism compatibility: for the terminal of
477	  -- `Over (Spec k)`, `(MonObj.one).w` says
478	  -- `MonObj.one.left ≫ G.hom = (𝟙_).hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)` (defeq).
479	  exact (MonObj.one (X := G)).w
480	
481	/-- Instance (iter-193 Lane A.3.i, propagates through the sorry-bodied
482	`geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` helper): the structural
483	morphism `(IdentityComponent G).hom` is **geometrically connected**.
484	
485	Derived from:
486	- `identityComponent_connectedSpace`: `ConnectedSpace (IdentityComponent G).left`
487	  (axiom-clean iter-192).
488	- `identityComponentSection_isSection`: existence of a section
489	  `Spec k ⟶ (IdentityComponent G).left` (axiom-clean iter-193).
490	- `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section`: Stacks 04KU helper
491	  (sorry-bodied iter-193, planner-sanctioned).

493	Once `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` lands axiom-clean
494	(via the Stacks 037Q substrate or descent-of-clopen-partitions substrate),
495	this instance becomes axiom-clean automatically. Downstream consumers can

498	to derive `ConnectedSpace (pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom g)` for any
499	`g : Y ⟶ Spec (.of k)` from a `ConnectedSpace Y`. -/
500	private instance identityComponent_geometricallyConnected
501	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
502	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
503	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
504	    GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom :=
505	  geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section
506	    (IdentityComponent G).hom (identityComponentSection G)
507	    (identityComponentSection_isSection G)

509	/-- **The identity component inclusion is a group-scheme homomorphism.**
510	
511	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (b): the clopen subscheme `G^0`
512	inherits a `k`-group-scheme structure from `G`, and the inclusion morphism
513	`G^0 ↪ G` (from `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`) is compatible
514	with the group laws on source and target. The statement-level pin asserts
515	the existence of the inherited `GrpObj` structure; the compatibility of
516	the inclusion with the group operations is the substantive content of
517	Kleiman's argument (the product `G^0 ×_k G^0` is connected by
518	EGA IV₂ 4.5.8; the group-multiplication map sends this connected subset
519	containing the identity into the connected component `G^0`).
520	
521	The full statement (existence of `GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)` *together*
522	with the homomorphism-compatibility of the inclusion in the relevant
523	`Hom`-form) lives in iter-193+. -/
524	theorem IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism {k : Type u} [Field k]
525	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
526	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
527	    Nonempty (GrpObj (IdentityComponent G)) :=
528	  sorry

530	/-- **The identity component is of finite type and geometrically irreducible.**
531	
532	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (c): the open subgroup `G^0` of
533	a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite type is itself
534	locally-of-finite-type-plus-quasi-compact (i.e., of finite type) over `k`,
535	and is geometrically irreducible. The proof reduces (after base change to
536	`\bar k`) to picking a nonempty smooth affine open subset `U ⊆ G^0`; the
537	translates `hg⁻¹U` give an open cover of `G^0` by smooth, hence irreducible,
538	neighbourhoods, so `G^0` is locally irreducible at every closed point; with
539	connectedness this gives irreducibility globally (EGA I 6.1.10). The image
540	`α(U × U) = G^0` is the image of the affine, hence quasi-compact, scheme
541	`U × U`, so `G^0` is quasi-compact.
542	
543	iter-189 partial progress: the `LocallyOfFiniteType` conjunct closes
544	axiom-clean via composition of an open immersion with `G.hom`. The
545	remaining two conjuncts (`QuasiCompact`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`)
546	require the group-structure argument and are bundled into the residual
547	sorry. -/
548	theorem IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible
549	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
550	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
551	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
552	    LocallyOfFiniteType (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
553	      QuasiCompact (IdentityComponent G).hom ∧
554	      GeometricallyIrreducible (IdentityComponent G).hom := by
555	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
556	  · -- `LocallyOfFiniteType`: `(IdentityComponent G).hom` unfolds to
557	    -- `(identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom`. Open immersion ∘ LFT
558	    -- is LFT (`locallyOfFiniteType_of_isOpenImmersion` +
559	    -- `locallyOfFiniteType_comp`).
560	    change LocallyOfFiniteType ((identityComponentCarrier G).ι ≫ G.hom)
561	    infer_instance
562	  · -- `QuasiCompact ∧ GeometricallyIrreducible`: Kleiman's group-theoretic
563	    -- argument. `α(U × U) = G^0` quasi-compact via the affine `U × U`;
564	    -- geometric irreducibility via base change to `\bar k`, picking a
565	    -- smooth affine `U ⊆ G^0`, and translating `hg⁻¹U` to cover. Cannot
566	    -- close axiom-clean without the substrate `isSubgroupHomomorphism`
567	    -- + EGA IV₂ 4.5.8 / 4.6.1 / EGA I 6.1.10 (not yet in Mathlib).
568	    sorry
