570	/-- **Formation of the identity component commutes with base change.**
571	
572	Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3) conclusion (d): for any field extension
573	`K/k`, the natural comparison map `(G^0)_K → (G_K)^0` is an isomorphism of
574	`K`-schemes. The proof: `G^0` is geometrically connected as a connected
575	scheme with a `k`-rational point (EGA IV₂ 4.5.14), so its base change
576	`(G^0)_K` remains connected; it is also open and closed in `G_K`, so
577	coincides with the identity component `(G_K)^0`.
578	
579	The statement-level pin asserts existence of: a `K`-group-scheme structure
580	on the base change `G ×_{Spec k} Spec K` (with appropriate
581	locally-of-finite-type instance), and an isomorphism on underlying schemes
582	identifying the two iterated constructions.
583	
584	iter-192 partial closure (Lane A.3.i, axiom-clean modulo the iso slot):
585	the `_grpInst` slot closes via `CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd`
586	(per `analogies/lane-a3i-isconnected-prod.md` second analogue — the
587	category-theoretic base-change of `GrpObj` directly fitting the
588	`Over.mk (pullback.snd ...)` shape of `G_K`); the `_locFTInst` slot
589	closes via Mathlib's stability of `LocallyOfFiniteType` under base
590	change (`inferInstance` fires on the pullback). The remaining iso slot
591	`(IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅ pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ`
592	is the substantive content of Stacks 04KS / EGA IV₂ 4.5.16: identifying
593	the iterated identity-component construction with the base change of
594	the identity component, which requires the
595	`geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section` helper above plus
596	descent of clopen partitions along base change (not yet in Mathlib). -/
597	theorem IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
598	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
599	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra k K] :
600	    let φ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) :=
601	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k K))
602	    let G_K : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of K)) :=
603	      Over.mk (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ)
604	    Nonempty (Σ' (_grpInst : GrpObj G_K)
605	                 (_locFTInst : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom),
606	      (IdentityComponent G_K).left ≅
607	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ) := by
608	  intro φ G_K
609	  -- `GrpObj G_K` via the category-theoretic base-change lemma
610	  -- `CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd`. The instance bridge
611	  -- `[GrpObj G] ⟹ [GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom)]` is by defeq (`G = Over.mk G.hom`
612	  -- when `G` is an over-category object whose right component is the unique
613	  -- inhabitant of `Discrete PUnit`).
614	  haveI hG : GrpObj (Over.mk G.hom) := ‹GrpObj G›
615	  haveI hGK_grp : GrpObj G_K := CategoryTheory.Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd
616	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom`: `G_K.hom = pullback.snd G.hom φ` is the
617	  -- base change of `G.hom` along `φ`; `LocallyOfFiniteType` is stable
618	  -- under base change (Mathlib instance).
619	  haveI hGK_lft : LocallyOfFiniteType G_K.hom :=
620	    (inferInstance : LocallyOfFiniteType (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.snd G.hom φ))
621	  -- The iso slot: substrate is now in place (iter-193 push-beyond):
622	  -- `identityComponent_geometricallyConnected` provides
623	  -- `GeometricallyConnected (IdentityComponent G).hom`, which combined with
624	  -- Mathlib's `[GeometricallyConnected f] [UniversallyOpen f]
625	  -- [ConnectedSpace Y] ⟹ ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` instance gives
626	  -- `ConnectedSpace (pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ)` (the connected-
627	  -- pullback substrate). The iso construction proceeds by:
628	  -- 1. Build an open immersion `e : pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ ⟶
629	  --    G_K.left` via `pullback.map _ _ _ _ (identityComponentCarrier G).ι
630	  --    (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) ...`, open-immersion status from
631	  --    `Scheme.pullback_map_isOpenImmersion` (i₁ = ι OI; i₂ = 𝟙 OI;
632	  --    i₃ = 𝟙 mono).
633	  -- 2. Show `Set.range e.base = identityComponentCarrier G_K (as Set)`
634	  --    via clopen-+-connected → connected-component bidirectional argument.
635	  -- 3. Apply `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` to get the iso between
636	  --    `pullback (IdentityComponent G).hom φ` and
637	  --    `(identityComponentCarrier G_K).toScheme = (IdentityComponent G_K).left`.
638	  -- Substantive content of step 2 (`~80-100 LOC`) deferred to iter-194+.
639	  refine ⟨⟨hGK_grp, hGK_lft, ?_⟩⟩
640	  sorry

644	/-! ## §2. The identity component of the Picard scheme
645	
646	We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
647	`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
648	integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).
649	
650	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.
651	`prp:pic0`). -/
652	
653	namespace Scheme
654	
655	/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.
656	
657	Defined as the identity component
658	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
659	`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
660	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
661	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
662	open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
663	geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
664	base field.
665	
666	iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
667	once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
668	`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
669	locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
670	`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
671	noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
672	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
673	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
674	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
675	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
676	  sorry
677	
678	/-! ## §3. The degree map
679	
680	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
681	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
682	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
683	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
684	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
685	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
686	
687	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
688	
689	namespace PicScheme
690	
691	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
692	
693	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
694	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
695	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
696	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
697	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
698	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
699	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
700	relative Picard functor).
701	
702	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
703	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
704	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
705	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
706	
707	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
708	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
709	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
710	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
711	`sorry`. -/
712	noncomputable def degree {k : Type u} [Field k]
713	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
714	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
715	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
716	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
717	  sorry
718	
719	end PicScheme
720	
721	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
722	
723	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
724	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
725	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
726	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
727	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
728	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
729	
730	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
731	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
732	
733	namespace Pic0Scheme
734	
735	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
736	
737	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
738	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
739	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
740	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
741	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
742	
743	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
744	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
745	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
746	
747	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
748	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
749	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
750	follow-up.
751	
752	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
753	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
754	  `PicScheme C`),
755	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
756	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
757	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
758	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
759	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
760	  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
761	  identity component).
762	For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
763	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
764	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
765	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
766	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
767	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
768	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
769	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
770	  sorry
771	
772	/-- **Dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals the genus of `C`.**
773	
774	Milne~§III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e): "The dimension of `J` is the genus of `C`".
775	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
776	`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
777	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
778	`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where
779	`Pic_{C/k}` is smooth, and for smooth proper curves
780	(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
781	(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`
782	by `def:genus`. -/
783	theorem finrank_eq_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
784	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
785	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
786	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
787	    topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
788	  sorry
789	
790	/-- **`k`-points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` are the kernel of the degree map.**
791	
792	Milne~§III.1, p.~88: `Pic⁰(C)` is the group of isomorphism classes of
793	invertible sheaves of degree zero on `C`. For a smooth proper geometrically
794	integral curve `C/k`, a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` lies in the image of
795	the inclusion `Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}` (the inclusion of
796	`def:pic_zero_subscheme`, packaged here via the existence of the
797	inclusion morphism) if and only if `degree C λ = 0`.
798	
799	The statement-level pin packages two pieces: existence of the inclusion
800	morphism `Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶ Pic_{C/k}` (extracted from
801	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` once `PicScheme C` has the
802	`GrpObj` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` instances), together with the
803	characterisation of `k`-points factoring through it as those with degree
804	zero. -/
805	theorem kPoints_iff_kerDegree {k : Type u} [Field k]
806	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
807	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
808	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
809	    Nonempty (Σ' (inc : (Pic0Scheme C).left ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
810	      ∀ (lambda : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
811	        (∃ mu : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left, mu ≫ inc = lambda) ↔
812	          PicScheme.degree C lambda = 0) :=
813	  sorry
814	
815	end Pic0Scheme
816	
817	end Scheme
818	
819	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (A.4.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.4.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula and the corollary "regular local ring
14	⟹ Cohen–Macaulay" that the sibling sub-build **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
15	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective surface)
16	consumes.
17	
18	Per STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
19	startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the Albanese
20	geometry and lives entirely under `RingTheory.*`. Mathlib at the project's
21	pinned commit (`b80f227`) exposes `IsRegularLocalRing` and the categorical
22	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on an abelian category (specialised to
23	`ModuleCat R`), as well as the regular-sequence definition
24	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`. The depth function `Module.depth`, the
25	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula, and the Cohen–Macaulay predicate are *not* in
26	Mathlib at the pinned commit — they are the new content scaffolded here.
27	
28	## Status (iter-178 update — projectiveDimension closed)
29	
30	This file was originally landed iter-175 (Lane F file-skeleton) with each
31	blueprint-pinned declaration carrying the *intended* substantive type
32	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
33	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex`). The bodies were
34	scheduled for iter-176+ work; the chapter is independent of the rest of
35	Route A's geometric infrastructure, making it a good parallel-work
36	candidate.
37	
38	iter-178 closed `Module.projectiveDimension` kernel-clean as a one-liner
39	re-export of the categorical `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension`. The
40	remaining five typed `sorry` bodies (`depth`, `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`,
41	`depth_of_short_exact`, `auslander_buchsbaum_formula`,
42	`CohenMacaulay.of_regular`) are substantive multi-iter content and stay

45	The 7 pinned declarations are:
46	
47	1. `RingTheory.Module.depth` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the `I`-depth of a
48	   finite `R`-module as the supremum of lengths of `M`-regular sequences in
49	   `I`, valued in `ℕ∞`.
50	2. `RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — for
51	   `(R, 𝔪)` Noetherian local and `M ≠ 0` finite, `depth(M)` equals the
52	   smallest `i` with `Ext^i_R(κ, M) ≠ 0`. Encoded via the depth-bound iff
53	   `Ext` vanishes below.
54	3. `Module.projectiveDimension` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) — an
55	   `R`-module-side wrapper for the categorical
56	   `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)`. The categorical
57	   version exists in Mathlib `b80f227`; this is the re-export pinned by the
58	   blueprint.
59	4. `RingTheory.depth_of_short_exact` (theorem, ~12 LOC) — the
60	   depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequalities (Stacks 00LE).
61	5. `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the formula
62	   `pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)` for a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite
63	   projective dimension over a Noetherian local ring.
64	6. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay` (class, ~3 LOC) — `IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing R`
65	   encoded as `depth(R) = krullDim R`.
66	7. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (theorem, ~6 LOC) — a regular local
67	   ring is Cohen–Macaulay (the consumer-facing input for A.4.a).
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `depth` returns `ℕ∞` and is defined by the regular-sequence supremum.
75	- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` is encoded as the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`
76	  vanishing characterisation (the smallest non-vanishing `Ext` index = depth).
77	- `projectiveDimension` re-exports the categorical
78	  `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on `ModuleCat.of R M`.
79	- `depth_of_short_exact` packages the three Stacks 00LE inequalities into a
80	  conjunction.
81	- `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` is the numeric equation
82	  `pd + depth = depth(R)`.
83	- `CohenMacaulay` is the equation `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`.
84	- `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` is an `IsRegularLocalRing → IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing`
85	  implication, the consumer-facing statement.
86	
87	Unfolding any declaration exposes the named substantive content (a regular-
88	sequence supremum, an `Ext`-vanishing characterisation, the categorical
89	projective dimension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` /
90	empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.
91	
92	## References
93	
94	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` (~560 LOC,
95	7 pins). Stacks Project tags 00LF (definition-depth), 00LP (lemma-depth-ext),
96	00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses), 090V (proposition-Auslander–Buchsbaum), 00N4
97	(definition-local-ring-CM), 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM). Matsumura,
98	*Commutative Ring Theory*, Theorem 19.1. Auslander–Buchsbaum, "Homological
99	dimension in local rings", 1957.
100	-/

112	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
113	
114	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
115	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
116	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
117	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
118	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
119	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
120	
121	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
122	
123	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
124	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
125	
126	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
127	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
128	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
129	
130	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
131	```
132	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
133	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
134	```
135	folded with the `IM = M` clause. The signature is non-tautological: it
136	asserts a function `(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks
137	00LF definition.
138	
139	**iter-179 Mathlib-gap check (Lane F Target 2 STRETCH)**: a pinned-commit
140	audit of `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Depth` (the only file under
141	`Mathlib/RingTheory/*` containing the word "depth") confirms that Mathlib
142	`b80f227` ships only depth-zero lemmas (`IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff`)
143	and *not* the numeric depth function itself. The one-liner re-export route
144	is therefore not available; the body stays a typed `sorry` until an
145	iter-180+ body lane fills the supremum-with-`IM=M` clause directly. -/
146	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
147	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
148	  open Classical in
149	  if _I • (⊤ : Submodule R _M) = ⊤ then (⊤ : ℕ∞)
150	  else sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
151	    (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ _I) ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular _M rs }
152	
153	end Module
154	
155	end RingTheory

157	/-! ## §2. Projective dimension
158	
159	Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
160	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C → WithBot ℕ∞` on an abelian category
161	with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
162	For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
163	`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
164	can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.
165	
166	Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/
167	
168	namespace Module
169	
170	/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
171	categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.
172	
173	This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
174	the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
175	infimum (in `WithBot ℕ∞`) of `n : ℕ` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
176	`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.
177	
178	Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
179	`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
180	wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
181	`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.
182	
183	The body is the one-line re-export
184	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`; iter-178 closed
185	this declaration kernel-clean. -/
186	noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
187	    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ℕ∞ :=
188	  CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)
189	
190	end Module
191	
192	namespace RingTheory
193	
194	namespace Module
195	
196	/-! ## §3. Depth via Ext characterisation
197	
198	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a
199	nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the depth of `M` equals the smallest index `i`
200	at which `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero (Stacks tag 00LP).
201	
202	This is the lemma A.4.a's downstream consumer ultimately reads off:
203	combined with the regular-sequence definition (`depth`), the Ext
204	characterisation provides both the *lower bound* (regular sequences exhibit
205	`Ext^i = 0` for `i < length(rs)`) and the *upper bound* (failure of any
206	extension lifts a non-zero element in `Ext^{depth}(κ, M)`).
207	
208	The signature pins the equivalence via the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`-vanishing-
209	below: `n ≤ depth(M) ↔ Ext^i(κ, M) = 0 for all i < n`. This is logically
210	equivalent to "depth(M) = smallest i with Ext^i ≠ 0" and is the form most
211	convenient for inductive proofs (Stacks 00LP proof: pick `x ∈ 𝔪`
212	non-zero-divisor, use long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`).
213	
214	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_via_ext` (Stacks tag 00LP). -/
215	
216	/-! ### Helper C (iter-183 Lane G, axiom-clean): `Ann`-killing of Ext via R-linearity
217	
218	For any `R`-modules `N, M` and any `x : R` in the annihilator of `N`, the
219	R-action `x • e` on `e : Ext^i_R(N, M)` is zero.
220	
221	Proof sketch: `x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i)` (by R-linearity:
222	`mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp`). For `x ∈ Ann(N)` the morphism
223	`x • 𝟙_N : N ⟶ N` is the zero map, so `mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N) = mk₀ 0 = 0`
224	(`mk₀_zero`), and `0.comp e = 0` (`zero_comp`).
225	
226	iter-183 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. This is the precise statement of the
227	Stacks-00LP "`x ∈ 𝔪` annihilates `Ext^*(κ, -)`" trick, lifted to the more
228	general `x ∈ Ann(N)` form so it covers both `N = κ` and `N = R/(x_1,…,x_k)`. -/
229	private lemma ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator
230	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
231	    {N M : ModuleCat.{u} R} {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N M i)
232	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ Module.annihilator R (N : Type u)) :
233	    x • e = 0 := by
234	  -- Step 1: x • 𝟙_N = 0 in ModuleCat (the underlying linear map sends m ↦ x • m,
235	  -- which is 0 since x ∈ Ann(N)).
236	  have hkill : (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N)) = (0 : N ⟶ N) := by
237	    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
238	    apply LinearMap.ext
239	    intro n
240	    change x • n = 0
241	    exact Module.mem_annihilator.mp hx n
242	  -- Step 2: x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i) by R-linearity.
243	  have hreflect :
244	      (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))).comp e (zero_add i)
245	        = x • e := by
246	    have hmk : (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))
247	                : Abelian.Ext.{u} N N 0)
248	        = x • CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (𝟙 N) :=
249	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) x (𝟙 N)
250	    rw [hmk, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_comp,
251	        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
252	  -- Step 3: substitute hkill to collapse mk₀ … to mk₀ 0 = 0, then zero_comp.
253	  rw [← hreflect, hkill, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_zero,
254	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
255	
256	/-- **Depth via Ext characterisation.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
257	with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the
258	depth-bound `n ≤ depth(M)` is equivalent to the vanishing of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)`
259	for all `i < n`. Equivalently, `depth(M)` is the smallest `i` at which
260	`Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero.
261	
262	iter-176+: the body proceeds by induction on `n` via the long exact sequence
263	of `Ext^*(κ, -)` applied to `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0` for a non-zero-divisor
264	`x ∈ 𝔪`. The base case `n = 0` is `Hom(κ, M) ≠ 0 ↔ depth(M) = 0`, which is
265	the standard "the maximal ideal contains a zero-divisor on `M` iff
266	`𝔪 ∈ Ass(M)`" characterisation (Stacks 00LC).
267	
268	iter-183 Lane G structural progress:
269	* Generalised the inductive hypothesis over `M` (so `ih` is universally
270	  quantified in `M`, allowing recursive use on `M/xM`).
271	* Base case `n = 0` closed kernel-clean (both directions trivial).
272	* New axiom-clean helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (lines
273	  229–254) discharges substantive piece (a) of the Stacks 00LP proof:
274	  *"`x ∈ Ann N` annihilates `Ext^*(N, ·)`"*, via
275	  `mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp + mk₀_zero + zero_comp`.
276	* The inductive step's **backward direction** has the regular-element
277	  extraction stage closed structurally: from the `Ext^0(κ, M) = 0`
278	  hypothesis we derive `Subsingleton (κ →ₗ[R] M)` (via `mk₀_eq_zero_iff +
279	  ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff`) and then invoke
280	  `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` + `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`
281	  to obtain `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular M x`, with
282	  `nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal` providing the `Nontrivial
283	  (M/xM)` instance for the recursive IH application.
284	
285	Residual `sorry`s (2 named inline branches):
286	1. **Forward direction**  `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M → ∀ i ≤ n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0` —
287	   needs: extract regular sequence of length n+1 from supremum (handle `⊤`
288	   via Nakayama); cons-decompose via `isRegular_cons_iff`; LES of Ext on
289	   `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`; recurse via `ih` on M/xM.
290	2. **Backward direction**, final assembly — after the regular-element
291	   extraction (already done), needs: LES chase to derive Ext-vanishing on
292	   M/xM at indices `j < n` (using the new helper to kill the
293	   multiplication-by-x maps); apply `ih (M := M/xM)` to obtain regular
294	   sequence `rs'`; cons `x :: rs'`; conclude via `le_sSup`. -/
295	theorem depth_eq_smallest_ext_index
296	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
297	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
298	    [Nontrivial M] (n : ℕ) :
299	    (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M ↔
300	      ∀ i : ℕ, i < n →
301	        ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
302	            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
303	            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 := by
304	  -- We generalize `M` so the inductive hypothesis is universally quantified
305	  -- over the module — this lets the induction step recursively apply the IH
306	  -- to the quotient `M / xM` (a *different* module of the same shape).
307	  induction n generalizing M with
308	  | zero =>
309	    -- LHS: `(0 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` is `bot_le`.
310	    -- RHS: `∀ i < 0, …` is vacuous since no `i` satisfies `i < 0`.
311	    exact ⟨fun _ i hi _ => absurd hi (Nat.not_lt_zero i), fun _ => bot_le⟩
312	  | succ n ih =>
313	    -- The Stacks 00LP inductive step. The blueprint sketch is:
314	    --
315	    -- (⇒) Assume `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M`. Then `Nontrivial M` rules out
316	    --     `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤` (Nakayama), so `depth M` is the supremum and we can
317	    --     extract an `M`-regular sequence `rs = x :: rs'` of length `n+1` in
318	    --     `𝔪`. The cons-decomposition `RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff`
319	    --     gives `IsSMulRegular M x` and `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x M) rs'`.
320	    --     For `i = 0`: `Hom(κ, M) ↪ Hom(κ, M)` via `[x]` is `[x]` on the
321	    --     domain `Hom(κ, M)`, but `x ∈ 𝔪 = Ann(κ)` kills this on the κ side,
322	    --     so `Hom(κ, M) = 0`. Pass to `Ext^0` via `addEquiv₀`.
323	    --     For `1 ≤ i ≤ n`: the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0` (built via
324	    --     `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`) plus the fact that
325	    --     `[x]_*` is zero on `Ext^i(κ, M)` (because `x ∈ Ann κ` ⇒
326	    --     `x • 𝟙_κ = 0`, hence by `precomp_smul = smul_precomp` the
327	    --     R-action on `Ext^i(κ, M)` is annihilated by `x`) lets the
328	    --     LES connecting map `Ext^{i-1}(κ, M/xM) ↠ Ext^i(κ, M)` be
329	    --     surjective.  By IH applied to `M/xM` (we get `n ≤ depth (M/xM)`,
330	    --     so `Ext^j(κ, M/xM) = 0` for `j < n`) we conclude
331	    --     `Ext^i(κ, M) = 0` for `1 ≤ i ≤ n`.
332	    --
333	    -- (⇐) Assume `∀ i < n+1, ∀ e ∈ Ext^i(κ, M), e = 0`.
334	    --     Specialise at `i = 0` and use `Ext.addEquiv₀` to extract
335	    --     `Subsingleton (κ →ₗ[R] M)`.  Apply
336	    --     `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` (Mathlib) with
337	    --     `N := ResidueField R` and `Module.annihilator R (ResidueField R) =
338	    --     maximalIdeal R` to obtain `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular M x`.
339	    --     The SES + same "x annihilates Ext^*(κ, ·)" fact give
340	    --     `Ext^j(κ, M/xM) = 0` for `j < n` (via the LES at indices `j ≤ n-1`).
341	    --     `M/xM := QuotSMulTop x M` is `Nontrivial` by
342	    --     `nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal` and `Module.Finite`
343	    --     as a quotient.  Apply `ih` on `M/xM` at index `n` to get a
344	    --     regular sequence `rs'` of length `n` in `𝔪` on `M/xM`.  Then
345	    --     `x :: rs'` is a regular sequence of length `n+1` in `𝔪` on `M`
346	    --     by `RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff`. This gives
347	    --     `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` via `le_sSup` on the depth supremum.
348	    --
349	    -- iter-183: the helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` above closes
350	    -- the substantive piece (a) "`x ∈ Ann N` annihilates `Ext^i(N, M)`"; this
351	    -- is the algebraic fact under both directions of the iff. The remaining
352	    -- pieces are LES-of-Ext bookkeeping + supremum-extraction.
353	    --
354	    -- The remaining substantive Mathlib gaps for closing this step are:
355	    --   (b) Extracting `Module.annihilator R (ResidueField R) = maximalIdeal R`
356	    --       (a one-liner via `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`).
357	    --   (c) The "sSup gives a witness of length ≥ n+1" reasoning, which
358	    --       in `ℕ∞` requires handling the `⊤` case (Nakayama rules it out
359	    --       under `Nontrivial M`).
360	    --   (d) Lifting `IsSMulRegular`-cons via `isRegular_cons_iff`.
361	    --
362	    -- We open the backward direction's regular-element-extraction step
363	    -- below to expose the API path for the next iter; the forward
364	    -- direction's regular-sequence-extraction + LES chase is the larger
365	    -- body remaining as `sorry`.
366	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
367	    · -- (⇒) Forward direction: `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M → ∀ i ≤ n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0`.
368	      -- iter-184 Lane G: closed via Nakayama-driven `depth = sSup`
369	      -- extraction + cons-decomposition + LES chase using
370	      -- `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` + `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
371	      intro _hdepth i _hi _e
372	      -- Step 1: unfold `depth M = sSup S_M` (Nakayama rules out `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤`).
373	      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
374	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
375	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
376	      have hdepth_M_unfold :
377	          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
378	            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
379	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
380	              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
381	        rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
382	      -- Step 2: extract a regular sequence rs of length > n in 𝔪 on M.
383	      have hlt : (n : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
384	        calc (n : ℕ∞)
385	            < ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.lt_succ_self n
386	          _ ≤ _ := _hdepth
387	      rw [hdepth_M_unfold] at hlt
388	      obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
389	      have hrs_len_gt : n < rs.length := by
390	        rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
391	        exact_mod_cast hk_lt
392	      -- Step 3: rs is non-empty (length > n ≥ 0); decompose rs = x :: rs_tail.
393	      rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
394	      · -- impossible: empty list has length 0, not > n.
395	        simp at hrs_len_gt
396	      have hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self
397	      have htail_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_tail, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
398	        hrs_mem r (List.mem_cons_of_mem _ hr)
399	      have hcons := (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg
400	      have hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x := hcons.1

10	/-!
11	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
14	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
15	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
16	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
17	
18	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
19	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
20	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
21	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
22	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
23	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
24	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
25	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
26	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
27	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
28	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
29	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
30	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
31	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
32	  outside this iter's scope.
33	
34	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
35	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
36	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
37	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
38

69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
75	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
76	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
77	  not `True`.
78	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
79	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
80	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
81	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
82	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
83	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
84	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
85	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
86	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
87	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
88	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
89	
90	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
91	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
92	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
93	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
94	
95	## Variety conventions
96	
97	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
98	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
99	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
100	
101	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
102	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
104	
105	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
106	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
107

112	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.

127	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
128	
129	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
130	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
131	open subset of `X`).
132	
133	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
134	
135	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
136	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
137	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
138	
139	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
140	
141	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
142	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
143	
144	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
145	§I.3, p. 16). -/
146	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
147	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
148	
149	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
150	of an open). -/
151	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
152	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
153	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
154	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
155	
156	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
157	
158	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
159	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
160	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
161	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
162	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
163	`f.domain`.
164	
165	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
166	conclusion). -/
167	
168	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
169	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
170	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
171	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
172	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
173	
174	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
175	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
176	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
177	
178	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
179	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
180	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
181	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
182	
183	end RationalMap
184	
185	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
186	
187	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
188	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
189	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
190	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
191	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
192	
193	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
194	
195	/-! ### Stacks 00TT scaffolding (Lane M↓ iter-191 first scaffold)
196	
197	The Mathlib gap "smooth over `\bar k` ⟹ stalk is a regular local ring"
198	(Stacks tag `00TT`) is structured here as a four-stage pipeline:
199	
200	* **Stage 1** — smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk (Mathlib instance
201	  `AlgebraicGeometry.instFlatOfSmooth` + `Flat.stalkMap`).
202	* **Stage 2** — smooth at a point ⟹ exists a standard smooth presentation
203	  on an affine neighbourhood (Stacks tag `00T7`, packaged in Mathlib at
204	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`).
205	* **Stage 3** — the polynomial generators of the standard smooth
206	  presentation form a regular sequence after localising at any prime
207	  (Stacks `02JK`/`07BV`; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
208	* **Stage 4** — a Noetherian local ring whose maximal ideal is generated
209	  by a regular sequence of length equal to its Krull dimension is a
210	  regular local ring (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_regularSequence`; Stacks
211	  `00OE`/Matsumura 19.2; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
212	
213	Stages 1-2 are landed axiom-clean as named helpers below. Stages 3-4
214	remain a single scoped `sorry` inside the main theorem
215	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` until either the cotangent /
216	sheaf-of-relative-differentials route (Mathlib upstream) lands or the
217	project-side `02JK`/`00OE` chain is built (iter-192+). -/
218	
219	/-- **Stage 1 (Stacks 00TT, smooth ⟹ flat at stalk).** For a smooth
220	morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)` with `k̄` algebraically closed,
221	the induced ring map on stalks at every point `z : X.left` is flat. This
222	is the smooth ⟹ flat instance applied through the stalk-map flatness
223	characterisation `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`.
224	
225	Axiom-clean: composes the smooth ⟹ flat instance with the stalk-map
226	flatness lemma. No Mathlib gap. -/
227	private theorem stalkMap_flat_of_smooth
228	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
229	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
230	    [Smooth X.hom]
231	    (z : X.left) :
232	    ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
233	  AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.stalkMap X.hom z
234	
235	/-- **Stage 2 (Stacks 00T7, smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation at a
236	point).** For a smooth morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)`, every
237	point `z : X.left` admits affine open neighbourhoods `U ⊆ Spec (.of k̄)`,
238	`V ⊆ X.left` with `z ∈ V` and `V ⊆ X.hom ⁻¹' U` such that the induced
239	ring map `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom` is `IsStandardSmooth`.
240	
241	Axiom-clean: re-exports `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`
242	(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's
243	`Over (Spec (.of k̄))` calling convention. -/
244	private theorem exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth
245	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
246	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
247	    [Smooth X.hom]
248	    (z : X.left) :
249	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
250	      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
251	      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),
252	      (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
253	  AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z
254	
255	/-- **Stage 3 (Stacks 00TT, scheme-to-algebra bridge for the standard smooth
256	presentation).** Iter-192 axiom-clean intermediate helper: combines Stage 2's
257	existence-of-standard-smooth-presentation output with the `RingHom`-to-`Algebra`
258	bridge `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra`, plus the affine-open stalk
259	localisation `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`. Returns the full algebra-side
260	package needed by Stages 4-5 of the regularity proof: an affine neighbourhood
261	`V ∋ z`, an `Algebra Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)` instance under which
262	`Γ(X.left, V)` is `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth` over `Γ(Spec _, U)`, plus the
263	`IsLocalization.AtPrime` witness identifying the stalk at `z` with the
264	localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)` at the prime ideal of `z`.
265	
266	Axiom-clean: composition of `exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth` +
267	`RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`.
268	
269	This is the iter-192 Lane M↓ HARD BAR new axiom-clean helper: it packages the
270	"smooth ⟹ stalk is localisation of a standard-smooth Γ(Spec, U)-algebra"
271	content as a standalone declaration that downstream Stages 4-5 (the genuine
272	Stacks 02JK + 00OE Mathlib-gap chain) can consume directly. -/
273	private theorem exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth
274	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
275	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
276	    [Smooth X.hom]
277	    (z : X.left) :
278	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_hU : IsAffineOpen U)
279	      (V : X.left.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
280	      (hzV : z ∈ V) (_e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
281	      (alg : Algebra Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V)),
282	      @Algebra.IsStandardSmooth _ _ _ _ alg ∧
283	      letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
284	      IsLocalization.AtPrime
285	        (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := by
286	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, hStdSmooth⟩ :=
287	    exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z
288	  refine ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.toAlgebra,
289	    hStdSmooth.toAlgebra, ?_⟩
290	  -- Localisation witness: the affine-open stalk is `Aₚ` for `p = primeIdealOf z`.
291	  letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
292	  exact hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩
293	
294	/-- **Stage 4 (Stacks 00T7(2), Kähler-differentials freeness).** Iter-192
295	axiom-clean intermediate helper: given an algebra-level
296	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance (typically arising from
297	`Stage 3 = exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`),
298	the module of Kähler differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` is `Module.Free` over `S`. The
299	basis is the `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` family witnessed by
300	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`.
301	
302	Axiom-clean: re-export of `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`
303	+ `Module.Free.of_basis`. -/
304	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
305	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
306	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
307	    Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := by
308	  obtain ⟨_, _, b, _⟩ :=
309	    (Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential
310	      (R := R) (S := S)).mp ‹_›
311	  exact Module.Free.of_basis b
312	
313	/-- **Stage 5a (Stacks 02JK, freeness transports through localisation, algebra-side).**
314	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` whose Kähler
315	differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` are `S`-free (e.g.\ when `S` is `R`-standard smooth via
316	`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth`), the localisation `Sₘ` at any
317	submonoid `M ⊆ S` has Kähler differentials `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` which are `Sₘ`-free.
318	
319	Axiom-clean: composes `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (Mathlib re-export)
320	with `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`. This is the substantive localisation-of-freeness
321	step that Stage 5 of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline uses; extracted as a named helper
322	here so the main proof body of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` reads as a clean
323	chain of named lemmas rather than an inline `IsLocalizedModule` + `Module.free_of_*`
324	maneuver. -/
325	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization
326	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
327	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
328	    [Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R])]
329	    (M : Submonoid S)
330	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
331	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
332	    Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := by
333	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
334	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
335	  exact Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sₘ) (S := M)
336	    (M := Ω[S⁄R]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sₘ⁄R])
337	    (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
338	
339	/-- **Stage 5b (Stacks 02JK + 00T7, rank computation through localisation, algebra-side).**
340	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` that is
341	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n` over `R` and a localisation `Sₘ` at any
342	submonoid `M` (with `Sₘ` non-trivial), the `Sₘ`-rank of `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` equals `n`. This
343	upgrades Stage 5a's freeness conclusion with a *specific* rank.
344	
345	Axiom-clean: composes `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
346	(Mathlib, gives `rank S Ω[S⁄R] = n`) with `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`
347	(Mathlib, transports module rank through `IsLocalizedModule`). The combination
348	provides the rank of the stalk-side Kähler differentials, which is one of the two
349	ingredients of the regularity conclusion at the stalk (the other ingredient is the
350	Stacks 00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula, still a Mathlib gap). -/
351	private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension
352	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
353	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
354	    (n : ℕ) [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
355	    (M : Submonoid S)
356	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
357	    [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
358	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
359	    Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n := by
360	  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S := hS.isStandardSmooth
361	  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
362	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
363	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
364	  have hrank :
365	      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) =
366	      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank S (Ω[S⁄R])) :=
367	    Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Sₘ M
368	      (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
369	  rw [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential
370	        (S := S) n] at hrank
371	  simpa using hrank
372	
373	/-- **Stacks `00TT`, Jacobian-criterion direction (regularity conclusion).**
374	For a smooth integral variety `X` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, the
375	stalk of `X` at every point is a regular local ring.
376	
377	This is the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00TT` (Lemma 10.140.3
378	"Smooth algebras over fields"): given an affine chart `Spec A ⊆ X`, the
379	\(\bar k\)-algebra `A` is smooth at every prime `𝔮`, and the localisation
380	`A_𝔮 = X.presheaf.stalk z` (for `z ∈ X` lying over `𝔮`) is regular by the
381	"in this case the local ring `A_𝔮` is regular" clause of the cited lemma.
382

517	/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
518	over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
519	*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
520	of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the

526	smooth ⟹ regular (Stacks 00TT) and codim-1 ⟹ Krull-dim-1 — into an inline
527	`hreg_dim` conjunction whose first conjunct was a bare `sorry`. The Krull-dim

536	private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
537	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
538	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
539	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
540	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
541	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
542	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
543	    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
544	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
545	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
546	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
547	  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
548	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
549	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
550	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
551	  -- Regularity half: extracted to the named helper above
552	  -- (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`), which captures the Stacks-00TT
553	  -- Jacobian-criterion gap as a narrow named typed sorry. No further
554	  -- inline `sorry` is required at this step.
555	  have hreg : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
556	    isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth X z
557	  -- Krull-dim half: closes via the iter-183 axiom-clean CoheightBridge
558	  -- equality + the codim-1 witness `_hz`.
559	  have hdim : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := by
560	    rw [Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight]
561	    exact_mod_cast _hz
562	  -- From regularity + dim = 1, the cotangent space has `finrank = 1`
563	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
564	  have hfin :
565	      Module.finrank
566	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
567	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
568	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
569	    rw [hdim] at h
570	    exact_mod_cast h
571	  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
572	  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
573	  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
574	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
575	    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
576	  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
577	  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
578	  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.
579	  have hne : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
580	    intro hbot
581	    have hF : IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
582	      IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr hbot
583	    have h0 : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 0 :=
584	      ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField hF
585	    -- Contradiction: `1 = 0` in `WithBot ℕ∞`.
586	    rw [hdim] at h0
587	    exact_mod_cast h0
588	  exact ⟨hprin, hne⟩

590	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
591	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
592	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
593	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
594	
595	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
596	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
597	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
598	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
599	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
600	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
601	
602	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
603	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
604	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
605	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
606	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
607	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
608	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
609	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
610	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
611	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
612	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
613	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
614	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
615	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
616	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
617	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
618	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
619	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
620	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
621	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
622	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
623	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
624	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
625	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
626	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
627	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
628	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
629	    intro hF
630	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
631	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
632	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
633	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)

637	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
638	
639	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
640	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
641	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
642	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
643	
644	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
645	
646	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
647	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
648	
649	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
650	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
651	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
652	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
653	
654	The proof combines two steps:
655	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
656	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
657	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
658	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
659	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
660	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
661	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
662	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
663	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
664	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
665	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
666	
667	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
668	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
669	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
670	
671	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
672	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
673	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
674	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
675	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
676	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
677	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
678	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
679	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
680	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
681	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
682	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
683	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
684	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f :=
685	  sorry

687	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
688	
689	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
690	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
691	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
692	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
693	
694	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
695	
696	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
697	codim 1.**
698	
699	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
700	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
701	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
702	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
703	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
704	
705	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
706	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
707	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
708	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
709	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
710	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
711	
712	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
713	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
714	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
715	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
716	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
717	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
718	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
719	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
720	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
721	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
722	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
723	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
724	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
725	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
726	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
727	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) :=
728	  sorry

730	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
731	
732	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
733	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
734	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
735	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
736	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
737	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
738	witnesses are constructed in this project).
739	
740	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
741	
742	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
743	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
744	
745	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
746	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
747	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
748	
749	which requires the pullback machinery
750	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
751	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
752	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
753	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
754	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
755	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
756	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
757	
758	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
759	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
760	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
761	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
762	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
763	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
764	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
765	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
766	the pullback machinery lands.
767	
768	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
769	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
770	
771	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
772	
773	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
774	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
775	are equivalent:
776	
777	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
778	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
779	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
780	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
781	
782	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
783	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
784	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
785	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
786	construct partial-map representatives).
787	
788	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
789	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
790	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
791	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
792	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
793	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
794	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
795	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
796	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
797	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
798	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
799	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
800	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
801	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
802	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
803	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor) :
804	    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
805	      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
806	        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
807	  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
808	  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩
809	
810	end RationalMap
811	
812	end Scheme
813	
814	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.

4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 073 — Phase E closes by reduction)
15	
16	After the iter-073 refactor of `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J` (no genus-0 dite),
17	all three protected declarations reduce uniformly to the Albanese universal property
18	carried by `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`:
19	
20	* `ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
21	* `comp_ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
22	* `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`.
23	
24	The genus-0 rigidity content previously sitting at the existence step of
25	`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (classical rigidity `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) is now absorbed
26	into the single deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness` in
27	`Jacobian.lean`: the witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` field must verify the Albanese property
28	for genus-0 curves too, which is precisely the rigidity content.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
40	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
41	  [IsProper C.hom]
42	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
43	
44	namespace Jacobian
45	
46	variable {C} in
47	-- data
48	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
49	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. Projects the Albanese universal morphism carried
50	by the witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
51	noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
52	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
53	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
54	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
55	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
56	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve
57	
58	variable {C} in
59	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
60	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. Projects the pointed-property
61	of the Albanese witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
62	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
63	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
64	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
65	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
66	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
67	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
68	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve
69	
70	variable {C} in
71	/--
72	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
73	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
74	Jacobian of `C`.
75	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
76	
77	The existence-and-uniqueness data is projected unconditionally from the witness
78	`(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. The genus-0 rigidity content (`Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
79	Mumford §4) is absorbed into the deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
80	since the witness must verify the Albanese property for genus-0 curves too.
81	-/
82	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
83	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
84	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
85	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
86	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
87	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
88	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
89	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
90	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
91	
92	end Jacobian
93	
94	end AlgebraicGeometry

2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
