81	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
82	open scoped IntermediateField TensorProduct
83	
84	universe u
85	
86	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
87	
88	-- Mathlib's `Algebra.TensorProduct` ships only the LEFT algebra instance on
89	-- `B₁ ⊗[k] B₂`; the symmetric right-algebra is a `local instance` inside
90	-- `Mathlib.RingTheory.IsTensorProduct`. Re-enable it here so the canonical
91	-- `Algebra.IsPushout k B₁ B₂ (B₁ ⊗[k] B₂)` instance from Mathlib resolves.
92	attribute [local instance] Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
93	
94	namespace GrpObj
95	
96	/-- Chart-algebra (α): the affine pullback `Spec B₁ ×_(Spec k) Spec B₂ =
97	Spec (B₁ ⊗_k B₂)` carries the canonical `Algebra.IsPushout k B₁ B₂
98	(B₁ ⊗[k] B₂)` square at the ring level. Refined iter-146 to its real
99	signature (the algebra-level core of the blueprint's three-step
100	chart-scheme bridge); discharged by `inferInstance` after re-enabling
101	the local right-algebra instance. -/
102	theorem algebra_isPushout_of_affine_product
103	    (k B₁ B₂ : Type*) [CommRing k] [CommRing B₁] [CommRing B₂]
104	    [Algebra k B₁] [Algebra k B₂] :
105	    Algebra.IsPushout k B₁ B₂ (TensorProduct k B₁ B₂) :=
106	  inferInstance
107	
108	end GrpObj
109	
110	/-- (FT.3 base case) The universal Kähler derivation does not kill the
111	transcendental generator: `D (X) ≠ 0` in `Ω[Frac(k[X])⁄k]`. Verified route
112	(analogist iter-154): `Ω[Frac(k[X])⁄k]` is the localization of `Ω[k[X]⁄k]`
113	(`KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`, formally-étale localization),
114	so `D X = 0` would give `c • D_{k[X]} X = 0` for some non-zero-divisor `c`;
115	transporting along the `k[X]`-linear iso `polynomialEquiv` (with
116	`polynomialEquiv_D` ↦ `derivative X = 1`) yields `c • 1 = 0`, contradicting
117	`nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero`. -/
118	private lemma _ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero {k : Type*} [Field k] :
119	    _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k (FractionRing (Polynomial k))
120	      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) (FractionRing (Polynomial k)) Polynomial.X) ≠ 0 := by
121	  intro h
122	  have hmap : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map k k (Polynomial k)
123	      (FractionRing (Polynomial k))
124	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k (Polynomial k) Polynomial.X) = 0 := by
125	    rw [_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D]; exact h
126	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))
127	      (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map k k (Polynomial k)
128	        (FractionRing (Polynomial k))) :=
129	    _root_.KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map k (Polynomial k)
130	      (FractionRing (Polynomial k)) (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))
131	  rw [IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff (nonZeroDivisors (Polynomial k))] at hmap
132	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hmap
133	  rw [Submonoid.smul_def] at hc
134	  have hc1 := congrArg (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv (R := k)) hc
135	  rw [map_smul, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.polynomialEquiv_D, Polynomial.derivative_X,
136	    map_zero, smul_eq_mul, mul_one] at hc1
137	  exact nonZeroDivisors.coe_ne_zero c hc1
138	
139	/-- (FT.3 closer) Over an algebraically closed base field `k`, an element of a
140	`k`-algebra field `K` that is algebraic over `k` lies in the image of
141	`algebraMap k K`. Verified route (analogist iter-154): `k⟮b⟯` is finite over `k`
142	(`IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional`), hence integral, so
143	`IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` makes `algebraMap k k⟮b⟯`
144	surjective; pull the generator back. -/
145	private lemma _algebraic_mem_range {k K : Type*} [Field k] [Field K] [IsAlgClosed k]
146	    [Algebra k K] {b : K} (hb : IsAlgebraic k b) :
147	    b ∈ (algebraMap k K).range := by
148	  haveI : FiniteDimensional k k⟮b⟯ := IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional hb.isIntegral
149	  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k k⟮b⟯ := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k k⟮b⟯
150	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
151	    (IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral (k := k) (K := k⟮b⟯)).surjective
152	      (IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.gen k b)
153	  exact ⟨c, by
154	    have := congrArg (algebraMap k⟮b⟯ K) hc
155	    rwa [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.algebraMap_gen]
156	      at this⟩
157	
158	/-- Algebra-level core (KDM): for `k` an algebraically closed field of
159	characteristic 0 (`[IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]`) and `B` a finite-type
160	`k`-algebra that is an integral domain (`[IsDomain B]`), if `b : B` satisfies
161	`D b = 0` in `Ω[B⁄k]` then `b ∈ range (algebraMap k B)`. This is the
162	chart-algebra (β-core ring-side) helper of the iter-144 piece (ii) pivot,
163	formalising the "constants = kernel of the universal derivation" content of
164	the Kähler derivation in characteristic 0.
165	
166	The JOINT `[IsAlgClosed k]` + `[IsDomain B]` hypotheses are essential — the
167	bare `B`-only form is FALSE (counterexamples: `B = k × k` killed by
168	`[IsDomain B]`; `B = ℚ(√2)/ℚ` killed by `[IsAlgClosed k]`).
169	
170	CLOSED iter-154 via the verified single-element / perfect-field /
171	Jacobi–Zariski `H1Cotangent` route (analogist `ftthree-kernel`,
172	`analogies/ftthree-kernel-iter154.md`; blueprint `RigidityKbar.tex` § FT):
173	* (FT.1) push `D_B b = 0` to the fraction field `K = Frac B` via
174	  `KaehlerDifferential.map_D`; it suffices to put `algebraMap B K b` in
175	  `range (algebraMap k K)` and pull back along the injective `B ↪ K`;
176	* (FT.2) reduce to `IsAlgebraic k (algebraMap B K b)`: if it were
177	  transcendental, embed `F = Frac(k[X]) ↪ K` (`X ↦ bK`); char-0 ⟹
178	  `PerfectField F`, and `K` is `EssFiniteType` over `F`, so
179	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` gives `FormallySmooth F K`, hence
180	  `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent F K)`; the Jacobi–Zariski exact sequence
181	  (`Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange`) then makes `mapBaseChange k F K`
182	  injective, and `mapBaseChange (1 ⊗ D_F X_F) = D_K bK = 0` ⟹ (faithful
183	  flatness, `Module.FaithfullyFlat.one_tmul_eq_zero_iff`) `D_F X_F = 0`;
184	* (FT.3) the base case `_ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero` (`D_F X ≠ 0` via the localized
185	  Kähler module + `polynomialEquiv_D`) contradicts that, so `bK` is algebraic;
186	  `_algebraic_mem_range` (alg-closed `k`) then lands `bK ∈ range`.
187	
188	The standard-smooth / relative-dimension hypotheses (`n`,
189	`[IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]`) are NOT used by this route —
190	only `[IsDomain B]` + `[Algebra.FiniteType k B]` to build `K` and the
191	`EssFiniteType k K` chain — but they are retained on the signature (frozen,
192	harmless) for the downstream consumer.
193	
194	Reverse inclusion: `_hRev` records that `D ∘ algebraMap k B = 0` so the
195	forward direction stated here is the substantive content. Downstream
196	consumer: `GrpObj.df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` below. -/
197	theorem KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero
198	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]
199	    {B : Type u} [CommRing B] [IsDomain B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B]
200	    {n : ℕ} [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]
201	    {b : B} (hDb : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0) :
202	    b ∈ (algebraMap k B).range := by
203	  classical
204	  -- Reverse inclusion (recorded for downstream symmetry):
205	  -- `D : Derivation k B Ω[B⁄k]` vanishes on the algebraMap image by
206	  -- `Derivation.map_algebraMap`.
207	  have _hRev : ∀ a : k, _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B (algebraMap k B a) = 0 :=
208	    fun a => (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B).map_algebraMap a
209	  -- ===========================================================================
210	  -- iter-154 closure: the verified single-element / perfect-field /
211	  -- Jacobi–Zariski `H1Cotangent` route (analogist `ftthree-kernel`,
212	  -- `analogies/ftthree-kernel-iter154.md`). The standard-smooth structure is
213	  -- NOT used by this argument (only `[IsDomain B]` + `[FiniteType k B]` to
214	  -- build `K = Frac B` and `EssFiniteType k K`); `n` and standard-smoothness
215	  -- remain harmless ambient hypotheses.
216	  -- ===========================================================================
217	  -- (FT.1) Push `D_B b = 0` forward to the fraction field `K = Frac B`.
218	  set K := FractionRing B with hK
219	  have hDbK : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k K (algebraMap B K b) = 0 := by
220	    rw [← _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D k k B K b, hDb, map_zero]
221	  -- It suffices to put `algebraMap B K b ∈ range(algebraMap k K)`; pull back
222	  -- along the injective `algebraMap B K` (domain ↪ fraction field).
223	  suffices hbK : algebraMap B K b ∈ (algebraMap k K).range by
224	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hbK
225	    refine ⟨c, ?_⟩
226	    apply IsFractionRing.injective B K
227	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply k B K]
228	    exact hc
229	  -- Reduce to algebraicity of `bK := algebraMap B K b` over `k`, then close
230	  -- with `_algebraic_mem_range` (alg-closed `k`).
231	  apply _algebraic_mem_range
232	  -- (FT.2) Suppose, for contradiction, that `bK` is transcendental over `k`.
233	  by_contra hCon
234	  have hTr : Transcendental k (algebraMap B K b) := hCon
235	  have hInj : Function.Injective ⇑(Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)) :=
236	    transcendental_iff_injective.mp hTr
237	  -- Embed `F = Frac(k[X])` into `K` by `X ↦ bK` via `IsFractionRing.lift`.
238	  set F := FractionRing (Polynomial k) with hF
239	  have hg : Function.Injective ⇑((Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)).toRingHom) := hInj
240	  letI algFK : Algebra F K := (IsFractionRing.lift hg).toAlgebra
241	  have hψ : ∀ x : Polynomial k,
242	      algebraMap F K (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F x) =
243	        (Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)).toRingHom x :=
244	    fun x => IsFractionRing.lift_algebraMap hg x
245	  -- Scalar tower `k → F → K`: `algebraMap F K ∘ algebraMap k F = algebraMap k K`.
246	  haveI : IsScalarTower k F K := by
247	    refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (fun x => ?_)
248	    rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply k (Polynomial k) F x, hψ]
249	    change algebraMap k K x =
250	      (Polynomial.aeval (algebraMap B K b)) (algebraMap k (Polynomial k) x)
251	    rw [Polynomial.algebraMap_eq, Polynomial.aeval_C]
252	  -- `K` is essentially of finite type over `F` (via `EssFiniteType k K`).
253	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType k K := Algebra.EssFiniteType.comp k B (FractionRing B)
254	  haveI : Algebra.EssFiniteType F K := Algebra.EssFiniteType.of_comp k F K
255	  -- `F` is a perfect field (char 0) ⟹ `K/F` formally smooth ⟹ `H¹` vanishes.
256	  haveI : PerfectField F := PerfectField.ofCharZero
257	  haveI : Algebra.FormallySmooth F K := Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField
258	  -- (FT.2 core) `mapBaseChange k F K` is injective via the Jacobi–Zariski
259	  -- exact sequence + `Subsingleton (H1Cotangent F K)` from formal smoothness.
260	  have hMapInj : Function.Injective (_root_.KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange k F K) := by
261	    rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
262	    intro x hx
263	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := (Algebra.H1Cotangent.exact_δ_mapBaseChange k F K x).mp hx
264	    rw [Subsingleton.elim y 0, map_zero]
265	  -- `algebraMap F K (image of X) = bK`.
266	  have hbKeq : algebraMap F K (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X) =
267	      algebraMap B K b := by
268	    rw [hψ]; exact Polynomial.aeval_X _
269	  -- `mapBaseChange (1 ⊗ D_F X_F) = D_K (algebraMap F K X_F) = D_K bK = 0`.
270	  have hident : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange k F K
271	      (1 ⊗ₜ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k F
272	        (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X)) =
273	      _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k K
274	        (algebraMap F K (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X)) := by
275	    rw [_root_.KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange_tmul, one_smul,
276	      _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_D]
277	  have h1tmul : (1 ⊗ₜ _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k F
278	      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X) : K ⊗[F] Ω[F⁄k]) = 0 := by
279	    apply hMapInj
280	    rw [map_zero, hident, hbKeq]
281	    exact hDbK
282	  -- Faithful flatness of the field extension `F → K` cancels the `1 ⊗ -`.
283	  have hDFX : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k F
284	      (algebraMap (Polynomial k) F Polynomial.X) = 0 :=
285	    (Module.FaithfullyFlat.one_tmul_eq_zero_iff F (Ω[F⁄k]) _).mp h1tmul
286	  -- (FT.3 base case) But `D_F X_F ≠ 0` — contradiction.
287	  exact _ratfunc_D_X_ne_zero hDFX
288	
289	namespace GrpObj
290	
291	/-- Chart-algebra (β-core): per-chart translation-invariance Kähler-derivation.
292	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C/k` and an affine chart
293	`V ⊆ C` whose chart-ring `B = Γ(V, O_C)` carries a finite-type `k`-algebra
294	structure, a chart-section `b ∈ B` with vanishing universal Kähler derivation
295	`KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0` lies in `range (algebraMap k B)` — the chart-
296	local kernel-of-`D` content of the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot piece (ii) is
297	the algebra-level statement that constants are the image of the base field.
298	
299	Iter-147 signature refinement: the chart-of-proper-curve hypothesis is carried
300	as typeclass arguments on `C` (smooth, proper, reduced, geometrically
301	irreducible over `Spec k`); the chart-ring `B` is carried as a separate finite-
302	type `k`-algebra argument with the (chart-side identification `B ≃ₐ[k] Γ(V, O_C)`
303	deferred to the consumer site via the `Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` refinement
304	plan; iter-148+). The body delegates to the algebra-level KDM helper
305	`KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero` above.
306	
307	The full 5-step closure path is documented in `blueprint/src/chapters/
308	RigidityKbar.tex` § "Chart-algebra piece (ii) first-class decomposition"
309	under `lem:chart_algebra_df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`:
310	(1) chart-Kähler kernel extraction (this is KDM); (2) standard-smooth chart
311	witness from `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`;
312	(3) 2-chart Čech Mayer–Vietoris on `Ω_{C/k}^{⊕n}` reusing
313	`Cohomology/MayerVietoris*`; (4) 2-chart-cover existence per Stacks 0F8L;
314	(5) char-p Frobenius patch via `RingHom.iterateFrobenius_comm`. -/
315	theorem df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart
316	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] [CharZero k]
317	    {C : Scheme.{u}} [C.Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
318	    [IsProper (C ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
319	    [Smooth (C ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
320	    [IsReduced C]
321	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (C ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k))]
322	    {B : Type u} [CommRing B] [IsDomain B] [Algebra k B] [Algebra.FiniteType k B]
323	    {n : ℕ} [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]
324	    {b : B} (hDb : _root_.KaehlerDifferential.D k B b = 0) :
325	    b ∈ (algebraMap k B).range :=
326	  -- Iter-149 signature inflation: `[CharZero k]` and
327	  -- `[Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B]` propagated
328	  -- from the KDM (p2) bridge directive in `PROGRESS.md` § "Iter-149
329	  -- Lane 2". Char-p alternative (p1) path is deferred. The scheme-
330	  -- level chart-of-proper-curve hypotheses on `C` remain the
331	  -- standing premise for a future char-p reactivation if the
332	  -- project widens the KDM commitment.
333	  AlgebraicGeometry.KaehlerDifferential.mem_range_algebraMap_of_D_eq_zero (n := n) hDb
334	
335	end GrpObj
336	
337	/-- Integrally-closed-constants helper: in a (smooth) proper geometrically
338	irreducible scheme `X` over a base field `k`, the global sections
339	`Γ(X, O_X)` are exhausted by the image of the structure-morphism's
340	action on the top open, i.e.\ the `appTop` ring hom of the structure
341	morphism `X ⟶ Spec k` is surjective. Equivalently, `Γ(X, O_X) ≅ k` as
342	`k`-algebras (modulo the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≃ k`).
343	
344	Blueprint: `chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
345	`\lem:constants_integral_over_base_field`. CLOSED iter-153 under the
346	iter-152 alg-closed pivot (`[IsAlgClosed k]`); the proof is three steps:
347	(1) `IsReduced X` + `GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec k)` (over the
348	    singleton-base `Spec k`) ⇒ `IrreducibleSpace X`
349	    (`GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`) ⇒
350	    `IsIntegral X` (`isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`);
351	    `IsProper` ⇒ `UniversallyClosed` ⇒ `Γ(X, O_X)` is a field
352	    (`isField_of_universallyClosed`).
353	(2) `IsProper` (⇒ `UniversallyClosed` + `LocallyOfFiniteType`) ⇒ the
354	    structure-morphism `appTop` ring hom `k → Γ(X, O_X)` is finite
355	    (`finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`), hence `Γ(X, O_X)` is
356	    integral over `k`.
357	(3) `k` algebraically closed ⇒ an integral extension is trivial:
358	    `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` gives
359	    `algebraMap k Γ(X, O_X)` bijective, hence surjective, i.e.
360	    `RingHom.range (appTop.hom) = ⊤`. (Under `[IsAlgClosed k]` the
361	    former base-change-to-`\bar k` substep collapses entirely — no
362	    separability/inseparability factorisation is used. The old
363	    `ChartAlgebraS3` route was removed iter-155 as dead scaffolding.)
364	
365	The `[IsReduced X]` hypothesis is added explicitly because Mathlib
366	snapshot `b80f227` lacks the lemma `Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` over a field —
367	see the `Rigidity.lean` "Hypothesis history" block for the same
368	explicit-`IsReduced` discipline. -/
369	theorem constants_integral_over_base_field
370	    {k : Type u} [Field k] [IsAlgClosed k] {X : Scheme.{u}} [X.Over (Spec (.of k))]
371	    [IsProper (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
372	    [Smooth (X ↘ Spec (.of k))]
373	    [IsReduced X]
374	    [GeometricallyIrreducible (X ↘ Spec (.of k))] :
375	    RingHom.range ((X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).appTop.hom) = ⊤ := by
376	  -- Iter-153 alg-closed closure. Under `[IsAlgClosed k]` the proof
377	  -- collapses to three steps: (1) `X` integral ⟹ `Γ(X, O_X)` a field;
378	  -- (2) properness ⟹ `Γ(X, O_X)` finite (hence integral) over `k`;
379	  -- (3) `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` gives the
380	  -- structure ring hom `k → Γ(X, O_X)` bijective, hence surjective,
381	  -- which is the displayed `range = ⊤`.
382	  set f := X ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k) with hf
383	  -- (1) `X` is integral: smoothness gives `IsReduced X`, geometric
384	  -- irreducibility over the singleton base `Spec k` gives `IrreducibleSpace X`,
385	  -- and the two combine to `IsIntegral X`.
386	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X :=
387	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton f
388	  haveI : IsIntegral X := isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced X
389	  -- (1, cont.) properness ⟹ universally closed ⟹ `Γ(X, ⊤)` is a field.
390	  letI : Field Γ(X, ⊤) := (isField_of_universallyClosed k f).toField
391	  -- (2) The composite `F : k → Γ(X, ⊤)` (structure map through the
392	  -- canonical `ΓSpecIso`) is finite, hence integral over `k`.
393	  let F := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫ f.appTop
394	  have hFinF : F.hom.Finite := by
395	    apply RingHom.finite_respectsIso.2
396	      (e := (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv)
397	    exact finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k f
398	  letI : Algebra k Γ(X, ⊤) := F.hom.toAlgebra
399	  haveI : Module.Finite k Γ(X, ⊤) := hFinF
400	  haveI : Algebra.IsIntegral k Γ(X, ⊤) := Algebra.IsIntegral.of_finite k Γ(X, ⊤)
401	  -- (3) `k` algebraically closed ⟹ `algebraMap k Γ(X, ⊤) = F.hom` is
402	  -- bijective, hence surjective.
403	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k Γ(X, ⊤)) :=
404	    IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral
405	  -- Convert the range goal to surjectivity of `f.appTop.hom`; since
406	  -- `F.hom = f.appTop.hom ∘ (ΓSpecIso).inv.hom` is surjective, so is
407	  -- `f.appTop.hom`.
408	  rw [RingHom.range_eq_top]
409	  have hFsurj : Function.Surjective ⇑F.hom := hbij.surjective
410	  have hcomp : F.hom = f.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom := rfl
411	  rw [hcomp, RingHom.coe_comp] at hFsurj
412	  exact hFsurj.of_comp
413	
414	namespace Scheme
415	
416	namespace Over
417	
418	/-- Scheme-level lift: two morphisms `f, g : C ⟶ A` in `Over (Spec k)`
419	agreeing on a non-empty open subset `U ⊆ C.left` are equal, when `A`
420	is separated over `Spec k`, `C.left` is reduced, and `C` is
421	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k`.
422	
423	This is a thin renaming of the iter-125 packaging
424	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` (in
425	`AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean`) under the
426	`Scheme.Over.ext_of_diff_zero` name committed by the iter-145
427	chart-algebra planner. Per the blueprint's three-step
428	proof recipe (`chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
429	`\lem:Scheme_Over_ext_of_diff_zero`), the iter-125 packaging
430	discharges Step 3 (the `ext_of_eqOnOpen` packaging) directly given the
431	`eqOnOpen` hypothesis. Iter-147+ will refine the signature to *also*
432	take a chart-algebra `df = dg` hypothesis and *derive* `eqOnOpen` from
433	it via Steps 1–2 of the recipe (chart-algebra
434	`df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart` β-core; currently a
435	deferred sub-piece, see L97 above). The lift here is the iter-146
436	sorry-free closure under the planner's chart-algebra envelope; the
437	substantive Steps 1–2 derivation lands once the β-core sub-piece
438	closes.
439	
440	Hypotheses match `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`; the renaming preserves
441	the iter-125 proof discipline that `IsSeparated A.hom` (not the
442	stronger `IsProper`) is sufficient for the rigidity packaging. -/
443	theorem ext_of_diff_zero
444	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
445	    {C A : Over (Spec (.of k))}
446	    [IsSeparated A.hom]
447	    [IsReduced C.left]
448	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
449	    (f g : C ⟶ A) (U : C.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set C.left).Nonempty)
450	    (hUf : (U.ι : (U : C.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ C.left) ≫ f.left =
451	      (U.ι : (U : C.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ C.left) ≫ g.left) :
452	    f = g :=
453	  AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen f g U hU hUf
454	
455	end Over
456	
457	end Scheme
458	
459	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials

9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth

14	/-!
15	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
16	
17	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
18	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
19	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
20	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
21	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
22	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
23	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
24	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
25	
26	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
27	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
28	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
29	
30	This file contains the definition `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` below, the
31	structural-shape acceptance lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`
32	below, and the companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`
33	below (rank = relative dimension `n` from
34	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, closed iter-132).
35	
36	## Status (iter-132 close: rank lemma)
37	
38	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is fully constructed
39	(no `sorry`). The iter-128 body (presheaf evaluated at the top open then
40	extended scalars to `k`) was found in iter-129 to compute the zero
41	`k`-module for every `G` in the target class with `n ≥ 1` (`Spec` of a
42	field is a single point, so the pulled-back presheaf collapses to `k`
43	and the Kähler module of the identity `k → k` is zero). The iter-130
44	fix-up replaced it with **Replacement (B): affine-chart base change** —
45	extract an affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image
46	on which the algebraic Kähler module is free of rank `n` (via
47	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`), then base-change to `k` along the
48	identity section restricted to `V`. The iter-130 realisation passed
49	through `Classical.choice` to bridge `Prop` and `Type`, which left the
50	elaborated body with `Classical.choice` as its outermost head symbol —
51	opaque to downstream `unfold`/`whnf`. The **iter-131 fix-up** refactors
52	the body to a pure-term `noncomputable def`: `let`-bindings of
53	`Classical.choose`/`.choose_spec` on the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
54	existential expose `U, V, e, ψV` as named-but-opaque accessors, while
55	the outer expression is the explicit
56	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`. The
57	signature, set in iter-129 (free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
58	binder), is preserved. The iter-131 body shape is witnessed by
59	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below.
60	
61	The **iter-132 prover lane** closed the companion rank lemma
62	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below) against the
63	iter-131 body via a parallel `Classical.choose`-chain extraction on
64	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, reducing through
65	`Module.finrank_baseChange` + `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq` to pin the
66	`k`-finrank of the cotangent space at the identity to the relative
67	dimension `n` from the `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
68	
69	## References
70	
71	- Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i)".
72	- Analogist source-of-truth: `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
73	  (Replacement (B) construction; iter-130 prover lane closure chain).
74	- Project infrastructure consumed:
75	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`
76	  (in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`).
77	- Mathlib `GrpObj` API: `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`,
78	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth`.
79	- Mathlib Kähler / standard-smooth pieces (consumed via
80	  `smooth_locally_free_omega`):
81	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`,
82	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`.
83	-/
84	
85	set_option autoImplicit false
86	
87	universe u
88	
89	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
90	open TopologicalSpace Opposite
91	
92	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj
93	
94	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
95	
96	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
97	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
98	`ModuleCat k`).
99	
100	Mathematically: the base change to `k` of the algebraic Kähler module
101	`Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` of a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around
102	the identity-section image. By the forward Jacobian criterion
103	(`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, consuming
104	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`),
105	this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G, V)`, hence its base
106	change is a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank `n`; the
107	iter-132 companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (at
108	line 244 below) pins the rank to `n` from the
109	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
110	
111	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
112	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument. Downstream
113	consumers that need the dual tangent space `𝔤` may take
114	`Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`.
115	
116	**Construction (iter-130 prover lane; Replacement (B), affine-chart base
117	change).** The body is built as follows:
118	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
119	   a scheme morphism `ηleft : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
120	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
121	2. Take `x₀ := ηleft default ∈ G.left`, the image of the unique point of
122	   `Spec (.of k)` (which is a singleton because `k` is a field, so
123	   `PrimeSpectrum k` is `Unique`).
124	3. By `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` (forward Jacobian criterion,
125	   project lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`), there exists an
126	   affine open `V ⊆ G.left` containing `x₀`, an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k`
127	   with `V ≤ G.hom⁻¹U`, and the `appLE` algebra
128	   `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G, V)` exhibits `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` as a
129	   free `Γ(G, V)`-module of rank `n`.
130	4. Restricting `ηleft` to `V` yields a ring map
131	   `ηleft.appLE V ⊤ _ : Γ(G, V) → Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the
132	   canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) gives
133	   `ψV : Γ(G, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
134	5. Extension of scalars from `Γ(G, V)` to `k` along `ψV` of the algebraic
135	   Kähler module gives the cotangent space at the identity as a
136	   `k`-module.
137	
138	**Caveat on canonicity.** The chart `V` used in the body is extracted
139	via `Classical.choose` from `smooth_locally_free_omega`'s existential.
140	The resulting `k`-module is therefore non-canonical *as a value*. What
141	the iter-131 body refactor guarantees is that the body's **structural
142	shape** — a base change to `k` of the chart-level Kähler module — is
143	exposed to downstream consumers: after `unfold cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
144	and delta-reduction of the `let`-bindings, the outer head symbol is
145	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars _).obj (ModuleCat.of _ Ω[_ ⁄ _])`. This is
146	formalized as `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below, which
147	the iter-132 rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244
148	below) rewrites against. For the live consumer
149	(rigidity over `k`, via `rigidity_over_kbar`), the existence of a
150	rank-`n` free `k`-module is the only structural content needed, so
151	chart-canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent)
152	presentation via the stalk-side cotangent `𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of
153	the blueprint's `lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
154	
155	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The rank (`= n`) is
156	now pinned by `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below;
157	closed iter-132). The free/finite structural properties
158	(`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`) are content for follow-up companion
159	lemmas (not yet in this file); the structural-shape accessibility needed
160	by those lemmas is witnessed here by
161	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` (line 198 below). -/
162	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
163	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
164	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
165	    ModuleCat k :=
166	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
167	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
168	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
169	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
170	  -- `Classical.choose`-chain through the `Prop`-level existential output of
171	  -- `smooth_locally_free_omega`. Each `let` names one component (chart `U`,
172	  -- chart `V`, the appLE inclusion `e`, and `hxV : x₀ ∈ V`) so the outer
173	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`
174	  -- wrapper at the top level (iter-131 body-shape refactor).
175	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
176	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
177	  let h₁ := h.choose_spec
178	  let V : G.left.Opens := h₁.choose
179	  let h₂ := h₁.choose_spec
180	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h₂.choose
181	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h₂.choose_spec.1
182	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
183	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
184	  let htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
185	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
186	    exact hxV
187	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
188	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
189	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
190	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
191	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
192	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
193	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
194	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
195	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
196	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
197	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
198	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
199	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
200	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
201	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])
202	
203	/-- Defensive lemma: `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is definitionally equal to its
204	explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module form. The chart `V`, the algebra
205	structure, and the Kähler module are opaque (`Classical.choose`-extracted) but
206	the outer `extendScalars`/`ModuleCat.of`-of-`Ω` form is exposed. This proves
207	that the iter-131 body refactor closed the iter-130 opacity defect: a
208	downstream rank lemma can `simp only [cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars]`
209	(combined with a `Classical.choose`-chain `obtain` on the same existential)
210	to rewrite the body to the explicit form. -/
211	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
212	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
213	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
214	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) (V : G.left.Opens) (e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
215	        (htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤
216	          (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]) ⁻¹ᵁ V),
217	      letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
218	        (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
219	      (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G : ModuleCat k) =
220	        (ModuleCat.extendScalars
221	          ((CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]).appLE V ⊤ htop ≫
222	            (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom).hom).obj
223	          (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)]) := by
224	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
225	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
226	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
227	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ h.choose_spec.choose := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
228	  refine ⟨h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
229	    fun s _ => ?_, rfl⟩
230	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) s ∈ h.choose_spec.choose
231	  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
232	  exact hxV
233	
234	/-- **Rank lemma for the cotangent space at the identity.**
235	
236	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `G` over `k` of
237	relative dimension `n`, the `k`-vector space `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` has
238	`finrank` equal to `n`.
239	
240	**Proof strategy (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
241	`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, Steps 1+2 live closure path).**
242	
243	* **Step 1 (chart-side Kähler rank).** Reproduce the iter-131 body's
244	  `Classical.choose`-chain on `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` to extract
245	  the same chart witnesses `U`, `V`, `e` plus `hfree : Module.Free Γ(G, V)
246	  Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` and `hrank : Module.rank ... = n`. Apply
247	  `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` to get
248	  `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…] = n`.
249	
250	* **Step 2 (base-change preserves finrank).** The body of
251	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is by construction the `extendScalars ψ_V`
252	  base change of `Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` from `Γ(G, V)` to `k`. Apply
253	  `Module.finrank_baseChange` to push `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…]` to
254	  `Module.finrank k (k ⊗_{Γ(G,V)} Ω[…])`, discharging the `Module.Free`
255	  hypothesis via `hfree` and the `Nontrivial Γ(G, V)` hypothesis from the
256	  existence of the ring map `ψ_V : Γ(G, V) → k` into the field `k`. -/
257	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
258	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
259	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
260	    Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G) = n := by
261	  -- Reproduce the same `Classical.choose`-chain as the body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`.
262	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
263	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
264	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
265	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
266	  let V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose
267	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose
268	  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
269	  -- Algebra structure on `Γ(G.left, V)` over `Γ(Spec k, U)` (matches the body's `letI`).
270	  letI algGV : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
271	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
272	  -- Freeness + rank witnesses from the existential.
273	  have hfree : Module.Free ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] :=
274	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
275	  have hrank : Module.rank ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] = (n : Cardinal) :=
276	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
277	  -- ψV ring map (matches the body's `ψV`).
278	  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
279	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
280	    exact hxV
281	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
282	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
283	  letI algGVk : Algebra ↥Γ(G.left, V) k := ψV.hom.toAlgebra
284	  -- The Γ(G, V) algebra is Nontrivial because it maps to the field `k`.
285	  haveI : Nontrivial ↥Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial
286	  -- The body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is `(extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`,
287	  -- whose carrier is `TensorProduct Γ(G, V) k Ω[…]` (under `algGVk`). Definitional equality
288	  -- of the carriers reduces the goal to `Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ...) = n`.
289	  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k
290	      (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
291	  -- Step 2: Base-change preserves finrank.
292	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ↥Γ(G.left, V))
293	        (M' := Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])]
294	  -- Step 1: Apply rank=n ⇒ finrank=n.
295	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank

297	/-! ## Piece (i.b) — shear-iso globalisation of the cotangent
298	
299	The next three declarations support the iter-134+ piece (i.b) lane (see
300	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § Piece (i.b)). Their goal is the
301	main lemma `mulRight_globalises_cotangent`:
302	
303	  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom  ≅
304	      pullback_along_str_map (pullback_along_η_G (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom))`
305	
306	stated at the **presheaf-of-modules level** (sheaf-level RHS per the iter-133
307	mathlib-analogist Decision 4 + iter-134 plan-agent pre-commitment). The proof
308	chain has three structural pieces:
309	
310	* Step 1 (`shearMulRight`): build the binary-product shear iso
311	  `σ = ⟨pr₁, μ⟩ : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G` in `Over (Spec k)` from `GrpObj` data only.
312	  This is a categorical statement (no scheme-side content); modelled on
313	  `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (one-input version,
314	  `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:277-281`).
315	
316	* Step 2 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`):
317	  the load-bearing presheaf-of-modules base-change identity
318	  `Ω_{(G ⊗ G)/G} ≅ pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}` on `(G ⊗ G).left`.
319	  NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (~150–300 LOC); chains
320	  `KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquiv` (algebra side) with
321	  `PresheafOfModules.pullback` / `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` (presheaf side).
322	
323	* Step 3 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`):
324	  the section-restriction identity bridging `s^* pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}` to
325	  `π_G^* η_G^* Ω_{G/k}` via `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp` and the
326	  categorical identity `pr_2 ∘ s = η_G ∘ π_G` for `s = ⟨𝟙_G, η_G⟩`.
327	-/
328	
329	section ShearIso
330	
331	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]
332	
333	/-- **Binary-product shear isomorphism** for a group object `G` in a Cartesian
334	monoidal category: the map
335	`σ = lift (fst G G) μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G ⊗ G`, informally `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b)`,
336	is an isomorphism. The inverse is
337	`σ⁻¹ = lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)`, informally
338	`(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b)`.
339	
340	This is the binary-product upgrade of
341	`CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:277-281`),
342	which packages only the one-input version `(· · f)` for a fixed global element
343	`f : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`. Here both factors of `G ⊗ G` are free.
344	
345	Piece (i.b) Step 1 (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` Step 1 of
346	`lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises`). NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (no packaged shear
347	iso for binary products in Mathlib; the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict
348	Decision 1 prescribes this exact construction). -/
349	@[simps]
350	def shearMulRight (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G where
351	  hom := lift (fst G G) μ
352	  inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)
353	  hom_inv_id := by
354	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · (a · b)) = (a, b)`.
355	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
356	    · simp
357	    · -- Goal: `(σ.hom ≫ σ.inv) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
358	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
359	      -- Goal: `σ.hom ≫ (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) = snd G G`
360	      rw [← Category.assoc, comp_lift]
361	      -- Goal: `lift (σ.hom ≫ (fst G G ≫ ι)) (σ.hom ≫ snd G G) ≫ μ = snd G G`
362	      rw [← Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]
363	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ = snd G G`
364	      -- Trick: use `lift_lift_assoc` with f := fst ≫ ι, g := fst, h := snd, then
365	      -- close by `lift_comp_inv_left` and `lift_comp_one_left`.
366	      rw [show (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
367	          lift (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (fst G G) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from by
368	            rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp],
369	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left, MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
370	  inv_hom_id := by
371	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b) ↦ (a, a · (a⁻¹ · b)) = (a, b)`.
372	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
373	    · simp
374	    · -- Goal: `(σ.inv ≫ σ.hom) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
375	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
376	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ = snd G G`
377	      -- Apply `lift_lift_assoc` (← direction): the RHS form `lift f (lift g h ≫ μ) ≫ μ`
378	      -- rewrites to `lift (lift f g ≫ μ) h ≫ μ` with f := fst, g := fst ≫ ι, h := snd.
379	      rw [show (lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ :
380	          G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
381	          lift (lift (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from
382	          (MonObj.lift_lift_assoc (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G)).symm,
383	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_right,
384	        MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
385	
386	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
387	lemma shearMulRight_hom_fst (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
388	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ fst G G = fst G G := by
389	  simp [shearMulRight]
390	
391	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
392	lemma shearMulRight_hom_snd (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
393	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ snd G G = μ := by
394	  simp [shearMulRight]
395	
396	end ShearIso

401	and main lemma of piece (i.b) at the presheaf-of-modules level. Their

408	/-- **Compatibility morphism of structure presheaves of rings** along a scheme
409	morphism `f`. This is the `φ' = ((adj).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c` shape used by
410	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`; we package it once here so the
411	`PresheafOfModules.pullback`-based statements below can refer to it
412	uniformly.

414	Specifically, for a scheme morphism `f : Y ⟶ Z`, this is the adjunction
415	transpose of the structure-sheaf morphism `f.c : O_Z ⟶ f.base _* O_Y`,
416	delivering a morphism `f.base⁻¹ O_Z ⟶ O_Y` of presheaves of rings on

422	obtained via `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom`. The two conventions
423	serve distinct downstream consumers. -/
424	noncomputable def schemeHomRingCompatibility {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ Z) :
425	    (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback (C := CommRingCat) f.base).obj Z.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf :=
426	  ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c

529	Decision 2). A morphism `f : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of modules over a presheaf
530	of rings `R` is an isomorphism iff its component `f.app X` is an iso in
531	`ModuleCat (R.obj X)` for every open `X`.
532	
533	Chains two Mathlib facts: `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R` reflects isomorphisms
534	(via `Balanced` + `Faithful` ⇒ `ReflectsMonomorphisms` + `ReflectsEpimorphisms`
535	⇒ `ReflectsIsomorphisms`, from
536	`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced`), and
537	`NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app` localises iso-checks on natural transformations
538	to pointwise iso-checks.
539	
540	Mirrors `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
541	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:132`) for the
542	`PresheafOfModules` (rather than `SheafOfModules`) category.
543	Upstream-PR candidate. -/
544	private theorem isIso_of_app_iso_module {C : Type*} [Category C]
545	    {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {M N : PresheafOfModules R}
546	    (f : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ X, IsIso (f.app X)) : IsIso f := by
547	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R),
548	      NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
549	  intro X
550	  exact Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat) (f.app X)
551	
552	-- iter-145 EXCISE: `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation`,
553	-- `basechange_along_proj_two_inv`, `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_app_isIso`,
554	-- and `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two` were
555	-- excised in iter-145 per the chart-algebra-skeleton-bundled-excise
556	-- refactor directive (the iter-144 chart-algebra pivot descoped the
557	-- bundled-route route (b'2) IsIso chain; iter-145 strategy-critic Q7
558	-- ruled the "auditable record" disposition was the iter-141 sunk-cost
559	-- pattern recurring). See `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`
560	-- for the iter-145+ chart-algebra route 5-piece skeleton.
561	/-- **Restriction of the relative differentials presheaf along the canonical
562	section** `s := ⟨𝟙_G, η_G⟩ : G ⟶ G ⊗ G` of the first projection in
563	`Over (Spec k)` (piece (i.b) Step 3).
564	
565	Signature is the intended sheaf-level section-restriction iso, per
566	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` Step 3 of
567	`lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` (`lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section`):
568	
569	  `s^*(pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}) ≅ π_G^*(η_G^* Ω_{G/k})`
570	
571	obtained from the categorical identity `pr_2 ∘ s = η_G ∘ π_G` (where
572	`π_G : G ⟶ Spec k` is the structure map and `pr_2 : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` the second
573	projection). The compatibility morphisms for the four scheme morphisms
574	involved are spelled inline via `(Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom _).hom`.
575	
576	**Iter-136 closure (Step 3)**: closed via the categorical identity
577	`s.left ≫ pr_2.left = G.hom ≫ η[G].left` (`section_snd_eq_identity_struct`
578	above) combined with `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp` on both sides. -/
579	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section
580	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G]
581	    {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
582	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
583	    (PresheafOfModules.pullback
584	          (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom
585	            (lift (𝟙 G) (toUnit G ≫ η[G])).left).hom).obj
586	        ((PresheafOfModules.pullback
587	            (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (snd G G).left).hom).obj
588	          (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)) ≅
589	      (PresheafOfModules.pullback
590	          (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom G.hom).hom).obj
591	        ((PresheafOfModules.pullback
592	            (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom
593	              (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G])).hom).obj
594	          (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)) := by
595	  -- Step 1. Use `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp` to merge each side's nested pullback
596	  -- into a single pullback along the composed compatibility morphism.
597	  -- Step 2. The composed compatibility morphisms on each side are *definitionally*
598	  -- equal to `(toRingCatSheafHom (lift.left ≫ snd.left)).hom` and
599	  -- `(toRingCatSheafHom (G.hom ≫ η[G].left)).hom` respectively (by Scheme c-composition
600	  -- `LocallyRingedSpace.comp_c` + commutation of `whiskerLeft`/`whiskerRight`).
601	  -- Step 3. `section_snd_eq_identity_struct` identifies the two single-morphism pullbacks.
602	  have iso1 := (PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp
603	    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (snd G G).left).hom
604	    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (lift (𝟙 G) (toUnit G ≫ η[G])).left).hom).app
605	    (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)
606	  have iso2 := (PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp
607	    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G])).hom
608	    (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom G.hom).hom).app
609	    (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)
610	  refine iso1 ≪≫ ?_ ≪≫ iso2.symm
611	  refine eqToIso ?_
612	  -- After `pullbackComp` both sides reduce to single-morphism pullbacks; the c-composition
613	  -- rule lets us re-express them as `pullback (toRingCatSheafHom <composite>).hom`.
614	  change (PresheafOfModules.pullback
615	        (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom
616	          ((lift (𝟙 G) (toUnit G ≫ η[G])).left ≫ (snd G G).left)).hom).obj
617	      (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom) =
618	    (PresheafOfModules.pullback
619	        (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom
620	          (G.hom ≫ (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]))).hom).obj
621	      (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)
622	  rw [section_snd_eq_identity_struct]
623	
624	-- iter-145 EXCISE: `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` (the iter-135 piece (i.b)
625	-- Main scaffold) was excised per the iter-145 chart-algebra-skeleton-bundled-
626	-- excise refactor directive. Its sorry-body was load-bearing only for the
627	-- bundled-route closure path, which iter-144 descoped via the chart-algebra
628	-- pivot. See `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` for the
629	-- iter-145+ chart-algebra route 5-piece skeleton.
630	
631	end AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
8	
9	/-!
10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
29	follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
30	consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).
31	
32	## Status (iter-177 Lane 7 file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-177 Lane 7** file-skeleton. Each of the six
35	blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
36	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
38	
39	The 6 pinned declarations are:
40	
41	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the
42	   regular morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
43	   `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`. Encoded against the file-internal
44	   placeholder `Pic0.jacobianScheme` for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (the A.3 upstream
45	   has not yet been split out; see §0 below).
46	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.SymmetricPower` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) —
47	   the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C` of the curve `C`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
48	   Body gated on iter-178+ `SymmetricPower.lean` substrate (Milne III.3
49	   Proposition 3.1; Mumford 1970 II.7 / III.11 affine-and-glue recipe).
50	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerAVMap` (noncomputable def, ~5 LOC) —
51	   the symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` produced by
52	   `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` from an input `φ : C ⟶ A`.
53	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerToJacobian` (noncomputable def,
54	   ~5 LOC) — the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
55	   `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`.
56	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.descentThroughBirationalSigma` (theorem,
57	   ~10 LOC) — the descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism
58	   `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` via Milne Theorem 3.2.
59	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.albanese_universal_property` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
60	   the headline UP theorem (Milne Proposition III.6.1).
61	
62	## File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
63	
64	The A.3 row of Route A (the identity-component refinement of the Picard
65	scheme together with its degree map) has not yet been split out into a
66	dedicated chapter or `.lean` file at the time of this file-skeleton
67	landing. We supply a **single helper** here: a `Pic0.Bundle` structure
68	bundling the underlying scheme together with its four abelian-variety
69	instances (`GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `Smooth`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`),
70	plus a typed-`sorry` carrier `Pic0.bundle C` and a derived definition
71	`Pic0.jacobianScheme C := (bundle C).scheme`. The four instances on
72	`jacobianScheme C` are derived from the bundle's fields (no extra
73	`sorry`). Once the A.3 chapter materialises this placeholder collapses to
74	a one-line re-export.
75	
76	Helper budget = 1: the single helper carrying a `sorry` is `Pic0.bundle`.
77	
78	## Note on type expressivity
79	
80	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
81	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
82	
83	- `abelJacobi C P₀ : C ⟶ jacobianScheme C` — a genuine morphism into the
84	  Pic⁰ scheme (not `𝟙 C` or any tautology).
85	- `SymmetricPower C g : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` — a `k̄`-scheme, not `C`
86	  itself or `𝟙_`.
87	- `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A` — a morphism into
88	  the abelian variety `A`.
89	- `symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C`
90	  — a morphism into Pic⁰.
91	- `descentThroughBirationalSigma` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
92	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying
93	  `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g ≫ ψ`.
94	- `albanese_universal_property` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
95	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ`.
96	
97	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
98	(a morphism into the Pic⁰ scheme, a unique factorisation through the
99	Abel–Jacobi map, …); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` /
100	empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Abelian-variety conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
105	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
106	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
107	
108	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
109	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
110	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
111	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
112	
113	A **smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
114	
115	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
116	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
117	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
118	
119	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site.
120	
121	## References
122	
123	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex` (~830 LOC,
124	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §III.6 Proposition 6.1, p. 104;
125	§III.3 Proposition 3.1 (symmetric power), p. 94; §III.5 Theorem 5.1(a)
126	(birationality of `f^{(g)}`), p. 101 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`).
127	-/
128	
129	set_option autoImplicit false
130	
131	universe u
132	
133	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
134	
135	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
136	
137	namespace Pic0
138	
139	/-! ## §0. File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
140	
141	The A.3 row of Route A (identity component of the Picard scheme together
142	with its degree map) is not yet split out into a dedicated chapter / file.
143	The single helper below — a bundled structure carrying the Pic⁰ scheme
144	together with its four abelian-variety instances, plus a typed-`sorry`
145	carrier `bundle` — supplies the placeholder. Once the A.3 chapter
146	materialises this collapses to a one-line re-export.
147	
148	This is the **only** helper in the file that consumes a `sorry`. The
149	derived `jacobianScheme` def and the four typeclass instances below are
150	projections from `bundle` and do not introduce additional `sorry`s. -/
151	
152	/-- File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`: bundles the underlying
153	`k̄`-scheme together with the four abelian-variety structural instances
154	(group object, proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible) supplied at the
155	A.3 row of Route A.
156	
157	Collapses to a re-export of the A.3 chapter once that materialises. -/
158	structure Bundle {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
159	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
160	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
161	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
162	  /-- The underlying scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. -/
163	  scheme : Over (Spec (.of kbar))
164	  /-- Group-object structure on `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. -/
165	  grpObj : GrpObj scheme
166	  /-- Properness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
167	  proper : IsProper scheme.hom
168	  /-- Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
169	  smooth : Smooth scheme.hom
170	  /-- Geometric irreducibility of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
171	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible scheme.hom
172	
173	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
174	
175	variable (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
176	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
177	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
178	
179	/-- File-internal **placeholder carrier** for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` — a typed
180	`sorry` pending the A.3 row chapter. iter-178+: replace with the
181	`Bundle` produced by `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean` (A.2.c) after
182	its identity-component / degree-map refinement (A.3) lands. -/
183	noncomputable def bundle : Bundle C := sorry
184	
185	/-- The underlying `k̄`-scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of a smooth proper
186	geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed field
187	`k̄`. Read off from the `Bundle` placeholder. -/
188	noncomputable def jacobianScheme : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := (bundle C).scheme
189	
190	/-- Group-object structure on `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. -/
191	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (jacobianScheme C) := (bundle C).grpObj
192	
193	/-- Properness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
194	instance instIsProper : IsProper (jacobianScheme C).hom := (bundle C).proper
195	
196	/-- Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
197	instance instSmooth : Smooth (jacobianScheme C).hom := (bundle C).smooth
198	
199	/-- Geometric irreducibility of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
200	instance instGeomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible (jacobianScheme C).hom :=
201	  (bundle C).geomIrred
202	
203	/-! ## §1. The Abel–Jacobi morphism at a marked point
204	
205	The first sub-lemma packages the construction and elementary regularity
206	of the Abel–Jacobi morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` at the basepoint
207	`P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`. On `k̄`-points,
208	`ι_{P₀}(Q) = [𝓞_C(Q − P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`; at the basepoint
209	itself `ι_{P₀}(P₀) = η_J`.
210	
211	This is the content gated on A.3 (existence of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` as a
212	representable group scheme); the morphism is the moduli classifier of
213	the rigidified diagonal correspondence
214	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})` on `C × C`, taken as a
215	relative degree-zero line bundle on `C × C` over the second factor.
216	
217	Blueprint reference: `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism` (Milne, *Abelian
218	Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1 and Summary III.6.11). -/
219	
220	/-- **The Abel–Jacobi morphism at the marked basepoint `P₀`.**
221	
222	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an
223	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked `k̄`-point
224	`P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the **Abel–Jacobi morphism** is the unique regular
225	morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` characterised on `T`-points by the
226	formula `(1 × ι_{P₀})^* 𝓜^{P₀} ≈ 𝓛^{P₀}`, where `𝓜^{P₀}` is the
227	Poincaré–Picard correspondence on `C × Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` and
228	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})`.
229	
230	iter-178+: body constructed by applying the moduli interpretation of
231	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`def:pic_scheme` together with the identity-component
232	refinement A.3) to the relative degree-zero line bundle `𝓛^{P₀}` on
233	`C × C → C` along the second projection. Yoneda then produces the
234	unique classifying morphism `C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. The basepoint
235	condition `P₀ ≫ abelJacobi C P₀ = η[Pic⁰_{C/k̄}]` (`lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`)
236	holds because restriction of `𝓛^{P₀}` to `C × {P₀}` is the trivial
237	line bundle `𝓞_C(P₀ − P₀) ≅ 𝓞_C` whose moduli class is `η_J`.
238	
239	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
240	noncomputable def abelJacobi (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) :
241	    C ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=
242	  sorry
243	
244	/-! ## §2. The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`
245	
246	The `g`-th symmetric power of `C` is the quotient of the `g`-fold
247	Cartesian power `C^g` by the natural permutation action of the
248	symmetric group `S_g`. Mathlib `b80f227` has no formalised symmetric
249	power of a scheme — only `Sym` / `SymmetricPower` for types and
250	modules — so the scheme-theoretic construction must be supplied
251	project-side, following Milne's recipe of §III.3 Proposition 3.1: on
252	each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C` form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then glue
253	along the standard open-cover patching (Mumford 1970 §II.7 / §III.11).
254	
255	This sub-build is a non-trivial dependency of A.4.d that the prover
256	must absorb before the body of `symmetricPowerAVMap` /
257	`symmetricPowerToJacobian` closes; the carrier here records the
258	substantive *target* `Sym^g C : Over (Spec k̄)` with a typed `sorry`
259	body, deferring the affine-and-glue construction to iter-178+
260	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean`.
261	
262	Blueprint reference: `def:symmetric_power_curve` (Milne, *Abelian
263	Varieties*, §III.3, Proposition 3.1, p. 94). -/
264	
265	/-- **The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`** of a complete nonsingular
266	curve `C` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
267	
268	Milne III.3 Proposition 3.1 constructs `Sym^g C` as the quotient of
269	`C^g` by the natural permutation action of the symmetric group `S_g`:
270	on each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C`, form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then
271	glue along the standard open-cover patching. The construction comes
272	equipped with a canonical finite surjective symmetric morphism
273	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` characterised by the universal property that
274	every `S_g`-symmetric morphism `C^g ⟶ T` factors uniquely through `π`.
275	
276	This pin records the substantive *target* scheme `Sym^g C`; the
277	projection `π` and its universal property are recorded as helper API
278	in the iter-178+ `Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate file.
279	
280	iter-178+: body is the gluing-of-affines `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`
281	construction over an open affine cover of `C`. For the iter-177
282	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`.
283	
284	The signature mentions `C` explicitly (not via the file's `variable`
285	block) so that the declaration's first explicit argument is the curve
286	`C`, matching the call sites `SymmetricPower C g`. -/
287	noncomputable def SymmetricPower
288	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
289	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
290	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
291	    (_g : ℕ) : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
292	  sorry
293	
294	/-! ## §3. The morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`
295	
296	The second sub-lemma is the elementary fact that the symmetric
297	assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` from `C^g` to `A`
298	descends through `π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C`. The key point is that the
299	`g`-fold addition morphism `add_A : A^g ⟶ A` is itself `S_g`-symmetric
300	because the underlying group object `A` is commutative as a group
301	scheme; combined with the universal property of `Sym^g C`, this yields
302	the unique factorisation.
303	
304	Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` (Milne's proof of
305	III.6.1, p. 104, "Clearly this is symmetric, and so it factors through
306	`C^{(g)}`"). -/
307	
308	/-- **The symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`.**
309	
310	Let `φ : C ⟶ A` be a morphism into an abelian variety `A` over `k̄`.
311	The composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g} : C^g ⟶ A^g ⟶ A` sending
312	`(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is `S_g`-equivariant (where `S_g`
313	permutes the factors of `C^g` and acts trivially on `A`), because
314	addition in a commutative group object is order-independent. By the
315	universal property of `Sym^g C` (`def:symmetric_power_curve`) it
316	factors uniquely as `Sym^g φ ∘ π = add_A ∘ φ^{×g}`.
317	
318	iter-178+: body is the universal-property invocation on the
319	symmetric composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g}` once the iter-178+
320	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate exposes the projection `π`
321	and its UP. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
322	noncomputable def symmetricPowerAVMap (g : ℕ)
323	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
324	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
325	    (_φ : C ⟶ A) :
326	    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A :=
327	  sorry
328	
329	/-! ## §4. The birational morphism `Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
330	
331	The third sub-lemma is the birational identification of `Sym^g C` with
332	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. After choosing the basepoint `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the cycle
333	map `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ [𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)]` defines a regular
334	morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` which is birational by
335	Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) (the proof uses dimension count plus the
336	Riemann–Roch identification of the generic fibre as a single point on
337	a curve of genus `g`).
338	
339	Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian` (Milne,
340	*Abelian Varieties*, Theorem III.5.1(a), p. 101). -/
341	
342	/-- **The birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`.**
343	
344	Given a basepoint `P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the symmetrisation of the Abel–Jacobi
345	morphism `add_J ∘ ι_{P₀}^{×g} : C^g ⟶ J^g ⟶ J` descends through
346	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` to a regular morphism
347	`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` sending an effective divisor class
348	`[P₁ + ⋯ + P_g]` to `[𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`.
349	By Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) this morphism is *birational*: it is
350	dominant with one-dimensional fibres, and its generic fibre is a single
351	`k̄`-point (the linear system `|D|` of a general degree-`g` divisor `D`
352	on a curve of genus `g` is `0`-dimensional by Riemann–Roch).
353	
354	The birationality data (a dense open subset `U ⊆ Sym^g C` mapping
355	isomorphically onto a dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) is recorded as
356	helper API in the iter-178+ proof body of
357	`descentThroughBirationalSigma`.
358	
359	iter-178+: body is the specialisation of `symmetricPowerAVMap` to
360	`A := jacobianScheme C` and `φ := abelJacobi C P₀`. For the iter-177
361	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
362	noncomputable def symmetricPowerToJacobian
363	    (_P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) (g : ℕ) :
364	    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=
365	  sorry
366	
367	/-! ## §5. Descent through the birational quotient
368	
369	The final auxiliary lemma packages the two-step descent that promotes
370	`Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` (after passing through the birational morphism
371	`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) to a regular morphism
372	`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The descent is a rational map
373	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A` on the dense open of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` on which
374	`f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism via
375	Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (the sibling `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`).
376	This is the unique step of the proof that consumes A.4.c.
377	
378	Blueprint reference: `lem:descent_through_birational_sigma` (Milne's
379	proof of III.6.1, p. 104, "It therefore defines a rational map
380	`ψ : J → A`, which (I 3.2) shows to be a regular map"). -/
381	
382	/-- **Descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.**
383	
384	There is a *unique* regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
385	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ`
386	(equivalently, `ψ` is the unique regular extension of the rational map
387	`Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1} : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`).
388	
389	The rational map is constructed on the dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
390	where `f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism
391	on all of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` via Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (sibling
392	`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` in
393	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c). Uniqueness is the
394	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle: two regular
395	morphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` agreeing on a dense open are equal.
396	
397	iter-178+: body invokes `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` on the
398	rational map produced by inverting `f^{(g)}` on its dense open of
399	isomorphism. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed
400	`sorry`. -/
401	theorem descentThroughBirationalSigma
402	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
403	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
404	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
405	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
406	    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
407	      symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
408	        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ := by
409	  sorry
410	
411	/-! ## §5.5. Albanese ⇔ symmetric-power equation (connecting biconditional)
412	
413	The iter-182 structural decomposition extracts the precise *connecting
414	identity* between the Albanese-form factorisation
415	`φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` and the symmetric-power-form factorisation
416	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀
417	(genus C) ≫ ψ` as a named typed-`sorry` biconditional helper. With this
418	biconditional in hand the body of `albanese_universal_property` reduces
419	to sorry-free assembly from `descentThroughBirationalSigma`. -/
420	
421	/-- **Connecting biconditional: Albanese-form equation ⇔ symmetric-power-form
422	equation.**
423	
424	For every candidate descent morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`, the Albanese-form
425	factorisation `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` holds iff the symmetric-power-form
426	factorisation `symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian
427	C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ` holds. The forward direction precomposes both sides of
428	the Albanese equation with the symmetric `g`-fold sum, then descends through
429	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` and uses the additivity of `ψ` (a homomorphism of group
430	schemes). The reverse direction restricts the symmetric-power-form equation
431	along the diagonal embedding `Q ↦ (Q, P₀, …, P₀)` and uses
432	`φ(P₀) = η_A`.
433	
434	iter-200+: body uses the `Sym^g` projection π and its UP from
435	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` (substrate file not yet opened),
436	together with the explicit Milne III §6 identification of `ι_{P₀}` with
437	the composite of `Q ↦ Q + (g - 1) P₀` followed by `f^{(g)}`. For the
438	iter-182 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry` — but the named
439	substantive helper exposes the precise connecting identity, allowing
440	`albanese_universal_property` to be assembled sorry-free from it. -/
441	theorem albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq
442	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
443	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
444	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
445	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
446	    ∀ (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
447	      (φ = abelJacobi C P0 ≫ ψ) ↔
448	      (symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
449	        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ) :=
450	  sorry
451	
452	/-! ## §6. The Albanese universal property — headline theorem
453	
454	The headline result of the chapter: for every pointed morphism
455	`φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with `φ(P₀) = η_A`, there
456	exists a unique regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
457	`φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}`. The proof follows Milne Proposition III.6.1
458	verbatim, using the four sub-lemmas above plus Milne Theorem I.3.2
459	(`extend_to_av`) and Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
460	consequence).
461	
462	Blueprint reference: `thm:albanese_universal_property` (Milne,
463	*Abelian Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1, p. 104). -/
464	
465	/-- **Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` — Milne III §6
466	Proposition 6.1.**
467	
468	For a complete nonsingular curve `C` of genus `g > 0` over an
469	algebraically closed field `k̄`, a marked `k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, and
470	every pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with
471	`φ(P₀) = η_A`, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
472	`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that `φ = ι_{P₀} ≫ ψ`.
473	
474	Proof sketch (Milne III.6.1):
475	1. **Symmetrisation.** `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is
476	   `S_g`-symmetric (`A` commutative), so factors through
477	   `Sym^g C` as `Sym^g φ` (`lem:symmetric_product_av_map`).
478	2. **Descent to a regular morphism on `J`.** The birational morphism
479	   `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`)
480	   exhibits `Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1}` as a rational map
481	   `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, which by Milne Theorem I.3.2 extends uniquely to
482	   a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`
483	   (`lem:descent_through_birational_sigma`).
484	3. **Factorisation `ψ ∘ ι_{P₀} = φ`.** Use Milne's identification of
485	   `ι_{P₀}` with the composite `Q ↦ Q + (g − 1) P₀` followed by
486	   `f^{(g)}`; together with `φ(P₀) = η_A` and the additive structure
487	   of `A`, this gives `ψ(ι_{P₀}(Q)) = φ(Q)` on `k̄`-points, hence
488	   globally by the reduced-and-separated agreement principle.
489	4. **Homomorphism property.** `ψ` sends `η_J = ι_{P₀}(P₀)` to
490	   `φ(P₀) = η_A`, so by Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
491	   consequence) it is a homomorphism of group schemes.
492	5. **Uniqueness.** Two homomorphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` with the same
493	   restriction to `ι_{P₀}(C)` agree on the `g`-fold sum
494	   `ι_{P₀}(C) + ⋯ + ι_{P₀}(C) = Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (since `f^{(g)}` is
495	   surjective), hence are equal.
496	
497	The iter-177 file-skeleton encodes only the existence-and-uniqueness
498	of the factoring morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` (matching the
499	project's `IsAlbanese` precedent in `Jacobian.lean`); the
500	group-homomorphism property is implicit in the construction. The body
501	consumes its four sub-lemma exports plus
502	`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (A.4.c) and Milne Corollary I.1.2
503	(registered in `chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
504	
505	iter-182 update: structural decomposition lands the body as
506	sorry-free assembly from `descentThroughBirationalSigma` (§5)
507	combined with the named typed-`sorry` biconditional helper
508	`albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq` (§5.5). The remaining substantive
509	gaps (substrate files `Pic⁰` representability, `Sym^g C` construction,
510	`extend_to_av` rational-map extension, and the connecting biconditional
511	body itself) remain blocking and are deferred to iter-200+. -/
512	theorem albanese_universal_property
513	    (_hg : 0 < genus C)
514	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
515	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
516	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
517	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
518	    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A), φ = abelJacobi C P0 ≫ ψ := by
519	  -- Structural decomposition (iter-182): reduce to the symmetric-power
520	  -- descent `descentThroughBirationalSigma` plus the connecting
521	  -- biconditional `albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq`. The remaining
522	  -- substantive gap is the biconditional helper (named typed-sorry); the
523	  -- proof body below is sorry-free assembly.
524	  have key := albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq C P0 φ _hφ
525	  obtain ⟨ψ, hψ_sym, huniq_sym⟩ := descentThroughBirationalSigma C P0 φ _hφ
526	  exact ⟨ψ, (key ψ).mpr hψ_sym,
527	    fun ψ' hψ' => huniq_sym ψ' ((key ψ').mp hψ')⟩
528	
529	end Pic0
530	
531	end AlgebraicGeometry
