6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Coheight ↔ Krull dimension of stalk bridge
10	
11	This file packages the project-side bridge between the **topological coheight**
12	of a scheme point and the **ring-theoretic Krull dimension** of its stalk. It
13	is the iter-183 Lane M deliverable per
14	`analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md` Decision 2, with blueprint chapter
15	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CoheightBridge.tex`.
16	
17	The four declarations are:
18	
19	1. `Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding` — for `X` a topological space and
20	   `U ⊆ X` open, `coheight z (in X) = coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in U)` (both wrt the
21	   specialisation preorder, pinned explicitly via `@`).
22	2. `Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum` — for `R` a `CommRingCat` and
23	   `p : Spec R`, `coheight p (in Spec R) = height ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩
24	   (in PrimeSpectrum R)`.
25	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` — the bridge:
26	   `ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z) = coheight z` for any scheme point.
27	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one` — codim-1
28	   wrapper: `coheight z = 1 → Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk z)`.
29	
30	The first three lemmas are natural Mathlib upstream candidates; the fourth is
31	the project-facing consumer wrapper used in
32	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (`hreg_dim` Krull-dim conjunct) and
33	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` (`Scheme.RationalMap.order` instance argument).
34	-/
35	
36	open Order TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory
37	
38	universe u
39	
40	namespace Order
41	
42	/-- **Open subsets preserve `Order.coheight`** for points of a topological
43	space carrying the specialisation preorder. Both `Order.coheight` instances
44	are pinned to `specializationPreorder` explicitly (via `@`) so the statement
45	is well-defined even when `X` carries no other `Preorder` instance.
46	
47	Forward bound: `Subtype.val` is continuous, hence spec-monotone, and is
48	injective; therefore strictly monotone. Reverse bound: every generisation of a
49	point in an open set lies in that open (`Specializes.mem_open`), so a chain
50	starting at `z` in `X` lifts to a chain in `↥U` of the same length. -/
51	lemma coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding
52	    {X : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] {U : Set X} (hU : IsOpen U)
53	    (z : X) (hz : z ∈ U) :
54	    @Order.coheight X (specializationPreorder X) z
55	      = @Order.coheight U (specializationPreorder U) ⟨z, hz⟩ := by
56	  letI : Preorder X := specializationPreorder X
57	  letI : Preorder ↥U := specializationPreorder ↥U
58	  -- `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is monotone for the spec preorder (since it is
59	  -- continuous), and is injective; hence strictly monotone.
60	  have hmono : Monotone (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) :=
61	    continuous_subtype_val.specialization_monotone
62	  have hstrict : StrictMono (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) := by
63	    intro a b hab
64	    refine ⟨hmono hab.le, fun h => ?_⟩
65	    apply hab.not_ge
66	    change a ⤳ b
67	    exact (subtype_specializes_iff a b).mpr h
68	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
69	  · -- Reverse direction: coheight z (in X) ≤ coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in ↥U).
70	    refine Order.coheight_le_iff'.mpr ?_
71	    intro p hphead
72	    -- Every p i lies in U: p.head ≤ p i in X means p i ⤳ z; combined with
73	    -- `z ∈ U` open, gives p i ∈ U via `Specializes.mem_open`.
74	    have hmem : ∀ i, p i ∈ U := by
75	      intro i
76	      have hle : z ≤ p i := by
77	        have := p.head_le i
78	        rwa [hphead] at this
79	      exact Specializes.mem_open (show p i ⤳ z from hle) hU hz
80	    -- Construct the LTSeries in ↥U.
81	    let q : LTSeries ↥U :=
82	      { length := p.length
83	        toFun := fun i => ⟨p i, hmem i⟩
84	        step := by
85	          intro i
86	          have hlt : p i.castSucc < p i.succ := p.step i
87	          have hspec : (p i.succ) ⤳ (p i.castSucc) := hlt.le
88	          have hsub : (⟨p i.succ, hmem _⟩ : ↥U) ⤳ ⟨p i.castSucc, hmem _⟩ :=
89	            (subtype_specializes_iff _ _).mpr hspec
90	          refine ⟨hsub, fun hbad => ?_⟩
91	          -- hbad : (⟨p i.castSucc, _⟩) ⤳ ⟨p i.succ, _⟩ in ↥U
92	          apply hlt.not_ge
93	          exact (subtype_specializes_iff _ _).mp hbad }
94	    have hqhead : q.head = ⟨z, hz⟩ := by
95	      apply Subtype.ext
96	      change p 0 = z
97	      exact hphead
98	    have hlen : q.length = p.length := rfl
99	    have hbound :=
100	      Order.length_le_coheight (x := (⟨z, hz⟩ : ↥U)) (p := q) (by rw [hqhead])
101	    simpa [hlen] using hbound
102	  · -- Forward direction: coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in ↥U) ≤ coheight z (in X).
103	    have h :=
104	      Order.coheight_le_coheight_apply_of_strictMono
105	        (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) hstrict ⟨z, hz⟩
106	    simpa using h
107	
108	end Order
109	
110	namespace Order
111	
112	/-- **The specialisation preorder on `Spec R` is the dual of the inclusion
113	preorder on `PrimeSpectrum R`.** Coheight in `Spec R` equals height in
114	`PrimeSpectrum R`. -/
115	lemma coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum
116	    {R : CommRingCat} (p : Spec R) :
117	    Order.coheight (α := Spec R) p
118	      = Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum R) ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ := by
119	  -- The order iso `Spec R ≃o (PrimeSpectrum R)ᵒᵈ` built from `spec_le_iff`.
120	  let e : Spec R ≃o (PrimeSpectrum R)ᵒᵈ :=
121	    { toFun := fun q => OrderDual.toDual ⟨q.asIdeal, q.isPrime⟩
122	      invFun := fun q => (OrderDual.ofDual q : PrimeSpectrum R)
123	      left_inv := fun _ => rfl
124	      right_inv := fun _ => rfl
125	      map_rel_iff' := by
126	        intro a b
127	        -- After unfolding the dual order, this becomes
128	        -- `b.asIdeal ≤ a.asIdeal ↔ a ≤ b in Spec R`, i.e. `spec_le_iff`.
129	        exact (AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.spec_le_iff R a b).symm }
130	  have h1 :
131	      Order.coheight (α := (PrimeSpectrum R)ᵒᵈ) (e p)
132	        = Order.coheight (α := Spec R) p :=
133	    Order.coheight_orderIso e p
134	  rw [← h1]
135	  -- `e p = OrderDual.toDual ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩`; reduce via
136	  -- `coheight (toDual x) = height x`.
137	  rfl
138	
139	end Order
140	
141	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
142	
143	namespace Scheme
144	
145	/-- **Coheight ↔ Krull dimension bridge for scheme points.** For any scheme
146	`X` and any point `z : X`, the Krull dimension of the stalk at `z` equals the
147	coheight of `z` in the underlying topological space (with the specialisation
148	preorder). -/
149	theorem ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight
150	    (X : Scheme.{u}) (z : X) :
151	    ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z) = Order.coheight z := by
152	  -- Step 1: pick an affine open U ∋ z.
153	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hzU, _⟩ :=
154	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := z) (U := ⊤)
155	      (by trivial)
156	  -- Step 2: name the prime corresponding to z inside U.
157	  set p : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U) := hU.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzU⟩ with hp
158	  -- Bind the section-to-stalk algebra explicitly so the localisation theorem
159	  -- sees the expected `Algebra` instance.
160	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, U) (X.presheaf.stalk z) :=
161	    TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨z, hzU⟩
162	  -- Step 3: stalk is the localisation of Γ(X,U) at p.
163	  haveI hloc : IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.presheaf.stalk z) p.asIdeal :=
164	    hU.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzU⟩
165	  -- Step 4: ringKrullDim stalk = p.asIdeal.height (= primeHeight p).
166	  have h4 :
167	      ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z)
168	        = (Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) p : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
169	    rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height
170	          (R := Γ(X, U)) p.asIdeal (X.presheaf.stalk z),
171	        Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight]
172	    rfl
173	  -- Step 5: relate height(p) to coheight(z) via Decls 1 + 2 and the
174	  -- homeomorphism `Spec Γ(X,U) ≃ U.toScheme` from `hU.isoSpec`.
175	  -- (5a) Decl 1: coheight z in X = coheight ⟨z, hzU⟩ in U.1 subspace.
176	  have h1' :
177	      @Order.coheight X (specializationPreorder X) z
178	        = @Order.coheight U.1 (specializationPreorder U.1) ⟨z, hzU⟩ :=
179	    Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding (X := X) (U := U.1) U.isOpen z hzU
180	  -- For a Scheme, the standard `Preorder` instance is definitionally
181	  -- `specializationPreorder`. Cast h1' to use the Scheme's preorder on the
182	  -- X side and U.toScheme's preorder on the U side (both defeq).
183	  have h1 :
184	      Order.coheight (α := X) z
185	        = Order.coheight (α := U.toScheme) ⟨z, hzU⟩ := h1'
186	  -- (5b) The scheme iso `hU.isoSpec : U.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(X,U)` is a Scheme
187	  -- iso; the underlying carrier-level map is a homeomorphism, giving an
188	  -- order iso of spec preorders.
189	  let hHomeo : U.toScheme ≃ₜ Spec Γ(X, U) :=
190	    TopCat.homeoOfIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso hU.isoSpec)
191	  let eOrder : U.toScheme ≃o Spec Γ(X, U) :=
192	    { toEquiv := hHomeo.toEquiv
193	      map_rel_iff' := by
194	        intro a b
195	        constructor
196	        · intro h
197	          have hsp : hHomeo b ⤳ hHomeo a := h
198	          have hsp' := hsp.map hHomeo.symm.continuous
199	          rw [hHomeo.symm_apply_apply, hHomeo.symm_apply_apply] at hsp'
200	          exact (hsp' : a ≤ b)
201	        · intro h
202	          have hsp : b ⤳ a := h
203	          exact (hsp.map hHomeo.continuous : hHomeo a ≤ hHomeo b) }
204	  have h2 : Order.coheight (α := U.toScheme) ⟨z, hzU⟩
205	      = Order.coheight (α := Spec Γ(X, U)) (eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩) :=
206	    (Order.coheight_orderIso eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩).symm
207	  have h3 : eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩ = p := rfl
208	  -- (5c) Decl 2: coheight (Spec R) p = height (PrimeSpectrum R) p.
209	  have h6 : Order.coheight (α := Spec Γ(X, U)) p
210	      = Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U))
211	          ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ :=
212	    Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum p
213	  have h7 : (⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) = p := rfl
214	  -- Assemble.
215	  rw [h4, h1, h2, h3, h6, h7]
216	
217	end Scheme
218	
219	end AlgebraicGeometry
220	
221	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
222	
223	namespace Scheme
224	
225	/-- **Codim-1 wrapper: coheight = 1 ⟹ stalk Krull dim ≤ 1.** Consumer-facing
226	specialisation of `ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` for the codim-`1` case used
227	in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` and `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. -/
228	lemma ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one
229	    (X : Scheme.{u}) (z : X) (hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
230	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk z) := by
231	  rw [Ring.krullDimLE_iff, ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight, hz]
232	  -- Goal: ((1 : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ((1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)
233	  norm_cast
234	
235	end Scheme
236	
237	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31	
32	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
35	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
36	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`
38	body. The body is iter-176+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
39	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (A.4.a) and
40	`Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b) landing their substantive
41	statements.
42	
43	The 1 pinned declaration is:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
47	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
48	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
49	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
50	
51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
58	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
59	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
60	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).
61	
62	## Abelian-variety conventions
63	
64	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
65	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
66	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
67	
68	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
69	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
70	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
71	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
72	
73	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
75	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
76	
77	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
78	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
79	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
80	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
81	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
82	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
83	
84	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
85	the call site.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
90	(250 LOC, 1 pin). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.2,
91	p. 17 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, PDF page 23).
92	-/

94	set_option autoImplicit false
95	
96	universe u
97	
98	open CategoryTheory Limits
99	
100	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
101	
102	namespace Scheme
103	
104	namespace RationalMap
105	
106	/-! ## Milne Theorem 3.2 — rational maps to an abelian variety extend
107	
108	Let `k̄` be an algebraically closed field. Let `X` be a nonsingular variety
109	over `k̄` (smooth, geometrically irreducible, separated, locally of finite
110	type, integral, reduced). Let `A` be an abelian variety over `k̄`
111	(a group object that is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible). For
112	every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` there is a *unique* regular morphism
113	`g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
114	
115	Milne's argument (`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1.4, Theorem 3.2):
116	let `U ⊆ X` be a maximal open of definition of `f` and `Z := X ∖ U`.
117	By the codim-$\geq 2$ extension theorem (Theorem 3.1, project-side
118	`thm:codim_one_extension`) the closed subset `Z` has codimension `≥ 2`.
119	By the pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy lemma for maps into a group variety
120	(Lemma 3.3, project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`), `Z` is either
121	empty or of pure codimension `1`. Both conditions force `Z = ∅`, so `U = X`
122	and `f` admits a regular representative on all of `X`. Uniqueness is the
123	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle (cf.
124	`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two regular morphisms `X ⟶ A` that
125	agree on a dense open of `X` agree everywhere.
126	
127	Blueprint reference: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne, *Abelian
128	Varieties*, Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17). The pin in the chapter is at
129	`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50.
130	
131	iter-176+ proof skeleton:
132	1. Pick a `PartialMap` representative `φ : X.PartialMap A` of `f` (using
133	   `Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`, which exists for `X` reduced and `A`
134	   separated; both hold from the abelian-variety + variety instances).
135	2. Show `φ.domain = ⊤` by combining `thm:codim_one_extension` (codim `≥ 2`
136	   complement) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (complement empty or
137	   pure codim `1`).
138	3. Read off `g := φ.hom` (the morphism on the full domain) and verify
139	   `g.toRationalMap = f`.
140	4. Uniqueness: two `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ A` with `g₁.toRationalMap = g₂.toRationalMap`
141	   agree on a dense open of `X`, so by
142	   `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` and reducedness of `X` they agree
143	   everywhere. -/
144	
145	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (1/2): integrality of the abelian variety.**
146	
147	For an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` —
148	encoded in the project as `A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying
149	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` —
150	the underlying scheme `A.left` is integral.
151	
152	**Derivation.** The proof has three steps:
153	
154	1. `Spec k̄` is a singleton scheme (Mathlib instance
155	   `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf` provides `Unique`,
156	   which yields both `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty`).
157	2. `GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom + Spec k̄ a single point ⟹ IrreducibleSpace A.left`
158	   via Mathlib's `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`.
159	3. `IrreducibleSpace + IsReduced ⟹ IsIntegral` via Mathlib's
160	   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.
161	
162	The substantive Mathlib gap is the implication `Smooth A.hom ⟹ IsReduced A.left`
163	(at the project's pinned commit, Mathlib's `Smooth` morphism class does not
164	ship a `IsReduced` consequence for the source over a reduced base). The
165	gap is isolated to the single `IsReduced A.left` hypothesis in the proof,
166	which is left as a `sorry`; all other steps close axiom-clean.
167	
168	When Mathlib ships the bridge (Stacks `034V` / `02G4` over a field
169	direction), the single `sorry` discharges and this helper becomes
170	axiom-clean automatically.
171	
172	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor of the iter-179 helper
173	`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, splitting its `IsIntegral A.left`
174	conjunct into a self-contained named lemma that does not depend on the
175	rational map `f`. -/
176	private theorem av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred
177	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
178	    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
179	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
180	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] :
181	    IsIntegral A.left := by
182	  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point (Mathlib instance
183	  -- `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`); both
184	  -- `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty` follow.
185	  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
186	  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
187	  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
188	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=
189	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton A.hom
190	  -- Step 3 (Mathlib gap): smoothness over a field forces reducedness of the
191	  -- source. This is Stacks `034V`/`02G4` over a (reduced) base, but the
192	  -- scheme-level packaging does not ship in Mathlib at the project's pinned
193	  -- commit.
194	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := sorry
195	  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced A.left
196	
197	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
198	Milne's two-input argument.**
199	
200	For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
201	abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
202	codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).
203	
204	**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
205	Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:
206	
207	1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
208	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
209	   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
210	   either empty or pure codim 1.
211	2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
212	   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
213	   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
214	   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.
215	
216	Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
217	indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
218	every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.
219	
220	**Status of the substantive content.** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
221	Milne 3.1 is not currently exposed by the project as a standalone lemma;
222	it is encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body (which
223	is itself `sorry` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`). Once the project
224	exposes a lemma `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` separating
225	out the codim-≥2 conclusion, this helper closes via the two-line
226	combination above. Until then it is left as a named `sorry` whose
227	docstring exhaustively records the combination strategy, replacing the
228	iter-179 conjunction-level `sorry` with a more targeted one.
229	
230	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
231	conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
232	substantive missing content localised here. -/
233	private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
234	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
235	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
236	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
237	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
238	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
239	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
240	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
241	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
242	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
243	    CodimOneFree f := by
244	  -- **Iter-182 structural refactor.** Materialise the variety-package
245	  -- instances on `A.left` needed by Milne Lemma 3.3
246	  -- (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): `IsIntegral` from the
247	  -- split helper `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` from
248	  -- `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
249	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`.
250	  haveI : IsIntegral A.left := av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A
251	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
252	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
253	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
254	  -- Apply Milne Lemma 3.3: the indeterminacy locus is empty or every point
255	  -- of it lies in the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
256	  have hdisj := indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f
257	  intro x hx
258	  rcases hdisj with hempty | hpure
259	  · -- **Branch 1 (CLOSED).** Empty indeterminacy locus: every point of `X`
260	    -- — in particular `x` — lies in `f.domain` (no codim-1 obstruction).
261	    by_contra hnotin
262	    have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
263	    rw [hempty] at hxZ
264	    exact hxZ.elim
265	  · -- **Branch 2 (PROJECT GAP).** The disjunct gives, for any `x ∈ Z(f)`,
266	    -- a codim-1 generic point `z` with `x ∈ closure {z}`. To prove
267	    -- `x ∈ f.domain` for our codim-1 `x`, contrapositive: assume
268	    -- `x ∈ Z(f)`. The disjunct produces a `z` with `coheight z = 1` and
269	    -- `x ∈ closure {z}` — but for a codim-1 `x`, sobriety of the scheme
270	    -- forces `z = x` (chains of length 1 starting at `z` and ending at a
271	    -- specialisation `x` of equal coheight collapse in a sober T₀ space),
272	    -- so `x = z` is a codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The contradiction needs
273	    -- the **codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1** (project-side
274	    -- `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s unbundled half), which is
275	    -- currently encapsulated inside that theorem's `sorry`'d body in
276	    -- `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` rather than exposed as a separate
277	    -- lemma. Once a lemma
278	    -- `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete : ∀ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f, coheight z ≥ 2`
279	    -- lands in `CodimOneExtension.lean`, branch 2 closes via:
280	    --   `by_contra hnotin; obtain ⟨z, hz1, hxz⟩ := hpure x hnotin;`
281	    --   `obtain rfl := sober_eq_of_specializes_of_coheight_eq hxz hx hz1;`
282	    --   `exact absurd hx (lt_irrefl 1 ∘ ... ∘ codimGe2 hnotin)`.
283	    sorry
284	
285	/-- **Iter-182 documentation helper: branch-1 of `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`.**
286	The empty-indeterminacy branch of Milne Lemma 3.3 closes `CodimOneFree`
287	without depending on the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1. Exposed as a
288	standalone lemma so the structural split survives any future refactor of
289	the parent helper. Axiom-clean. -/
290	private theorem codimOneFree_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty
291	    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f : X.RationalMap Y}
292	    (h : indeterminacyLocus f = ∅) : CodimOneFree f := by
293	  intro x _
294	  by_contra hnotin
295	  have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
296	  rw [h] at hxZ
297	  exact hxZ.elim
298	
299	/-- **Iter-179 Lane E + iter-180 Lane G: the two missing project inputs
300	for `extend_to_av`.**
301	
302	The conjunction of the two named iter-180 Lane G splits:
303	`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct, with
304	its `IsReduced A.left` Mathlib gap isolated) and
305	`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (the `CodimOneFree f` conjunct, with its
306	codim-≥2-from-Milne-3.1 gap isolated).
307	
308	This wrapper preserves the iter-179 call-site API (`extend_to_av` invokes
309	the conjunction in one `obtain`) while routing the proof to the two
310	narrower lemmas. The body itself is axiom-clean — all substantive content
311	is localised in the two split helpers above. -/
312	private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
313	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
314	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
315	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
316	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
317	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
318	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
319	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
320	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
321	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
322	    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
323	  ⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,
324	    av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy f⟩
325	
326	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
327	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
328	
329	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
330	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
331	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
332	
333	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
334	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
335	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
336	`J → A`.
337	
338	**Iter-179 Lane E body.** The proof is the standard one-line reduction of
339	Milne's two-line argument:
340	
341	1. Apply `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (Milne Theorem 3.1, project-side
342	   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a
343	   `CodimOneFree` rational map from a smooth integral variety to a complete
344	   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires
345	   the variety-package instances on `A.left` (separated, locally of finite
346	   type, integral, reduced) in addition to the four AV instances supplied
347	   by the caller.
348	
349	2. Discharge `CodimOneFree f` by invoking the helper
350	   `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, which combines
351	   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure
352	   codim 1 or empty indeterminacy for grpScheme targets) with the codim-≥2
353	   half of Milne 3.1. Both pieces are documented in that helper's
354	   docstring; the helper carries the single `sorry` that captures the
355	   remaining math content the project spec does not yet expose.
356	
357	3. Materialize the remaining variety-package instances on `A.left`:
358	   `IsSeparated A.hom` from `IsProper A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
359	   `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` from `Smooth A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
360	   `IsReduced A.left` from `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib instance
361	   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`).
362	
363	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
364	content is concentrated in the single named helper above. -/
365	theorem extend_to_av
366	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
367	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
368	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
369	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
370	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
371	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
372	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
373	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
374	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
375	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
376	  -- Step 3: materialise the missing variety-package instances on A.left.
377	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
378	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
379	  obtain ⟨hint, hcod⟩ := av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree f
380	  haveI := hint
381	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
382	  -- Steps 1–2: apply Milne Theorem 3.1 with the `CodimOneFree` discharged.
383	  exact extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod
384	
385	end RationalMap
386	
387	end Scheme
388	
389	end AlgebraicGeometry
390

12	/-!
13	# Genus-`0` base objects — re-export shim
14	
15	Iter-175 split this file into four sub-modules. All declarations are re-exported
16	from their new homes via `import` (Lean has no `export` ceremony for non-namespaced
17	decls — re-export is automatic from imports).
18	
19	* `BareScheme.lean` — `ProjectiveLineBar` scheme + the 2-chart affine cover.
20	* `ChartIso.lean`   — `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` chart-ring iso
21	                     + its load-bearing helpers.
22	* `Points.lean`     — `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`; `Ga`; `Gm`.
23	* `GmScaling.lean`  — chart-bridge + `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` action + product-stability
24	                     instances on `ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m`.
25	
26	Downstream files that `import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` see exactly the same
27	surface as before the split.
28	-/

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.

4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 1): the bare scheme `ProjectiveLineBar` and its 2-chart cover
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 1** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
13	
14	* the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`;
15	* the `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f`;
16	* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
17	  `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`;
18	* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
19	* the scaffold `GeometricallyIrreducible` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instances;
20	* the 2-chart affine open cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`.
21	
22	Downstream strata: `ChartIso` (chart-ring iso), `Points` (k̄-points + `Ga` + `Gm`),
23	`GmScaling` (chart-bridge + `σ_×`).
24	-/
25	
26	set_option autoImplicit false
27	set_option linter.style.setOption false
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
32	
33	noncomputable section
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
38	
39	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
40	
41	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
42	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
43	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
44	
45	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
49	
50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
51	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
52	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
59	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
60	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
61	    Algebra kbar
62	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
63	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
152	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
153	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
154	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
155	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
156	  sorry
157	
158	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
159	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
160	acceptable for iter-165). -/
161	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
162	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
163	  sorry
164	
165	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
166	
167	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
168	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
169	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
170	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
171	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
172	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
173	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
174	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
175	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
176	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
177	  fun i => by
178	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
179	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
180	
181	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
182	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
183	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
184	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
185	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
186	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
187	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
188	
189	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
190	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
191	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
192	
193	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
194	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
195	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
196	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
197	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
198	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
199	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
200	    (m := ![1, 1])
201	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
202	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
203	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
204	    (by
205	      classical
206	      intro p hp
207	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
208	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
209	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
210	        have := hp
211	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
212	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
213	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
214	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
215	        exact this
216	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
217	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
218	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
219	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
220	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
221	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
222	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
223	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
224	        by_contra h
225	        push Not at h
226	        apply hd_ne
227	        ext k
228	        fin_cases k
229	        · simpa using h.1
230	        · simpa using h.2
231	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
232	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
233	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
234	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
235	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
236	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
237	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
238	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
239	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
240	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
241	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
242	
243	end AlgebraicGeometry
244	
245	end

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	
8	/-!
9	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 2): the chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`
10	
11	This file is **Stratum 2** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
12	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships the
13	load-bearing chart-ring iso
14	
15	```
16	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i)
17	  ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar
18	```
19	
20	together with the `kbar`-algebra preservation lemma. The forward direction is via
21	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`; the inverse via
22	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i = isLocalizationElem`.
23	
24	Upstream stratum: `BareScheme`. Downstream strata: `Points`, `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The chart-ring iso: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]` -/
39	
40	/-- **The "other" `Fin 2` index** used in the chart-`i` affine coordinate `X (other i) / X i`. -/
41	def otherFin : Fin 2 → Fin 2
42	  | 0 => 1
43	  | 1 => 0
44	
45	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_zero : otherFin 0 = 1 := rfl
46	@[simp] private lemma otherFin_one : otherFin 1 = 0 := rfl
47	
48	private lemma otherFin_ne (i : Fin 2) : otherFin i ≠ i := by
49	  fin_cases i <;> decide
50	
51	/-- **The chart-`i` evaluation `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ →+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄`**: sends
52	`X i ↦ 1` and `X (otherFin i) ↦ X ()`. -/
53	private noncomputable def chartEvalRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
54	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
55	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))
56	    (fun j : Fin 2 => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) else MvPolynomial.X ())
57	
58	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_self (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
59	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X i) = 1 := by
60	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
61	
62	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_X_other (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
63	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i)) = MvPolynomial.X () := by
64	  unfold chartEvalRingHom
65	  rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
66	  exact if_neg (otherFin_ne i)
67	
68	@[simp] private lemma chartEvalRingHom_C (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) (r : kbar) :
69	    chartEvalRingHom kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) = MvPolynomial.C r := by
70	  simp [chartEvalRingHom]
71	
72	/-- **The forward direction of the chart-ring iso**: `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* k̄[u]` via
73	`Localization.awayLift` from the chart evaluation `X i ↦ 1`. -/
74	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
75	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
76	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) →+*
77	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
78	  (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
79	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
80	      (by rw [chartEvalRingHom_X_self]; exact isUnit_one)).comp
81	    (algebraMap (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
82	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
83	      (Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)))
84	
85	/-- **The base ring map `k̄ →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)`** — the composite
86	`k̄ → 𝒜 0 → Away 𝒜 (X i)` of the algebra map into degree-`0` with `fromZeroRingHom`. -/
87	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
88	    kbar →+*
89	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
90	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
91	  (HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
92	    (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))).comp
93	    (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
94	
95	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
96	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
97	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
98	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
99	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
100	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
101	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
102	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
103	    (fun _ : Unit =>
104	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
105	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
106	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))
107	
108	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
109	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
110	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
111	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
112	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
113	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
114	  · intro r
115	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
116	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
117	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
118	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
119	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
120	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
121	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
122	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
123	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
124	      MvPolynomial.C r
125	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
126	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
127	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
128	  · intro _
129	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
130	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
131	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
132	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
133	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
134	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
135	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
136	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
137	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
138	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
139	      MvPolynomial.X ()
140	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
141	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
142	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]
143	
144	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
145	
146	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
147	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
148	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
149	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
150	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
151	
152	Proof structure (iter-172):
153	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
154	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
155	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
156	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
157	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
158	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
159	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
160	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
161	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
162	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
163	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
164	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
167	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
168	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
169	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
170	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
171	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
172	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
173	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
174	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
175	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
176	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
177	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
178	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
179	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
180	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
181	    apply top_unique
182	    intro p _
183	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
184	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
185	      intro r
186	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
187	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
188	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
189	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
190	      rw [← h]
191	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
192	    · -- add case
193	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
194	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂
195	    · -- mul_X case
196	      intro p₁ j hp₁
197	      refine Subalgebra.mul_mem _ hp₁ (Algebra.subset_adjoin ⟨j, ?_⟩)
198	      fin_cases j <;> simp [v]
199	  -- Step 2: Apply the Mathlib theorem.
200	  have htop := HomogeneousLocalization.Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top hfi (ι' := Fin 2)
201	    v hx dv hxd
202	  -- Key intermediate: surjectivity of `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)` (constants → degree-0 piece).
203	  -- Used for the `algebraMap` case of the adjoin-induction below.
204	  have hkbar_sur : Function.Surjective
205	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar) 0)) := by
206	    rintro ⟨v, hv⟩
207	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
208	    apply Subtype.ext
209	    rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
210	    have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
211	    simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
212	    exact key
213	  -- Helper for the `mem` case: each generator equals `isLocalizationElem^k` for some `k`.
214	  -- The numerator `X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁` of degree `a = a₀ + a₁`, denominator `X i^a`.
215	  -- After simplification: this equals `(X (otherFin i) / X i)^(a_{otherFin i})`
216	  -- where `k = a₁` if `i = 0` and `k = a₀` if `i = 1`.
217	  have gen_eq_pow : ∀ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
218	      (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1)
219	      (hP : (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (a • 1)),
220	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) hP =
221	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi)^(ai (otherFin i)) := by
222	    intro a ai hai _hai_le hP
223	    apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
224	    have ha_eq : ai 0 + ai 1 = a := by
225	      have h := hai
226	      simp only [Fin.sum_univ_two, smul_eq_mul, dv, Matrix.cons_val_zero,
227	        Matrix.cons_val_one] at h
228	      omega
229	    -- Step A: compute LHS `.val` via `Away.val_mk`.
230	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
231	    -- Step B: compute RHS via `val_pow` + `Away.val_mk`. Use explicit `change` to make the
232	    -- isLocalizationElem definitionally visible.
233	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_pow]
234	    change _ = (HomogeneousLocalization.val (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
235	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi 1
236	        (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) ^ 1) _))^(ai (otherFin i))
237	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_pow]
238	    simp only [pow_one]
239	    -- Now both sides are `Localization.mk`. Reduce via `mk_eq_mk_iff` + `r_iff_exists`.
240	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
241	    refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
242	    -- Goal: 1 * (∏ j, v j ^ ai j) * ↑(⟨X i, _⟩^(ai (otherFin i))) =
243	    --       1 * X (otherFin i)^(ai (otherFin i)) * X i^a
244	    -- Use that `↑(⟨X i, _⟩^k) = X i^k` (defeq via `SubmonoidClass.coe_pow` + Subtype.val).
245	    -- Case-split via `Fin.ext + omega` to get clean `0`/`1` for `i`.
246	    have hi_val : i.val = 0 ∨ i.val = 1 := by omega
247	    rcases hi_val with hi | hi
248	    · -- i = 0, otherFin 0 = 1
249	      have heq_i : i = (0 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
250	      subst heq_i
251	      simp only [otherFin_zero, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
252	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
253	      -- Goal: X 0^(ai 1) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 0^a * X 1^(ai 1)
254	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
255	    · -- i = 1, otherFin 1 = 0
256	      have heq_i : i = (1 : Fin 2) := Fin.ext hi
257	      subst heq_i
258	      simp only [otherFin_one, Fin.prod_univ_two, Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
259	        v, OneMemClass.coe_one, _root_.one_mul, SubmonoidClass.coe_pow]
260	      -- Goal: X 1^(ai 0) * (X 0^(ai 0) * X 1^(ai 1)) = X 1^a * X 0^(ai 0)
261	      rw [show a = ai 0 + ai 1 from ha_eq.symm, pow_add]; ring
262	  -- Step 4: surjectivity. Every y is in `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {generators}` = ⊤.
263	  intro y
264	  have hy_in : y ∈ Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
265	      { x | ∃ (a : ℕ) (ai : Fin 2 → ℕ)
266	          (hai : ∑ j, ai j • dv j = a • 1) (_ : ∀ j, ai j ≤ 1),
267	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) hfi a
268	          (∏ j, v j ^ ai j)
269	          (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i) = x } := by
270	    rw [htop]; trivial
271	  refine Algebra.adjoin_induction
272	      (p := fun y _ => y ∈ Set.range (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i))
273	      ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ hy_in
274	  · -- mem case
275	    rintro x ⟨a, ai, hai, hai_le, rfl⟩
276	    have hgen :=
277	      gen_eq_pow a ai hai hai_le (hai ▸ SetLike.prod_pow_mem_graded _ _ _ _ fun i _ ↦ hxd i)
278	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.X ()^(ai (otherFin i)), ?_⟩
279	    rw [hgen]
280	    have hX : (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.X ()) =
281	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem hfi hgi := by
282	      change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()) = _
283	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
284	    rw [map_pow, hX]
285	  · -- algebraMap case
286	    intro r
287	    -- r : ↥(𝒜 0). Find `r₀ ∈ kbar` with `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀ = r`.
288	    obtain ⟨r₀, hr₀⟩ := hkbar_sur r
289	    refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C r₀, ?_⟩
290	    -- Goal: mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r₀) = algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r
291	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.C r₀) = _
292	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
293	    change kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r₀ = _
294	    simp only [kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply]
295	    rw [hr₀]
296	    rfl
297	  · -- add case
298	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
299	    exact ⟨pu + pw, by rw [map_add, hpu, hpw]⟩
300	  · -- mul case
301	    rintro u w _ _ ⟨pu, hpu⟩ ⟨pw, hpw⟩
302	    exact ⟨pu * pw, by rw [map_mul, hpu, hpw]⟩
303	
304	/-- Round-trip on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`: `inverse ∘ forward = id`.
305	
306	Closed by the "cancel surjective" route per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q2: from
307	`mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective` (surjectivity of `inverse`) +
308	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right` (`forward ∘ inverse = id` on `MvPoly Unit kbar`),
309	conclude `inverse ∘ forward = id` on `Away 𝒜 (X i)`. The cancellation step itself is
310	mechanical; the only remaining substance is the surjectivity helper above. -/
311	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
312	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i).comp
313	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i) =
314	      RingHom.id _ := by
315	  ext x
316	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective kbar i x
317	  -- Goal: ((mvPoly←Away) ∘ (Away→mvPoly)) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = (mvPoly←Away) p
318	  -- The inner `(Away→mvPoly) ((mvPoly←Away) p) = p` by aux_right; the result follows.
319	  have h : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
320	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) p) = p :=
321	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) p
322	  simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply, h]
323	
324	/-- **The chart-ring iso `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≃+* k̄[u]`** — built from the forward map (via
325	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
326	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
327	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
328	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
329	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
330	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
331	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
332	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
333	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
334	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
335	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
336	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
337	
338	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
339	helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
340	`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
341	`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).
342	
343	Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
344	algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
345	`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
346	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
347	lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
348	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
350	        (algebraMap kbar
351	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
354	  ext r
355	  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
356	  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
357	  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
358	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
359	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
360	  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
361	      algebraMap kbar
362	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
363	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
364	    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
365	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
366	    rfl
367	  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
368	  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
369	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
370	      MvPolynomial.C r :=
371	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
372	  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
373	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
374	    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
375	  rw [← hinv, hround]
376	  rfl
377	
378	end AlgebraicGeometry
379	
380	end

10	/-!
11	# Cross01 substrate — Lane B (𝔾ₘ-scaling) reusable lemmas
12	
13	This file ships **substrate** lemmas needed to close the Lane B
14	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` cocycle proof (path (III.c) of
15	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`) together with two off-target sorries
16	(`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`) in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`.
17	
18	The substrates are generic in the geometric situation and live in their own
19	file rather than inline so they can be re-used by the three GmScaling consumer
20	applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.  Their construction
21	follows the recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` (§2) and the prover-facing
22	specification at `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`.
23	
24	## Contents (this iter)
25	
26	* `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` —
27	  Substrate 1 (iter-189, axiom-clean): the closed-immersion universal
28	  property phrased as a topological range containment (under reduced
29	  source / target hypotheses).

32	  Substrate 2 (iter-190): the tensor

34	  `Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` per
35	  `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3.

38	set_option autoImplicit false

41	set_option linter.unusedSimpArgs false
42	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

44	universe u

46	open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Topology

50	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
