82	/-! ## §1. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor -/
83	
84	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
85	
86	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
87	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
88	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
89	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
90	
91	/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth
92	proper curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144, definition of `ℒ(D)`).
93	
94	For a Weil divisor `D = Σ_Q n_Q · [Q] ∈ Div(C)` on a smooth proper
95	geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the invertible sheaf
96	`𝒪_C(D) := ℒ(D)` is the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant
97	sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` (Hartshorne Proposition 6.15: on an integral scheme
98	the sheaf of total quotient rings is the constant sheaf at the function
99	field) defined section-wise on each open `U ⊆ C` as
100	`Γ(U, 𝒪_C(D)) = { f ∈ K(C) | f = 0 ∨ ord_Q(f) ≥ −n_Q ∀ prime divisor Q
101	∈ U }`, with restriction along `V ⊆ U` given by the identity on `K(C)`.
102	
103	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
104	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf carrier used
105	by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so that `H⁰` and
106	`H¹` of `𝒪_C(D)` are accessible via
107	`Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf D) 0/1`).
108	
109	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The iter-184+
110	body recipe (per chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` §"Sheaf-property
111	correctness"): per-open `Submodule kbar K(C)` cut out by the order
112	conditions (the closure proofs reduce to `Ring.ordFrac`-multiplicativity
113	and the non-archimedean inequality of the DVR valuation at each prime
114	divisor); presheaf functor via the identity-on-`K(C)` restriction; sheaf
115	property via gluing-by-stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at
116	each prime divisor). The construction parallels the project's existing
117	`Scheme.toModuleKPresheaf` / `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` template in
118	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/`.
119	
120	**Coordination with `RRFormula.lean:168`.** The iter-174 typed-`sorry`
121	placeholder `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` in `RRFormula.lean` is
122	slated to be retired (Lane H) by re-export of this declaration. Both
123	files compile in isolation; cross-imports are coordinated by Lane H.
124	
125	Blueprint reference: `def:sheafOf` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144;
126	Propositions 6.13, 6.15; Remark 6.17.1).
127	
128	**iter-185 Lane K body fragment.** The case `D = 0` is implemented on the
129	nose as the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (Hartshorne's
130	``\(\mathcal O_C(0) = \mathcal O_C\)'' identification; chapter
131	`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `lem:sheafOf_zero`). The general case
132	remains an honest typed `sorry` pending the iter-186+ Hartshorne
133	subsheaf-of-`K_C` construction described in the docstring above. The
134	`if`-branching uses `Classical.dec` (via `open Classical in`) on the
135	`WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)` carrier, which has no canonical
136	decidable equality. -/
137	noncomputable def sheafOf (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
138	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
139	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
140	  open Classical in
141	  if D = 0 then Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C else sorry
142	
143	/-! ## §2. Immediate corollaries -/
144	
145	/-- **Sheaf of the zero divisor is the structure sheaf**
146	(chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `sheafOf_zero`).
147	
148	At `D = 0` the coefficient `n_Q = 0` at every prime divisor `Q`, so the
149	section condition of `sheafOf` reduces to the standard
150	"non-negative-order = regular" identification of the structure sheaf on
151	an integral scheme (Hartshorne II §6 immediately before Proposition 6.11).
152	Both sides are sub-`𝒪_C`-modules of `𝒦_C` with identity restriction
153	maps, so the equality of presheaves promotes to an equality of sheaves
154	of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules.
155	
156	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The body closes
157	on iter-184+ closure of `sheafOf` together with the standard `Γ(U, 𝒪_C)`
158	identification.
159	
160	**iter-185 Lane K body**: closes via the explicit `D = 0` branch in the
161	`sheafOf` def above (modified iter-185 to land the structure-sheaf value
162	on the `0` divisor on the nose). Unfolding `sheafOf` exposes the
163	`if 0 = 0 then toModuleKSheaf C else sorry` and `if_pos rfl` picks the
164	true branch.
165	
166	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_zero`. -/
167	lemma sheafOf_zero :
168	    sheafOf (C := C) (0 : C.left.WeilDivisor) = Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C := by
169	  unfold sheafOf
170	  exact if_pos rfl
171	
172	/-- **Sheaf at a single closed point is the line bundle of `RR.3`**
173	(chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `sheafOf_singlePoint`).
174	
175	For a closed point `P ∈ C` viewed as a Weil divisor `[P] ∈ Div(C)` via
176	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint`, the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C([P])` of
177	`sheafOf` agrees on the nose with the closed-point line bundle
178	`lineBundleAtClosedPoint P` of `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`).
179	
180	The agreement is by unfolding both definitions: at `D = [P]`, the order
181	conditions of `sheafOf` (`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P`, `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`)
182	are exactly those characterising sections of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint P`
183	(via `lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff` of `OCofP.lean`).
184	
185	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. Closes on
186	iter-184+ closure of both `sheafOf` and `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`.
187	
188	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_singlePoint`. -/
189	lemma sheafOf_singlePoint [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
190	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
191	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
192	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
193	    sheafOf (C := C) (ofClosedPoint P hP) =
194	      lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh := by
195	  sorry
196	
197	/-- **Short exact sequence for `D ↝ D + [P]`** (Hartshorne IV.1.3
198	inductive step, p. 296; chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma
199	`sheafOf_ses_single_add`).
200	
201	For a Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)` and a prime divisor `P` of `C`
202	(equivalently, a closed point on a smooth curve), the inclusion
203	`𝒪_C(D) ↪ 𝒪_C(D + [P])` of sub-`𝒪_C`-modules of `𝒦_C` fits into a short
204	exact sequence
205	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`
206	in `Sh(C, 𝐌𝐨𝐝_k̄)`, where `k(P)` is the skyscraper sheaf at `P` with
207	stalk `k̄`.
208	
209	The statement bundles the existence of a `ShortComplex` whose `X₁ ↦ X₂`
210	arm matches `sheafOf D ↪ sheafOf (Finsupp.single P 1 + D)` and that is
211	`ShortExact` (mono + epi + exact). The third term `X₃` is the
212	skyscraper `k(P)` — encoded here as a (nonempty) isomorphism class with
213	`Mathlib`'s `skyscraperSheaf` (decidability of `P.point ∈ U` supplied
214	classically). Lane H's `RRFormula` consumes this sequence via
215	χ-additivity to derive `χ(𝒪_C(D + [P])) = χ(𝒪_C(D)) + 1`.
216	
217	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The iter-184+
218	body recipe (per chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` §"Immediate
219	corollaries", Beat 1 + Beat 2): build the ideal-sheaf SES
220	`0 → 𝒪_C(−[P]) → 𝒪_C → k(P) → 0`, tensor with `𝒪_C(D + [P])` (which is
221	locally free of rank 1, hence the tensor is exact), and identify the
222	three terms via `𝒪_C(−[P]) ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ 𝒪_C(D)` (Hartshorne 6.13(b)),
223	`𝒪_C ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ 𝒪_C(D + [P])` (tensor unit), and
224	`k(P) ⊗ 𝒪_C(D + [P]) ≅ k(P)` (rank-1 stalk at `P`).
225	
226	The argument `D` carries the underlying `Finsupp` type rather than
227	`Scheme.WeilDivisor` so that the `Finsupp.single P 1 + D` term elaborates
228	cleanly (the addition is in the unambiguous Finsupp instance); the result
229	is consumed via the definitional equality `WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀
230	ℤ)`, matching the pattern of `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add`
231	in `RRFormula.lean`.
232	
233	Blueprint reference: `lem:sheafOf_ses_single_add` (Hartshorne IV.1, p. 296). -/
234	theorem sheafOf_ses_single_add
235	    (D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor) :
236	    ∃ S : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
237	        (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
238	          (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)),
239	      S.ShortExact ∧
240	      S.X₁ = sheafOf (C := C) D ∧
241	      S.X₂ = sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single P 1 + D) ∧
242	      Nonempty (S.X₃ ≅ skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar)
243	        P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) := by
244	  sorry
245	
246	end Scheme.WeilDivisor

248	end AlgebraicGeometry

11	# The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point and its global sections (RR.3)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.3** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)` and the
22	  degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and the Riemann–Roch dimension formula
25	  `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0`.
26	- **`RR.3` (this file)**: the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point
27	  `P ∈ C`, its `k̄`-vector space of global sections as the
28	  Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`, the `H¹`-vanishing
29	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a genus-`0` curve, the dimension formula
30	  `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`, and the existence of a non-constant
31	  rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with at most a simple pole at `P`.
32	- `RR.4` (`RationalCurveIso.lean`, future): the "two-section
33	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.

110	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
111	
112	/-! ## §1. The line bundle of a closed point on a smooth proper curve
113	
114	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` and a closed
115	point `P ∈ C`, the local ring `𝒪_{C,P}` is a DVR with maximal ideal
116	generated by a uniformiser `f_P`. Hartshorne's construction `ℒ(D)`
117	(II §6 p. 144) applied to the Cartier divisor `[P]` (locally cut out by
118	`f_P` near `P`, by `1` elsewhere) produces the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`:
119	the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)`
120	generated locally by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on `C \ {P}`. -/
121	
122	namespace Scheme
123	
124	/-! ### Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier (iter-183 Lane A scaffold)
125	
126	The substantive iter-183 contribution: a concrete per-open `Set`-valued
127	carrier of the line bundle `𝒪_C(P)`, realised directly as Hartshorne's
128	subsheaf of the function-field constant sheaf `K_C` (Hartshorne II §6
129	p. 144; analogist `ocofp-sheaf-internalhom.md` Decision 3
130	`ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB`).
131	
132	A section of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U` is a rational function `f ∈ K(C)`
133	satisfying the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
134	`Q ≠ P` with `Q.point ∈ U` (regularity on the complement of `P` inside
135	`U`) and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
136	The construction is independent of the choice of uniformiser at `P`:
137	any two uniformisers differ by a unit, so the order-`≥ −1` condition at
138	`P` is intrinsic.
139	
140	iter-184+ will upgrade `carrierSet` to a `Submodule kbar K(C)` via the
141	closure proofs (zero / addition / kbar-scalar), bundle the result as a
142	presheaf functor (identity-on-`K(C)` restrictions, monotone in `U` via
143	`carrierSet_mono`), and discharge the sheaf property via gluing-by-
144	stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at each prime divisor). -/
145	
146	/-- **Carrier set** of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U : (Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`: the
147	set of rational functions `f ∈ K(C)` satisfying the order conditions
148	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q` with `Q.point ∈ U.unop`,
149	`Q.point ≠ P`, and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U.unop`.
150	
151	iter-183 Lane A landed this as a concrete substantive `Set` definition;
152	no sorry. -/
153	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet
154	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
155	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
156	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
157	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
158	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
159	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
160	    Set C.left.functionField := by
161	  let Phat : C.left.PrimeDivisor := ⟨P, hPcoh⟩
162	  haveI := Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
163	    (X := C.left) Phat
164	  exact { f | (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ∈ U.unop.1 → Q.point ≠ P →
165	              0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
166	              (P ∈ U.unop.1 → (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Phat f) }
167	
168	/-- **Monotonicity of `carrierSet` in `U`**: when `V.unop ⊆ U.unop` (i.e.
169	the open `V` is contained in the open `U`), the carrier on `U` is
170	INCLUDED in the carrier on `V` (the order conditions over the smaller
171	open `V` involve fewer prime divisors, hence are easier to satisfy).
172	
173	This is the substantive monotonicity that drives the (identity-on-`K(C)`)
174	restriction map of the would-be `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.presheaf` functor:
175	in `(Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`, an arrow `U ⟶ V` corresponds to `V.unop ⊆ U.unop`,
176	and the restriction map `carrierSet U → carrierSet V` is the inclusion
177	delivered by this lemma.
178	
179	iter-183 Lane A landed this as the substantive monotonicity proof; no
180	sorry. -/
181	private lemma lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet_mono
182	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
183	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
184	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
185	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
186	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
187	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ}
188	    (hUV : V.unop.1 ⊆ U.unop.1) :
189	    lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh U
190	      ⊆ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh V := by
191	  intro f hf
192	  refine ⟨fun Q hQV hQP => hf.1 Q (hUV hQV) hQP, fun hPV => hf.2 (hUV hPV)⟩
193	
194	/-- **Nonempty top-open**: for an integral scheme `X`, the top open is
195	nonempty (its carrier is `Set.univ` and `X` itself is nonempty since
196	`IsIntegral X ⟹ IrreducibleSpace X ⟹ Nonempty X`). Supplied as an
197	instance so that the standard Mathlib `Algebra Γ(X, U) X.functionField`
198	instance fires at `U = ⊤`. -/
199	instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instNonemptyTopOpen
200	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] :
201	    Nonempty ((⊤ : X.Opens) : Scheme) :=
202	  (Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff (U := (⊤ : X.Opens))).mpr
203	    ⟨(inferInstance : Nonempty X).some, Set.mem_univ _⟩
204	
205	/-- **`kbar`-algebra structure on the function field `K(C)`**. The
206	project's `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraSection` instance gives
207	`Algebra kbar Γ(C.left, ⊤)`, and Mathlib's standard
208	`AlgebraicGeometry.instAlgebraCarrierObjOppositeOpens...` gives
209	`Algebra Γ(C.left, ⊤) K(C)` via `germToFunctionField`. Composing the
210	two algebra maps via `RingHom.toAlgebra` produces the desired
211	`Algebra kbar K(C)` instance, which is needed for `Submodule kbar K(C)`
212	to type-check. -/
213	noncomputable instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instAlgebraKbarFunctionField
214	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
215	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsIntegral C.left] :
216	    Algebra kbar C.left.functionField := by
217	  haveI : Nonempty (⊤ : C.left.Opens) :=
218	    (AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff
219	      (X := C.left) (U := (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).mpr

249	    -- both `0 ≤ 0` and `-1 ≤ 0` hold trivially.
250	    refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩

265	      intro Q n hn haₚ hbₚ
266	      by_cases hab : a + b = 0
267	      · simpa [hab, Scheme.RationalMap.order] using hn
268	      by_cases hae : a = 0

283	        · rw [heq]; exact (WithZero.log_le_log hob hoab).mpr (heq ▸ hmin)

287	        · rw [heq]; exact haₚ
288	        · rw [heq]; exact hbₚ

291	      fun hPU => key ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ (-1) (by norm_num) (ha₂ hPU) (hb₂ hPU)⟩

314	      intro Q y hy; simp [hy]

316	        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q
317	              (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) := by
318	      intro Q
319	      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
320	      let β : R := (C.left.presheaf.germ (⊤ : C.left.Opens) Q.point trivial).hom
321	        ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C
322	            (Opposite.op (⊤ : C.left.Opens))).hom c)
323	      have hα_eq : (algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c) =

326	            ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C

338	        rw [hα_eq]; exact Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero hβne

419	/-- **Type-level Subfunctor presentation of the carrier** (iter-189 Subfunctor
420	restructure). The carrier of `𝒪_C(P)` viewed as a `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor` of
421	the Type-valued presheaf `TopCat.presheafToType C.left.toTopCat C.left.functionField`

423	an open `U` is a function `g : U.unop → K(C)` that is constant with value
424	`f ∈ carrierSubmoduleSheaf U`.

427	- At `U ≠ ⊥`: a section is a constant function valued in
428	  `carrierSubmoduleSheaf U = carrierSubmodule U` (so the order conditions hold).
429	- At `U = ⊥`: the unique empty function (witnessed by `f = 0 ∈ ⊥`).

432	sheaf `TopCat.Presheaf.toType_isSheaf` for `presheafToType`, reduces the sheaf
433	condition for the (type-valued shadow of the) carrier to a stalk-locality check:
434	every section of `presheafToType K(C)` whose sieve-of-section is covering already
435	lies in the Subfunctor. On the irreducible scheme `C.left`, this stalk-locality
436	holds because any two non-empty opens intersect, forcing constant-function gluing

438	each open in the cover to their union pointwise. -/

442	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]

444	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
445	    CategoryTheory.Subfunctor

447	  obj U := { g : U.unop → C.left.functionField |
448	    ∃ f : C.left.functionField,

453	    intro U V i g hg

590	  -- `V = ⊥`. This makes `F(op ⊥) = ↥⊥` a singleton, which is what the
591	  -- empty-cover case of the sheaf condition requires.
592	  have htrivBot : ∀ (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (hV : V = ⊥),
593	      lineBundleAtClosedPoint.trivAtBot (C := C) (Opposite.op V) = ⊥ := by
594	    intro V hV
595	    apply Submodule.ext
596	    intro f
597	    constructor
598	    · rintro (h | h)
599	      · exact (h hV).elim
600	      · exact h
601	    · intro h
602	      change V ≠ ⊥ ∨ f = 0
603	      exact Or.inr h

606	    intro V hV

608	      lineBundleAtClosedPoint.trivAtBot _ = ⊥
609	    rw [htrivBot V hV, inf_bot_eq]

611	      (s : ↥(lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh
612	              (Opposite.op V))),
613	      s.1 = 0 := by
614	    intro V hV s
615	    have h0 : s.1 ∈ (⊥ : Submodule kbar C.left.functionField) := by
616	      rw [← hcsBot V hV]; exact s.2
617	    exact (Submodule.mem_bot kbar).mp h0

620	  · -- Case A: empty cover.
621	    have hUi : ∀ i, U i = (⊥ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) := by
622	      intro i
623	      apply le_antisymm _ bot_le
624	      calc U i ≤ iSup U := le_iSup U i
625	        _ = ⊥ := hSup

627	    · exact (lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh _).zero_mem
628	    · -- IsGluing: for each i, since U i = ⊥, both sides are 0.
629	      intro i

631	      have hsfi : (sf i).1 = 0 := hSubAt0 (U i) (hUi i) (sf i)
632	      -- The image `(F.map _).hom ⟨0, _⟩` also has `.1 = 0` (it lives in

635	      rw [hsfi]
636	      -- Both sides should now be `0` after extracting `.1`.

638	        (homOfLE (le_iSup U i)).op).hom ⟨0, _⟩).1 = 0

640	        _

642	      apply Subtype.ext
643	      exact (hSubAt0 _ hSup s').trans rfl

645	    --
646	    -- Substrate: `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierTypeSubfunctor P hPcoh`, a
647	    -- `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor` of

649	    -- over `op (U i)` are constant `↥(U i) → K(C)` functions with value in
650	    -- `carrierSubmoduleSheaf (op (U i))`. The lift of `(sf i).1` to such a section

653	    --
654	    -- Each `(sf i).1` lifts to a constant-function section of the Subfunctor over

657	    -- (compatibility in `carrierPresheaf`) implies pointwise equality of the
658	    -- constant values on overlaps.
659	    have hsub_mem : ∀ i,
660	        (fun (_ : ↑↑(U i)) => ((sf i).1 : C.left.functionField))
661	          ∈ (lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierTypeSubfunctor P hPcoh).obj
662	              (Opposite.op (U i)) := fun i => ⟨(sf i).1, (sf i).2, rfl⟩
663	    -- Strategy for the iter-190+ prover close (single typed sorry below):
664	    --
665	    --   1. Apply `CategoryTheory.Subfunctor.isSheaf_iff` against
666	    --      `TopCat.Presheaf.toType_isSheaf` to glue the family `hsub_mem`
667	    --      (after compatibility-promoting through the Subfunctor-section structure)
668	    --      to a section `g : ↥(iSup U) → K(C)` in
669	    --      `carrierTypeSubfunctor.obj (op (iSup U))`. Stalk-locality holds by
670	    --      irreducibility of `C.left.toTopCat`: any two non-empty opens of an
671	    --      irreducible space intersect, forcing all the `(sf i).1` to agree on a
672	    --      common value `v ∈ K(C)`, and `v` lies in `carrierSubmoduleSheaf
673	    --      (op (iSup U))` by pointwise transfer of the per-prime-divisor order
674	    --      conditions (`Q.point ∈ iSup U ⇒ ∃ i, Q.point ∈ U i` via
675	    --      `TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup`).

677	    --      the gluing is `⟨v, hv⟩` in `↥(carrierSubmoduleSheaf (op (iSup U)))`.
678	    --
679	    -- Helper: when target is non-bot, F.map is Submodule.inclusion (preserves .1).
680	    have map_val : ∀ {Uo Vo : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ}
681	        (g : Uo ⟶ Vo) (hV : Vo.unop ≠ ⊥)
682	        (x : ↥(lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh Uo)),
683	        (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map g).hom x).1 = x.1 := by
684	      intros Uo Vo g hV x
685	      simp only [lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf, dif_neg hV,
686	        ModuleCat.hom_ofHom]
687	      rfl
688	    -- Get a non-empty witness index since iSup U ≠ ⊥.
689	    have hexists_ne_bot : ∃ i, U i ≠ ⊥ := by
690	      by_contra h
691	      exact hSup (iSup_eq_bot.mpr (fun i => not_not.mp (not_exists.mp h i)))
692	    obtain ⟨i₀, hUi₀⟩ := hexists_ne_bot

695	    -- Irreducibility gives non-empty intersection for non-empty pair.

697	    have hPre : PreirreducibleSpace ↑C.left := hIrr.toPreirreducibleSpace

699	        (U i ⊓ U j : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) ≠ ⊥ := by
700	      intro i j hi hj h

702	      have ni : ((U i).1).Nonempty := by
703	        by_contra hh
704	        exact hi ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.not_nonempty_iff_eq_bot _).mp hh)
705	      have nj : ((U j).1).Nonempty := by
706	        by_contra hh
707	        exact hj ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.not_nonempty_iff_eq_bot _).mp hh)
708	      exact @nonempty_preirreducible_inter _ _ _ _ hPre
709	        (U i).isOpen (U j).isOpen ni nj
710	    -- Uniformity: (sf i).1 = v whenever U i ≠ ⊥.
711	    have key_val : ∀ i, U i ≠ ⊥ → (sf i).1 = v := by
712	      intro i hUi
713	      have hint : (U i ⊓ U i₀ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) ≠ ⊥ :=
714	        inter_ne_bot i i₀ hUi hUi₀
715	      have hc := hcompat i i₀
716	      have h1 : (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map
717	            (TopologicalSpace.Opens.infLELeft (U i) (U i₀)).op).hom (sf i)).1 = (sf i).1 :=
718	        map_val _ hint (sf i)
719	      have h2 : (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map
720	            (TopologicalSpace.Opens.infLERight (U i) (U i₀)).op).hom (sf i₀)).1 = (sf i₀).1 :=
721	        map_val _ hint (sf i₀)
722	      have hc_val := congr_arg Subtype.val hc
723	      simp only at hc_val
724	      rw [h1, h2] at hc_val
725	      exact hc_val
726	    -- Show v ∈ carrierSubmoduleSheaf (op (iSup U)).
727	    have hv_mem : v ∈ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmoduleSheaf P hPcoh
728	        (Opposite.op (iSup U)) := by
729	      refine ⟨⟨?_, ?_⟩, Or.inl hSup⟩
730	      · -- ord_Q(v) ≥ 0 for Q.point ∈ iSup U, Q.point ≠ P.
731	        intro Q hQ hQP
732	        obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp hQ
733	        have hUi : U i ≠ ⊥ := by
734	          intro hh
735	          rw [hh] at hi
736	          exact (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_bot.mp hi).elim
737	        have : (sf i).1 = v := key_val i hUi
738	        rw [← this]
739	        exact (sf i).2.1.1 Q hi hQP
740	      · -- ord_P(v) ≥ -1 when P ∈ iSup U.
741	        intro hP
742	        obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp hP
743	        have hUi : U i ≠ ⊥ := by
744	          intro hh
745	          rw [hh] at hi

747	        have : (sf i).1 = v := key_val i hUi
748	        rw [← this]
749	        exact (sf i).2.1.2 hi
750	    -- Build the gluing element and verify uniqueness.
751	    refine ⟨⟨v, hv_mem⟩, ?_, ?_⟩
752	    · -- IsGluing: ∀ i, F.map _ ⟨v, hv_mem⟩ = sf i.
753	      intro i
754	      apply Subtype.ext
755	      by_cases hUi : U i = ⊥

757	        have hsfi : (sf i).1 = 0 := hSubAt0 (U i) hUi (sf i)

759	          (CategoryTheory.homOfLE (le_iSup U i)).op).hom ⟨v, hv_mem⟩).1 = (sf i).1
760	        rw [hsfi]
761	        exact hSubAt0 (U i) hUi _
762	      · -- U i ≠ ⊥: image's .1 = v = (sf i).1.
763	        change (((lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierPresheaf P hPcoh).map
764	          (CategoryTheory.homOfLE (le_iSup U i)).op).hom ⟨v, hv_mem⟩).1 = (sf i).1

768	      apply Subtype.ext

779	/-- **The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
780	curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6, p. 144, Proposition 6.13(a)).
781	
782	The invertible sheaf cut out (in the dual / `𝒦_C`-subsheaf packaging of
783	Hartshorne `ℒ(D)`) by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on the complement,
784	where `f_P ∈ 𝔪_P ∖ 𝔪_P²` is any uniformiser of the DVR `𝒪_{C,P}`. The
785	result is independent of the choice of uniformiser (two uniformisers
786	differ by a unit) and is an invertible `𝒪_C`-module of rank one.
787	
788	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
789	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf
790	carrier used by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so
791	that `H⁰` and `H¹` of `𝒪_C(P)` are accessible via
792	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 0/1`).
793	
794	iter-187 body (per analogist `ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md`
795	Decision 3 + Step 5 of the 5-step recipe): bundle the `carrierPresheaf`
796	+ `carrierPresheaf_isSheaf` of the iter-186/187 Hartshorne
797	subsheaf-of-`K_C` direct construction. The two `IsLocallyNoetherian` /
798	`IsRegularInCodimensionOne` hypotheses propagate from the existing
799	`namespace lineBundleAtClosedPoint` variable block (already required by
800	every downstream consumer `globalSections_iff_*`,
801	`h1_vanishing_genusZero`, `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`,
802	`exists_nonconstant_genusZero`).
803	
804	Blueprint reference: `def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
805	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 144 + Proposition 6.13(a); Stacks tag 01X0). -/
806	noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint

12	/-!
13	# The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero (RR.2)
14	
15	This file is the **RR.2** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
16	headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically
17	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
18	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
19	
20	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
21	through:
22	
23	- `RR.1` (`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` / `WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil
24	  divisor group `Div(C)` and the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
25	- **`RR.2` (this file, `RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`)**: the Riemann–Roch
26	  dimension formula `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0` (with `deg D ≥ 0`),
27	  via the auxiliary Euler-characteristic identity
28	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`.
29	- `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, future): the invertible sheaf
30	  `𝒪_C(D)`, the linear-equivalence isomorphism `𝒪_C(D) ≅ 𝒪_C(D')` for
31	  `D ∼ D'`, and the `H¹`-vanishing input
32	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0` for `deg D ≥ 0` on a genus-`0` curve.
33	- `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`, future): the "two-section
34	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
35	
36	## Status (iter-174 Lane F file-skeleton)
37	
38	This file is the **iter-174 Lane F** file-skeleton: each of the four pinned
39	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
40	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`). The
41	Euler-characteristic carrier definition is concrete (a one-line subtraction
42	of `H⁰` and `H¹` `Module.finrank`s, mirroring the `genus` definition of
43	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus`); the remaining pins carry `sorry` bodies whose
44	closure is iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters `RR.3`
45	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`) and `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`) land.
46	
47	The 4 pinned declarations are:
48	
49	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic` — Euler characteristic
50	   `χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)` of a `ModuleCat k̄`-valued
51	   sheaf on `C` (the curve specialisation of the alternating sum, since
52	   `H^i = 0` for `i ≥ 2` on a one-dimensional scheme by Grothendieck
53	   vanishing).
54	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l` — the `ℓ`-invariant
55	   `ℓ(D) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D))` of a Weil divisor `D`.
56	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`
57	   — the Euler-characteristic identity `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` for
58	   every `D ∈ Div(C)` on a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
59	   `C / k̄` of genus `g = g(C)`.
60	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero`
61	   — the Riemann–Roch formula in genus `0`: `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` for any
62	   Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)` with `deg D ≥ 0` on a smooth proper
63	   geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` with `g(C) = 0` (threading
64	   the `H¹`-vanishing of `𝒪_C(D)` explicitly as a named premise until
65	   `RR.3` lands).
66	
67	## Note on `𝒪_C(D)` (the invertible sheaf of a divisor)
68	
69	The chapter's proof of `eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`
70	and the statement of `l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero` both reference
71	the line bundle `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D`. Mathlib `b80f227` ships no
72	`Scheme.lineBundleOfDivisor` (the closest is `WeierstrassCurve.lineBundle`
73	in the elliptic-curve formalisation), and the project-side construction of
74	`𝒪_C(D)` is queued for `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`). To keep the type
75	signatures of pins 2–4 substantive in the iter-174 skeleton, we expose a
76	**typed-`sorry` placeholder**
77	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` that pairs each divisor with
78	the `ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf carrier `𝒪_C(D)` is intended to occupy. The
79	iter-175+ closure of `RR.3` replaces this placeholder's body with the
80	honest invertible-sheaf construction; the present pins consume it only
81	through its `H⁰` and `H¹` cohomology, so the consumer signatures are
82	substantive in the type sense (each asserts an arithmetic identity on the
83	finiteness-of-`H^*` outputs).
84	
85	## References
86	
87	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex` (Hartshorne
88	IV.1 verbatim quotes; 4 pins).
89	Source: Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, IV §1 (pp. 294–297), Theorem 1.3
90	(Riemann–Roch) and Example 1.3.5 (genus-`0` specialisation). Stacks Project
91	tags 0BSC (Euler characteristic on a curve), 0AYO (Riemann–Roch).
92	-/

100	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

102	/-! ## §1. The Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve -/
103	
104	/-- **Euler characteristic of a `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf on a smooth proper
105	curve `C / k̄`.**
106	
107	On a curve (one-dimensional scheme), Grothendieck vanishing
108	(Hartshorne III.2.7) gives `H^i(C, 𝓕) = 0` for `i ≥ 2`, so the classical
109	alternating sum
110	`χ(𝓕) = Σ_{i ≥ 0} (-1)^i dim_{k̄} H^i(C, 𝓕)` collapses to the two-term
111	expression
112	`χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)`.
113	
114	This is the definition we ship. Coherence of `𝓕` on the proper `k̄`-scheme
115	`C` guarantees that both `H⁰` and `H¹` are finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector
116	spaces (Serre's coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem, the same
117	finiteness backing `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`), so the two `Module.finrank`s
118	are honest natural numbers and the difference is a well-defined integer.
119	
120	The `Module k̄`-valued cohomology pipeline is the project's
121	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009), the same wrapper used by
122	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`.
123	
124	Blueprint reference: `def:eulerChar_curve`
125	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295, displayed inside the proof of Theorem 1.3). -/
126	noncomputable def Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
127	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
128	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
129	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
130	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
131	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
132	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) : ℤ :=
133	  (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 0) : ℤ)
134	    - (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 1) : ℤ)
135	
136	/-! ## §2. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (sibling chapter)
137	
138	The honest construction of `𝒪_C(D)` lives in the sibling chapter `RR.3`
139	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OcOfD.lean`),
140	where the locally-principal ideal sheaves of closed points are glued
141	into an invertible `𝒪_C`-module. **iter-183 Lane K** opened that file
142	with the typed-`sorry` pin `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` plus the three
143	immediate corollaries `sheafOf_zero`, `sheafOf_singlePoint`, and
144	`sheafOf_ses_single_add`. **iter-183 Lane H** (this file) retires the
145	former local `sheafOf` typed-`sorry` placeholder (previously at L168 in
146	the iter-174 skeleton) by importing `OcOfD.lean`; downstream pins (the
147	`ℓ`-invariant, the χ-identity, the genus-`0` Riemann–Roch formula) now
148	reference the chapter's canonical `sheafOf` directly. -/
149	
150	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor

10	/-!
11	# The rational-curve isomorphism `C ≅ ℙ¹` (RR.4)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.4** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma pinned in

17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through the four sub-build chapters:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)`, the degree
22	  map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`, and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and Riemann–Roch in genus zero.
25	- `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`): the line bundle `𝒪_C([P])` of a closed point and
26	  the existence of a non-constant rational function
27	  `f ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P])) ∖ k̄ · 1`.
28	- **`RR.4` (this file)**: the global classification — the linear-system
29	  morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` produced from `(1, f)` (Pin 1), its degree identified
30	  via the pole divisor (Pin 2), and the degree-`1` ⇒ isomorphism step
31	  (Pin 3). The headline target `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
32	  (Pin 4) is the existing pinned declaration at
33	  `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290` — see §0 below.
34	
35	## Status (iter-177 file-skeleton; iter-181 Pin 3 signature refinement)
36	
37	This file was originally the **iter-177 Lane 8** file-skeleton. Each
38	of the three new pinned declarations carries the *intended*
39	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
40	`blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex`) with a
41	`sorry` body. Bodies are iter-182+ work after the RR.1/RR.2/RR.3
42	chain matures (in particular `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero`
43	and the `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P]))`-non-constant existence corollary of `RR.3`).
44	
45	iter-181 Lane I refined the Pin 3 signature of
46	`iso_of_degree_one`: the abstract function-field-iso existence
47	hypothesis is replaced by the `[Algebra K(C') K(C)]` typeclass binder
48	+ `Module.finrank K(C') K(C) = 1` equation, matching what the
49	iter-182+ body needs (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`
50	Decision 2 DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY).
51	
52	The 3 new pinned declarations are:
53	
54	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` — from a
55	   graded `k̄`-algebra hom `k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
56	   irrelevant ideal generates the unit ideal, produce the morphism
57	   `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` lifted to
58	   the slice category over `Spec k̄`.
59	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` — for a
60	   non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
61	   proper curve, extract a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` (the
62	   pole divisor `φ^*[∞]`).
63	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one` — a non-constant
64	   morphism between smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves
65	   whose induced function-field map is a `k̄`-algebra iso is an
66	   isomorphism of schemes (Hartshorne I.6.12 specialised to
67	   degree-`1`).
68	
69	The headline pin (Pin 4) is satisfied by the existing AVR.lean target.
70	
71	## Note on type expressivity
72	
73	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
74	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
75	
76	* `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` returns a *concrete* morphism
77	  `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` of `Over (Spec k̄)`, not `Iso.refl _` or a
78	  reflexive placeholder; the input data is the exact pair
79	  `(f, hf)` consumed by Mathlib's `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`.
80	* `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` asserts the existence of a
81	  positive-integer degree `d` together with a Weil divisor `D` on `C`
82	  realising it — the *named substantive content* of the degree-via-pole
83	  identification. Unfolds to `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = d`, not `True`.
84	* `iso_of_degree_one` asserts `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` from the
85	  non-constant hypothesis together with the *substantive* degree-`1`
86	  hypothesis `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`
87	  (paired with an `[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass
88	  binder for the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map). The
89	  `Nonempty`-wrapped iso is the exact downstream-consumer shape,
90	  matching the `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` headline signature in AVR.lean.
91	  This signature was refined in iter-181 Lane I from the iter-177
92	  file-skeleton placeholder `Nonempty (C'.ff ≃+* C.ff)` to make the
93	  degree-`1`-induced-by-`φ` hypothesis the substantive content; see
94	  `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2.
95	
96	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
97	(the concrete `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-derived morphism, the
98	existence-of-positive-degree-divisor statement, the existence of a
99	scheme isomorphism); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content `proof_wanted` /
100	`Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Curve conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`,
105	`Jacobian.lean`, `OCofP.lean`), a **smooth proper geometrically
106	irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
107	
108	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
109	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
110	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
111	
112	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site. The
113	`[IsIntegral C.left]` / `[IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]` instances
114	required by the Weil-divisor / `RationalMap.order` API are threaded
115	explicitly per-lemma where consumed.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form)
10	
11	A self-contained Phase E helper: two morphisms `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between
12	schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree
13	as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and geometrically irreducible over
14	`Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` — agree everywhere. Independent of
15	`AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian C` and provable from current Mathlib alone
16	(Stacks 0BFD plus standard separatedness/irreducibility lemmas).
17	
18	The historical motivation is Mumford's §4 rigidity for morphisms of group
19	schemes; the formalized statement is the minimal-hypothesis scheme-level
20	form, which the Mumford group-scheme rigidity follows from by specialization.
21	
22	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Rigidity.tex`.
23	
24	## Status
25	
26	`Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` — rigidity of morphisms agreeing as scheme
27	maps on a non-empty open of a reduced (geometrically) irreducible source
28	with separated target — is closed. See the "Hypothesis history" block below
29	for the discussion of why the original point-wise hypothesis was
30	strengthened to scheme-level equality on `U`.
31	-/
32	
33	set_option autoImplicit false
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
42	
43	/-!
44	## Hypothesis history
45	
46	### Scheme-level hypothesis (iter 003)
47	
48	The original scaffold from iter 002 had hypothesis
49	`h : ∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x`
50	(point-wise topological equality on `U`). This is too weak: in characteristic
51	`p`, the absolute Frobenius `F : X → X` of an elliptic curve over `𝔽_p` agrees
52	topologically with the identity on all of `X` (the identity on the underlying
53	topological space) but `F ≠ id` as scheme morphisms — they differ on stalks
54	(`a ↦ a` vs `a ↦ a^p`). Hence the symmetric-form rigidity statement
55	`g₁.base = g₂.base on U ⇒ g₁ = g₂` is mathematically false.
56	
57	We strengthen the hypothesis to scheme-level equality on `U`:
58	`h : U.ι ≫ g₁.left = U.ι ≫ g₂.left`. This is the version that is actually
59	needed at the use site (uniqueness half of `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`):
60	the application produces *scheme-level* agreement on a non-empty open
61	(through the symmetric-power surjection), not merely topological agreement.
62	
63	We also add `[IsReduced X.left]` because Mathlib `b80f227` lacks the lemma
64	`Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` for schemes over a field (the closest is the perfect-base
65	case `Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField`). This is a small Mathlib
66	gap that the downstream consumer must satisfy via the smoothness + irreducibility
67	of the curve / Jacobian (e.g. via `IsIntegral`).
68	
69	### Unused-hypothesis cleanup (iter 125)
70	
71	Eight decorative hypotheses (`{n m : ℕ}`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n X.hom]`,
72	`[IsProper X.hom]`, `[GrpObj X]`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension m Y.hom]`,
73	`[GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]`, `[GrpObj Y]`, and the strengthening of
74	`[IsSeparated Y.hom]` to `[IsProper Y.hom]`) were dropped per
75	`analogies/rigidity-refactor.md`. The declaration was also renamed from
76	`GrpObj.eq_of_eqOnOpen` to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`, mirroring
77	Mathlib's `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` naming idiom
78	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated:319–322`).
79	-/
80	
81	/-- Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form): two morphisms
82	`g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a
83	non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and
84	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` —
85	agree everywhere.
86	
87	This is the `Over (Spec (.of k))`-bundled specialization of Mathlib's
88	`ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`. The Mumford group-scheme rigidity
89	statement (a morphism vanishing on a non-empty open is zero) is
90	interderivable with this form using the group law on the target. -/
91	theorem Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen
92	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    [IsSeparated Y.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
95	    [IsReduced X.left]
96	    (g₁ g₂ : X ⟶ Y) (U : X.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set X.left).Nonempty)
97	    (h : (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₁.left =
98	      (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₂.left) :
99	    g₁ = g₂ := by
100	  -- (1) Provide the separatedness instance under the `OverClass.fromOver`
101	  --     form `Y.left ↘ Spec (.of k)` so that the downstream typeclass
102	  --     search at `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` finds it.
103	  haveI : IsSeparated (Y.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
104	    (‹IsSeparated Y.hom› : IsSeparated Y.hom)
105	  -- (2) `X.left` is irreducible: `Spec k` is a single point (`Unique`,
106	  --     hence `Subsingleton + Nonempty`), and `GeometricallyIrreducible`
107	  --     over a subsingleton base implies `IrreducibleSpace`.
108	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X.left :=
109	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton X.hom
110	  -- (3) `U.ι : U.toScheme ⟶ X.left` is dominant: a non-empty open in an
111	  --     irreducible space is dense, and a dense open immersion is dominant.
112	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) :=
113	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
114	  -- (4) Promote `g₁ = g₂` from the underlying scheme equality.
115	  --     The `Scheme.Over` and `IsOver` instances for `X.left`, `Y.left`,
116	  --     `g₁.left`, `g₂.left` over `Spec (.of k)` are inferred globally
117	  --     from the `Over (Spec (.of k))` category structure
118	  --     (`OverClass.fromOver`).
119	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
120	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of k))
121	    (X := X.left) (Y := Y.left) (f := g₁.left) (g := g₂.left) U.ι h
122	
123	end AlgebraicGeometry
124

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Rigidity

8	/-!
9	# Rigidity over `k̄`: morphisms `ℙ¹_{k̄} → A_{k̄}` are constant
10	
11	The keystone classical input for the M2.a sub-step of the genus-0
12	Albanese-witness argument (Jacobian.tex § C.2): morphisms from the
13	projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
14	group scheme `A_{k̄}` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` that hit
15	the identity at a `k̄`-rational point are the constant morphism at the
16	identity. Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Chapter~II~§4.
17	
18	See `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`.
19	
20	## Status (iter-126 scaffold)
21	
22	The named declaration `rigidity_over_kbar` is scaffolded with a single
23	`sorry` body. The body closure is gated on the shared cotangent-
24	vanishing Mathlib pile (`analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile.md`,
25	iter-129+) per STRATEGY.md § M2.a + § M2.d-alt. The proof decomposition
26	into sub-steps C.2.b (reduction to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`),
27	C.2.c (image-dimension dichotomy), C.2.d (the cotangent-vanishing
28	keystone), and C.2.e (set-to-scheme promotion) is documented in
29	`RigidityKbar.tex` § "Proof decomposition".
30	
31	## Encoding choice (iter-126 refactor agent note)
32	
33	The directive offered two encodings of the source `ℙ¹_{k̄}`: a literal
34	`Over.mk (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom MvPolynomial.C))` (Option A) or
35	an abstract "smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over `k̄` of
36	genus 0" (Option B). The literal Option A is mathematically wrong as
37	written — `Spec(MvPolynomial σ kbar) ⟶ Spec kbar` is the affine, not
38	projective, line — and Mathlib `b80f227` has no `ProjectiveSpace n S`
39	construction packaged as a `Scheme` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj` is the
40	general `Proj` of a graded ring, not specialised). The refactor agent
41	took the directive-recommended Option B: the source is a smooth proper
42	geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of relative dimension
43	`1` with `genus C = 0`. This is what the proof decomposition C.2.c–C.2.d
44	actually consume (the keystone uses the curve's `H¹(·, 𝒪) = 0` ↔
45	`genus = 0` characterisation, not a literal `ℙ¹` API).
46	-/
47	
48	set_option autoImplicit false
49	
50	universe u
51	
52	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
53	
54	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
55	
56	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
57	
58	/-- Rigidity for morphisms `C ⟶ A` from a genus-`0` curve over an
59	algebraically closed field `k̄` (encoding `ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the abstract
60	smooth-proper-geometrically-irreducible-of-relative-dimension-`1`-with-
61	`genus = 0` characterisation): every morphism from such a curve to a
62	smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `A` over `k̄`
63	that hits the identity at a `k̄`-rational point is the constant
64	morphism at the identity.
65	
66	This is the keystone classical input for the M2.a sub-step of the
67	genus-0 Albanese-witness argument. Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*,
68	Chapter~II~§4.
69	
70	**Status**: iter-126 scaffold — body is a single `sorry`. The closure
71	(C.2.b reduction via `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` + C.2.c image-
72	dimension dichotomy + C.2.d cotangent-vanishing keystone) is gated on
73	the shared cotangent-vanishing Mathlib pile (iter-129+). See
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex`. -/
75	theorem rigidity_over_kbar
76	    [IsAlgClosed kbar] [CharZero kbar]
77	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
78	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
79	    [IsProper C.hom]
80	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
81	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
82	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
83	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
84	    (f : C ⟶ A)
85	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
86	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
87	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) :=
88	  sorry
89	
90	end AlgebraicGeometry
91

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-!
9	# Mumford Rigidity Lemma and its Milne §I.1 corollaries
10	
11	This file is the **abstract rigidity foundation** of the project's genus-`0` route. It hosts the
12	proven, axiom-clean **Mumford Rigidity Lemma (Form I)** and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries it
13	implies — the additive decomposition of a morphism out of a product (Cor 1.5) and the fact that
14	a pointed regular map of abelian varieties is a homomorphism (Cor 1.2).
15	
16	The genus-`0` final assembly (the `ℙ¹`-scaling shortcut, the headline
17	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`, the Riemann–Roch bridge `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`) lives
18	downstream in `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`, which imports this file.
19	
20	## Contents
21	
22	The Rigidity-Lemma chain (PROVEN axiom-clean, iters 157–162; cube-free, cohomology-free, char-free):
23	
24	* `rigidity_snd_lift` — the cartesian-monoidal skeleton step of the Rigidity Lemma.
25	* `snd_left_isClosedMap` — bridge 1: completeness of `X` makes `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y` a closed map.
26	* `morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints` — the dense-closed-points hom-extensionality connective
27	  (route B's Step 2).
28	* `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral` — the deep algebra of bridge 2 / Step 1: a proper
29	  integral `k̄`-scheme mapping to an affine is constant on `k̄`-points.
30	* `isIntegral_of_retract` — integrality descends to a retract; closes the slice/section
31	  assembly of route B's Step 1.
32	* `rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine` — route B Step 1 assembled.
33	* `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine` — bridge 2 (slice-constancy) end to end.
34	* `rigidity_eqOn_dense_open` — the genuine geometric content: a non-empty open of agreement.
35	* `rigidity_core` — the geometric core `f = retract ≫ f`.
36	* `rigidity_lemma` — **Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I).** The headline.
37	
38	The Milne §I.1 corollaries (both PROVEN axiom-clean from `rigidity_lemma`):
39	
40	* `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` — Corollary 1.5 (additive decomposition over a product).
41	* `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` — Corollary 1.2 (a pointed regular map of abelian varieties
42	  is a homomorphism).
43	
44	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
45	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §4; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Cor 1.2, Cor 1.5).
46	-/
47	
48	set_option autoImplicit false
49	
50	universe u
51	
52	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
53	
54	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
55	
56	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
57	
58	/-- **Cartesian-monoidal identity (skeleton step of the Rigidity Lemma).** Post-composing the
59	second projection `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y` with the slice section `y ↦ (x₀, y)` is the "collapse the
60	`X`-axis onto `x₀`" endomorphism `(x, y) ↦ (x₀, y)` of `X ⊗ Y`:
61	`snd ≫ lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) = lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y)`.
62	
63	Pure cartesian-monoidal algebra (no geometry): `comp_lift` distributes the `snd`, the
64	`𝟙 Y` component simplifies by `Category.comp_id`, and the `toUnit Y` component collapses by
65	uniqueness of maps into the terminal object. -/
66	theorem rigidity_snd_lift
67	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
68	    (x₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ X) :
69	    snd X Y ≫ lift (toUnit Y ≫ x₀) (𝟙 Y) =
70	      lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) := by
71	  ext1 <;> simp
72	
73	/-- **Bridge 1 of the Rigidity Lemma (closed-map step), PROVEN.** When `X` is complete (proper)
74	over `k̄`, the second monoidal projection `snd : X ⊗ Y ⟶ Y` has, on underlying schemes, a
75	*closed* base map. This is Mumford's "completeness of `X` makes `p₂` a closed map" (Abelian
76	Varieties, Ch. II §4, p. 43).
77	
78	Proof: the underlying scheme morphism `(snd X Y).left` is the pullback projection
79	`Limits.pullback.snd X.hom Y.hom` (`Over.snd_left`), i.e. the base change of `X.hom` along
80	`Y.hom`. `IsProper X.hom ⟹ UniversallyClosed X.hom` (`IsProper.toUniversallyClosed`), and
81	`UniversallyClosed` is stable under base change
82	(`universallyClosed_isStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback` on the canonical pullback square), so
83	`(snd X Y).left` is universally closed and hence its base map is closed
84	(`Scheme.Hom.isClosedMap`). Char-free; no theorem of the cube, no cohomology. -/
85	theorem snd_left_isClosedMap
86	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper X.hom] :
87	    IsClosedMap (snd X Y).left.base := by
88	  haveI hp : UniversallyClosed X.hom := IsProper.toUniversallyClosed
89	  haveI : UniversallyClosed (snd X Y).left := by
90	    rw [Over.snd_left]
91	    exact universallyClosed_isStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback

95	/-- **Dense-closed-points hom-extensionality (the bespoke globalisation connective, PROVEN).**
96	Two morphisms `g₁ g₂ : W ⟶ Z` out of a *reduced* scheme `W` whose closed points are *dense*
97	(`[JacobsonSpace W]` — e.g. when `W` is locally of finite type over a field) into a *separated*
98	scheme `Z` are equal as soon as they agree at every closed point `x ∈ closedPoints W` after the
99	canonical residue-field probe `W.fromSpecResidueField x : Spec κ(x) ⟶ W`.
100	
101	This is the one connective the iter-159 `mathlib-analogist` flagged that Mathlib does **not**
102	package directly: Mathlib supplies only the single-dominant-morphism `ext_of_isDominant`. Here we
103	assemble all the closed points into one dominant probe — the coproduct
104	`∐_{x ∈ closedPoints W} Spec κ(x) ⟶ W`, whose topological range is exactly the (dense) set of
105	closed points — and feed it to `ext_of_isDominant`. It is `Step 2` of bridge 2's route B
106	(cohomology-free) and is fully proven here, reusable independently of the rigidity context. -/
107	theorem morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints
108	    {W Z : Scheme.{u}} [IsReduced W] [JacobsonSpace W] [Z.IsSeparated]
109	    {g₁ g₂ : W ⟶ Z}
110	    (h : ∀ x ∈ closedPoints W,
111	      W.fromSpecResidueField x ≫ g₁ = W.fromSpecResidueField x ≫ g₂) :
112	    g₁ = g₂ := by
113	  -- The dominant probe: the coproduct of the residue-field `Spec`s over the closed points.
114	  let F : closedPoints W → Scheme.{u} := fun x => Spec (W.residueField x.1)
115	  let probe : (∐ F) ⟶ W := Sigma.desc fun x => W.fromSpecResidueField x.1
116	  -- Its topological range contains every closed point, hence (Jacobson) is dense.
117	  haveI : IsDominant probe := by
118	    refine ⟨(dense_iff_closure_eq.mpr (closure_closedPoints (X := W))).mono ?_⟩
119	    intro x hx
120	    obtain ⟨pt⟩ : Nonempty (Spec (W.residueField x)) := inferInstance
121	    refine ⟨(Sigma.ι F ⟨x, hx⟩).base pt, ?_⟩
122	    have hcomp : Sigma.ι F ⟨x, hx⟩ ≫ probe = W.fromSpecResidueField x := Sigma.ι_desc _ _
123	    have e1 : probe.base ((Sigma.ι F ⟨x, hx⟩).base pt) = (W.fromSpecResidueField x).base pt := by
124	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, hcomp]
125	    rw [e1]
126	    exact Set.eq_of_mem_singleton (Scheme.range_fromSpecResidueField x ▸ Set.mem_range_self pt)
127	  -- Componentwise the probe equalises `g₁` and `g₂`; dominance then forces `g₁ = g₂`.
128	  refine ext_of_isDominant probe (Sigma.hom_ext _ _ fun x => ?_)
129	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc]
130	  exact h x.1 x.2

132	/-- **A proper integral `k̄`-scheme mapping into an affine is constant on `k̄`-points (the deep
133	algebraic content of Step 1, PROVEN).** Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, let `W` be an
134	integral scheme that is universally closed and locally of finite type over `Spec k̄` (e.g. a proper
135	integral slice `X_y`), and let `g : W ⟶ V` be a morphism into an *affine* scheme `V`. Then `g`
136	takes the same value on any two `k̄`-points (sections `a`, `b` of the structure map `wk`):
137	`a ≫ wk = 𝟙` and `b ≫ wk = 𝟙` force `a ≫ g = b ≫ g`.
138	
139	This is the cohomology-free realisation of "a global regular function on a proper integral
140	`k̄`-variety is constant". The global sections `Γ(W, ⊤)` form a field
141	(`isField_of_universallyClosed`) that is module-finite over `k̄`
142	(`finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`, hence the structure map is integral on `Γ`); algebraic
143	closedness collapses the finite extension (`IsAlgClosed.ringHom_bijective_of_isIntegral`), so the
144	structure map's global-sections map `wk.appTop` is an isomorphism. Both sections `a`, `b` are
145	left inverses of `wk.appTop` on `Γ`, hence have equal `appTop`; and a morphism into the affine `V`
146	is pinned by its `appTop` (`ext_of_isAffine`). No coherent cohomology, no relative Stein
147	factorisation. -/
148	theorem eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral
149	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
150	    {W : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral W] (wk : W ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))

1967	`x ∈ 𝔪` that is `R`-regular (a non-zero-divisor on `R`) such that the quotient

1998	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1) :
1999	    ∃ (x : R), x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ∧ IsSMulRegular R x ∧

2004	    exists_isSMulRegular_notMemSq_of_regularLocal_succ (k := k) hdim
2005	  refine ⟨x, hxMem, hxReg, ?_⟩
2006	  -- Step 2: assemble the structural instances on `R/(x)`.
2007	  have hxNonunit : ¬ IsUnit x := fun hu =>
2008	    (IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mpr hu) hxMem

2010	    Ideal.span_singleton_ne_top hxNonunit
2011	  haveI : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
2012	    Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr hspan_ne_top
2013	  haveI : IsLocalRing (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) :=
2014	    IsLocalRing.of_surjective' (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2015	  -- IsNoetherianRing (R ⧸ I) is automatic via `Ideal.Quotient.isNoetherianRing`.

2021	    rw [← IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace R]
2022	    exact hdim
2023	  have hR'_cot_eq :
2024	      Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)))
2025	          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))) = k := by

2033	        (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R))]

2068	/-- **Lane G inductive step (iter-185 structural scaffold).** Packages the
2069	inductive step of Stacks 00OD: given a regular local ring `R` of dimension
2070	`k + 1`, plus the inductive hypothesis at dimension `k` (universally
2071	quantified in the ring), produce a regular sequence of length `k + 1` in
2072	the maximal ideal of `R`.
2073	
2074	The body is **axiom-clean** modulo the typed-`sorry` substrate helper
2075	`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`. Assembly path:
2076	
2077	1. Helper extracts `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular R x` AND `IsRegularLocalRing
2078	   (R⧸(x))` of `spanFinrank = k`.
2079	2. IH applied on `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` produces a regular sequence
2080	   `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` of length `k` in the maximal ideal there.
2081	3. Lift `rs'_q` to `rs : List R` via `Function.surjInv` of
2082	   `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the section property gives
2083	   `rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk _) = rs'_q`.
2084	4. Members of `rs` lie in `𝔪` because the maximal ideal of `R⧸(x)` is the
2085	   image of `𝔪` (`IsLocalRing.le_maximalIdeal` applied to the comap chain).
2086	5. Cons via `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons'` to form the length-`(k+1)`
2087	   sequence `x :: rs`.
2088	
2089	This factoring isolates the Mathlib gap to one narrow typed `sorry` instead of
2090	spreading it across the lemma body; future iterations can attack Stacks 00NQ
2091	+ 00NU directly. -/
2092	private lemma regularLocal_inductive_step {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2093	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] {k : ℕ}
2094	    (hdim : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = k + 1)
2095	    (IH : ∀ (R' : Type u) [CommRing R'] [IsLocalRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R']
2096	            [IsRegularLocalRing R'],
2097	            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').spanFinrank = k →
2098	            ∃ rs : List R', rs.length = k ∧
2099	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') ∧
2100	              RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs) :
2101	    ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = k + 1 ∧
2102	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
2103	      RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
2104	  -- Step 1: extract `x ∈ 𝔪` regular on `R` with `R ⧸ (x)` regular local of
2105	  -- `spanFinrank = k`.  This is the consolidated Stacks 00NQ + 00NU substrate.
2106	  obtain ⟨x, hxMem, hxReg, hRLR, hdim_quot⟩ :=
2107	    exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ hdim
2108	  -- Step 2: apply IH on `R ⧸ (x)` — this gives a regular sequence of length
2109	  -- `k` on the quotient, valued in its maximal ideal.
2110	  obtain ⟨rs'_q, hlen_q, hmem_q, hreg_q⟩ := IH (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) hdim_quot
2111	  -- Step 3: lift `rs'_q : List (R ⧸ (x))` to `rs' : List R` via the right
2112	  -- inverse of the (surjective) quotient ring hom.
2113	  let mkq : R →+* R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
2114	  let g : R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} → R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2115	  have hg : ∀ y, mkq (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
2116	  let rs' : List R := rs'_q.map g
2117	  have hlen_rs' : rs'.length = k := by simp [rs', hlen_q]
2118	  have hmkmap : rs'.map mkq = rs'_q := by
2119	    change (rs'_q.map g).map mkq = rs'_q
2120	    rw [List.map_map]
2121	    conv_rhs => rw [← List.map_id rs'_q]
2122	    exact List.map_congr_left fun y _ => hg y
2123	  -- Step 4: each element of `rs'` lies in `𝔪 R` via the comap of `𝔪 (R⧸(x))`.
2124	  -- The maximal ideal of `R ⧸ (x)` comaps back to `𝔪 R` (it's the *unique*
2125	  -- maximal ideal of `R` containing `Ideal.span {x} ⊆ 𝔪`).
2126	  have hmem_rs' : ∀ r ∈ rs', r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
2127	    intro r hr
2128	    simp only [rs', List.mem_map] at hr
2129	    obtain ⟨y, hy_mem, rfl⟩ := hr
2130	    -- The comap of `𝔪 (R⧸(x))` under the surjective `mkq` is a maximal ideal
2131	    -- of `R`; since `R` is local, it equals `𝔪 R`.  Hence `g y ∈ comap mkq 𝔪'`
2132	    -- iff `g y ∈ 𝔪 R`.
2133	    have hmax_comap : (Ideal.comap mkq
2134	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))).IsMaximal :=
2135	      Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
2136	    have heq : Ideal.comap mkq
2137	        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
2138	        = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R :=
2139	      (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax_comap
2140	    rw [← heq, Ideal.mem_comap]
2141	    exact (hg y).symm ▸ hmem_q y hy_mem
2142	  -- Step 5: cons `x` onto `rs'` to form the length-`(k+1)` regular sequence.
2143	  refine ⟨x :: rs', ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
2144	  · simp [hlen_rs']
2145	  · intro r hr
2146	    rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr_in
2147	    · exact hxMem
2148	    · exact hmem_rs' r hr_in
2149	  · -- `IsRegular R (x :: rs')` via `IsRegular.cons'`.
2150	    -- Need `IsSMulRegular R x` (have `hxReg`) AND
2151	    -- `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) (rs'.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {x})))`.
2152	    refine RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons' hxReg ?_
2153	    -- After `cons'`: goal is `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) (rs'.map mkq)`
2154	    -- = `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) rs'_q` (by `hmkmap`), implicit ring
2155	    -- `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` (inferred from list type).
2156	    rw [hmkmap]
2157	    -- Goal: `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x R) rs'_q` (implicit ring `R ⧸ (x)`).
2158	    -- IH provides: `IsRegular (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) rs'_q` (same implicit
2159	    -- ring, but M differs: `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` vs `QuotSMulTop x R = R ⧸ (x • ⊤)`).
2160	    --
2161	    -- **iter-185 typed `sorry` — TECHNICAL BRIDGE (NOT a Mathlib substrate gap).**
2162	    -- The two M's are *equal as sets* — both are the quotient of `R` by the
2163	    -- principal ideal `(x)`, written two different ways:
2164	    --   * `R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}` — quotient by `Ideal.span {x}` as a Submodule R R;
2165	    --   * `QuotSMulTop x R = R ⧸ (x • ⊤)` — quotient by `x • ⊤` as a Submodule R R.
2166	    -- Mathlib's `Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul` gives
2167	    -- `(x • ⊤ : Submodule R R) = Ideal.span {x} • ⊤ = Ideal.span {x}` (the
2168	    -- second equality because `I • ⊤_R = I` for an ideal `I` of `R`).  The
2169	    -- bridge between the two M's is an `R⧸(x)`-linear equivalence; future iter
2170	    -- can construct this in ~10-20 LOC via:
2171	    --   (a) `Submodule.quotEquivOfEq` from the submodule equality (gives R-linear);
2172	    --   (b) upgrade to `R⧸(x)`-linear via `QuotSMulTop.mem_annihilator`
2173	    --       (since `x ∈ Module.annihilator R (QuotSMulTop x R)`, the R-action
2174	    --       factors through `R⧸(x)`, and any R-linear equiv between two such

2176	    --   (c) close via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr` on this `R⧸(x)`-linear equiv.
2177	    -- This bridge has no substrate dependencies — it's pure bookkeeping.
2178	    --
2179	    -- iter-186 Lane G: closed kernel-clean via the explicit `R⧸(x)`-linear
2180	    -- equiv between the two quotients, then `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`
2181	    -- transports `hreg_q` across.  The two `mapQ` halves use `LinearMap.id`
2182	    -- with `heq.le` / `heq.ge`, and `map_smul'` reduces to `rfl` after
2183	    -- `Quotient.inductionOn` on the scalar (the `R⧸(x)`-action on both sides
2184	    -- is `[s] • [r] = [s * r]` definitionally).
2185	    open scoped Pointwise in
2186	    have heq : (x • (⊤ : Submodule R R)) = (Ideal.span {x} : Submodule R R) := by
2187	      ext y
2188	      simp [Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists, Ideal.mem_span_singleton,
2189	        eq_comm, Dvd.dvd]
2190	    let e : (R ⧸ (x • (⊤ : Submodule R R))) ≃ₗ[R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}]
2191	        (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) := {
2192	      toFun := Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.le
2193	      invFun := Submodule.mapQ _ _ LinearMap.id heq.ge
2194	      left_inv := by rintro ⟨r⟩; rfl
2195	      right_inv := by rintro ⟨r⟩; rfl
2196	      map_add' := map_add _
2197	      map_smul' := by
2198	        rintro q ⟨r⟩
2199	        induction q using Quotient.inductionOn with
2200	        | _ s => rfl
2201	    }
2202	    exact (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e.symm rs'_q).mp hreg_q

2204	lemma exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal
2205	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
2206	    [IsRegularLocalRing R] :
2207	    ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank
2208	        ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
2209	        ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs := by
2210	  -- Strong induction on `n = spanFinrank R`, generalising `R` so the inductive
2211	  -- hypothesis can be applied to the quotient `R/(x)` at smaller dimension.
2212	  suffices haux : ∀ (n : ℕ) (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2213	      [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R],
2214	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = n →
2215	      ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧
2216	        (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
2217	        RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs by
2218	    exact haux _ R rfl
2219	  intro n
2220	  induction n with
2221	  | zero =>
2222	    -- Base case `dim 0`: spanFinrank = 0, hence `maximalIdeal R = ⊥` (so `R` is
2223	    -- a field). The empty list is trivially `R`-regular on the nonzero ring `R`.
2224	    intros R _ _ _ _ _hdim
2225	    refine ⟨[], rfl, by simp, ?_⟩
2226	    exact RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil R R
2227	  | succ k ih =>
2228	    -- Inductive case `dim (k + 1)`: delegate to `regularLocal_inductive_step`,
2229	    -- supplying the inductive hypothesis at dimension `k`. The helper handles
2230	    -- the substantive NZD-extraction + quotient-regularity + cons assembly.
2231	    intros R _ _ _ _ hdim
2232	    exact regularLocal_inductive_step (k := k) hdim (fun R' _ _ _ _ h => ih R' h)

2234	/-- **Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay.** Every regular Noetherian
2235	local ring is Cohen–Macaulay: a minimal generating set of `𝔪` is an
2236	`R`-regular sequence of length `dim R`, so `depth(R) ≥ dim R`; combined
2237	with the standard upper bound `depth(R) ≤ dim R` (Stacks 00LK) this gives
2238	`depth(R) = dim R`.
2239	
2240	This is the consumer-facing input for **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
2241	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective
2242	surface): the local ring `O_{S,x}` of a regular projective surface at a
2243	closed point is regular of Krull dimension `2`, hence Cohen–Macaulay,
2244	hence has depth `2`, which is exactly the input the local-cohomology
2245	vanishing `H^i_x(O_S) = 0` for `i < 2` needs (Stacks 0AVF; see Hartshorne
2246	III.7).

2248	iter-181 Lane G **STRUCTURAL**: body is now decomposed into two
2249	typed helper lemmas — `length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular` (the upper
2250	bound, closed kernel-clean from
2251	`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) and
2252	`exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (the lower bound, typed `sorry` on the
2253	Mathlib gap `IsRegularLocalRing ⟹ IsDomain` + regular-quotient induction).
2254	The combined assembly into `depth = ringKrullDim` is closed inline below,
2255	so the only residual `sorry` in this `instance` body is the named helper. -/
2256	instance of_regular (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
2257	    [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsRegularLocalRing R] : CohenMacaulay R where
2258	  depth_eq_krullDim := by
2259	    -- Step 1: simplify `Module.depth` via the `else` branch
2260	    --   (since `𝔪 • ⊤ = 𝔪 ≠ ⊤` for a local ring's maximal ideal).
2261	    have h𝔪 : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R R)
2262	        ≠ (⊤ : Submodule R R) := by
2263	      have heq : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R R)
2264	          = IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by simp
2265	      rw [heq]
2266	      exact (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal.isMaximal R).ne_top
2267	    rw [Module.depth, if_neg h𝔪]
2268	    -- Step 2: convert RHS to the spanFinrank using
2269	    -- `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal`.
2270	    rw [← IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal]
2271	    -- Goal: ((sSup {n | …} : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞)
2272	    --         = ((spanFinrank 𝔪 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)
2273	    -- Step 3: it suffices to show the sSup equals spanFinrank as ℕ∞,
2274	    -- via antisymmetry: upper bound from Helper 1, lower bound from Helper 2.
2275	    have h1 : (sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
2276	        (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
2277	          ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs }
2278	        : ℕ∞) = ((IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank : ℕ∞) := by
2279	      apply le_antisymm
2280	      · -- Upper bound: every element of the sSup-set is at most spanFinrank.
2281	        apply sSup_le
2282	        rintro n ⟨rs, rfl, _, hreg⟩
2283	        have hub := length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular hreg
2284	        rw [← IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal] at hub
2285	        exact_mod_cast hub
2286	      · -- Lower bound: spanFinrank is achieved by Helper 2's regular sequence.
2287	        obtain ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩ := exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal R
2288	        apply le_sSup
2289	        refine ⟨rs, ?_, hmem, hreg⟩
2290	        exact_mod_cast hlen
2291	    rw [h1]
2292	    -- Final coercion: `((n : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) = ((n : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)`
2293	    -- is the standard `Nat.cast`-tower commutation.
2294	    rfl
2295	
2296	end CohenMacaulay
2297	
2298	end RingTheory
2299

600	          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
601	        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
602	          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
603	      -- Step E: cons x to form a length-(n+1) M-regular sequence in 𝔪.
604	      have hrs_M_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M (x :: rs_n) :=
605	        (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_n).mpr ⟨hxReg, hrs_n_reg⟩
606	      have hrs_M_mem : ∀ r ∈ (x :: rs_n), r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
607	        intro r hr
608	        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr
609	        · exact hxMem
610	        · exact hrs_n_mem r hr
611	      have hrs_M_len : (x :: rs_n).length = n + 1 := by simp [hrs_n_len]
612	      -- Step F: conclude `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` via `le_sSup`.
613	      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
614	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
615	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
616	      rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
617	      apply le_sSup
618	      refine ⟨x :: rs_n, ?_, hrs_M_mem, hrs_M_reg⟩
619	      exact_mod_cast hrs_M_len
